<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063882 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00291: (2026x) Accessing web applications

- page_id: `247063882`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063882/2026x+Accessing+web+applications
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-15T10:11:35.009+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying the license' space=''] and starting falseTeamwork CloudtwcfalseMagic Collaboration Studiomcs, you can log in to access web applications.

To sign in to Teamwork Cloud web applications

1. In an internet browser, go to*http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp*. For example, if the machine IP is **10.1.1.123** and the default port is used, the URL will be *[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp)*. The default port is **8****443**.
2. When the authentication page opens, enter your username and password to sign in. The default credentials are **Administrator/****Administrator**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The main Web Application Platform page(*[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp))*displayed after signing in.

Once you are logged in, you will be redirected to the landing page depending on your role/ permissions. To navigate to a specific web application, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using the app bar' space='']. Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Teamwork Cloud system or view its details.

The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.

##### Signing out of Teamwork Cloud web applications

To sign out of Teamwork Cloud web applications

- In the top right corner click [ATTACHMENT filename='sign_out.png'] button and select Sign out .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying the product license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and starting <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6837aeee-3dd7-45ca-9847-53525636cff1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71c8eb2c-ba8c-46ca-be0b-87939a8de78a"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>, you can log in to access web applications. </p><p><br /></p><p>To sign in to Teamwork Cloud web applications</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In an internet browser, go to </span><em>http(s)://&lt;server_address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>. For example, if the machine IP is <strong>10.1.1.123</strong> and the default port is used, the URL will be <em><a href="https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp">https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp</a></em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76b071df-da46-4bc9-a2d8-1f7a21c1a8b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port is <strong>8</strong><strong>443</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the authentication page opens, enter your username and password to sign in.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28ed6574-b4ed-4c9f-b2bd-1bab834dceab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default credentials are <strong>Administrator/</strong><strong>Administrator</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signing_into_twc.png" /></ac:image><br /><span class="nolink">The main Web Application Platform page</span><span class="nolink"> (<em style="text-align: center;"><span class="nolink"><a href="https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp">https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp</a>) </span></em></span><span class="nolink">displayed after signing in.</span></h6></li></ol><p>Once you are logged in, you will be redirected to the landing page depending on your role/ permissions. To navigate to a specific web application, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using the app bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Teamwork Cloud system or view its details.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1799632d-6f6a-4317-ab77-d1f47a6c8738"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Signing out of Teamwork Cloud web applications</h3><p><br /></p><p>To sign out of Teamwork Cloud web applications</p><hr /><ul><li>In the top right corner click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sign_out.png" /></ac:image> button and select <strong>Sign out</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064629 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00292: (2026x) Adding a logo to the cover page

- page_id: `247064629`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064629/2026x+Adding+a+logo+to+the+cover+page
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-08T09:11:06.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x) Adding logos
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways to add your logo to the cover page:

- Configuring the logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template properties and using this template when publishing a document. The logo will appear in the Cameo Collaborator document and on the cover page of the exported document. The cover page template has a placeholder at the top of the page where the logo is placed.
- Removing the current placeholder for the logo and adding another logo image in the desired place of the cover page the template. In this case you will need to modify the <template folder>/common/coverPage.html file. To add the logo image to the cover page template, do one of the following:
  - Encode the image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the *img src* attribute.This is the preferred way to indicate a source of an image in templates. The URL must be absolute, relative URLs are not going to work.The URL must be accessible from the server where the web application platform is deployed if users are exporting a document to the PDF format.The URL must be accessible from the user computers if users are exporting a document to the HTML format. The URL will appear in the output HTML file as is.Use a URL to the remote image as the value of the *img src*attribute:

##### Example of adding a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template

Let’s assume that you want to make your logo appear on the cover page without configuring the logo for the project. Or you just want to use another logo image for the cover page than the one configured in the template used for publishing. In addition, you want your new logo to appear at the top of the cover page, the same place where the placeholder for the default logo is configured.

To add a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template

1. Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or your own code or tools to make the conversion.
2. Open the <template folder>/common/cover-page.html file.
3. Find the div tag with the company-logo-container.
4. Locate a single img tag within the div .
5. Replace the src of the img tag with <img src="{data:image/<image type>;base64,<image as a Base64 encoded string> }" alt="<alt text>">.
6. If necessary, use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want. **Code example when using the PNG image type**<div id="company-logo-container"> 
 <img src="data:image/png;base64," width="200" height="200" alt="Logo"/></div>

If the document is exported using the modified template, the cover page with a custom logo may look like the one below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the cover page with a custom logo.

##### Example of configuring a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template

Let’s assume that you want to configure a logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template you use for publishing a document. In this case, you can either modify the default template or create your own custom template. For more information on hoe to create custom document templates, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a custom template' space=''].

This example demonstrates how to modify the *SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator*.

To configure a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template based on the *SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator*

1. Open the SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator Cameo template in a modeling tool.
2. Clear the Standard/System Profile checkbox in the Project Properties dialog so the template is not considered a system file. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating a custom template' space=''] .
3. Open the Specification window of the Document element as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='specification_window_of_document_element.png']
4. In the Specification window, select your logo image as the value of the Image property. [ATTACHMENT filename='image_property.png']
5. Save the template file.
6. Open the project from which you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document.
7. In the main menu, select Tools > Cameo Collaborator > Publish .
8. In the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog, select to use your custom template. Select Use Local Template or Use Server Template depending on where the template is stored. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_template.png']
9. After the template is used select it in the Template drop-down box.
10. Define other publishing properties and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing documents' space=''] .

If the logo image was successfully added, it will be displayed in the App bar of the Cameo Collaborator document as shown below. Furthermore, the logo image will be displayed on the cover page of the exported document as well. If you want the logo to be displayed in a non-default location or make other changes, you can adjust the styles of the cover page in the *common/cover-page-styles.html* file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cameo Collaborator document with a custom logo.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are two ways to add your logo to the cover page:</p><ul><li>Configuring the logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template properties and using this template when publishing a document. The logo will appear in the Cameo Collaborator document and on the cover page of the exported document. The cover page template has a placeholder at the top of the page where the logo is placed.</li><li>Removing the current placeholder for the logo and adding another logo image in the desired place of the cover page the template. In this case you will need to modify the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/coverPage.html</em> file.<br />To add the logo image to the cover page template, do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Encode the image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">img src</em> attribute.<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> This is the preferred way to indicate a source of an image in templates.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="137c6e6d-2256-4997-8cc0-b8f9763c705e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The URL must be absolute, relative URLs are not going to work.</li><li>The URL must be accessible from the server where the web application platform is deployed if users are exporting a document to the PDF format.</li><li>The URL must be accessible from the user computers if users are exporting a document to the HTML format. The URL will appear in the output HTML file as is.<p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a URL to the remote image as the value of the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">img src</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> attribute:<br /></span></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><h3 class="auto-cursor-target" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Example of adding a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to make your logo appear on the cover page without configuring the logo for the project. Or you just want to use another logo image for the cover page than the one configured in the template used for publishing. In addition, you want your new logo to appear at the top of the cover page, the same place where the placeholder for the default logo is configured.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or your own code or tools to make the conversion.</li><li>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/cover-page.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>div</em> tag with the <em>company-logo-container.</em></li><li>Locate a single <em>img</em> tag within the <em>div</em>.</li><li>Replace the <em>src</em> of the <em>img</em> tag with <em>&lt;img src=&quot;{data:image/&lt;image type&gt;;base64,&lt;image as a Base64 encoded string&gt; }&quot; alt=&quot;&lt;alt text&gt;&quot;&gt;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If necessary, use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want.</p><table class="fixed-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 1087.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="background-color: rgb(241,242,244);"><strong>Code example when using the PNG image type</strong></th></tr><tr><td style=""><p>&lt;div id=&quot;company-logo-container&quot;&gt;<br />   &lt;img src=&quot;data:image/png;base64,&quot; width=&quot;200&quot; height=&quot;200&quot; alt=&quot;Logo&quot;/&gt;</p><p>&lt;/div&gt;</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></li></ol><p><br />If the document is exported using the modified template, the cover page with a custom logo may look like the one below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logo_on_cover_page_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Example of the cover page with a custom logo.</h6><p><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Example of configuring a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to configure a logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template you use for publishing a document. In this case, you can either modify the default template or create your own custom template. For more information on hoe to create custom document templates, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating a custom template" /></ac:link>.</p><p>This example demonstrates how to modify the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template based on the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator</em></p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator Cameo</em> template in a modeling tool.</li><li>Clear the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> checkbox in the <strong>Project Properties</strong> dialog so the template is not considered a system file. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating a custom template" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the Document element as shown below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_document_element.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the Specification window, select your logo image as the value of the <strong>Image</strong> property.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_property.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Open the project from which you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Cameo Collaborator</strong> &gt; <strong>Publish</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog, select to use your custom template. Select <strong>Use Local Template</strong> or <strong>Use Server Template</strong> depending on where the template is stored.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_template.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>After the template is used select it in the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box.</li><li>Define other publishing properties and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Publishing documents" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[publish the document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If the logo image was successfully added, it will be displayed in the App bar of the Cameo Collaborator document as shown below. Furthermore, the logo image will be displayed on the cover page of the exported document as well. If you want the logo to be displayed in a non-default location or make other changes, you can adjust the styles of the cover page in the <em>common/cover-page-styles.html</em> file.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="document_with_logo.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cameo Collaborator document with a custom logo.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064661 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00293: (2026x) Adding a logo to the header and footer

- page_id: `247064661`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064661/2026x+Adding+a+logo+to+the+header+and+footer
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-10T13:28:21.401+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x) Adding logos
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To make changes to the footer or header of exported documents, you will need to modify the following files:

- For the header - <template folder>/common/header.html
- For the footer - <template folder>/common/footer.html

You can add a logo to the header or footer in one of the following ways:

- Encode the logo image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the img src attribute.
- Add a URL to the remote image as the value of the *img src* attribute. The URL must be absolute, relative URLs do not work.The URL must be accessible from the server where Web Application Platform is deployed.

##### Example of adding a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string

Let’s say you want to use a logo in the header of the exported PDF file pages.

To add a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string

1. Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or use your own code/tools to make the conversion.
2. Open the <template folder>/common/header.html file.
3. Find the closing style tag.
4. Below this tag, create a *div* with an *img* tag, where *<img src="{data:image/<image type>;base64,<Image as a Base64 encoded string> }" alt="<alt text>">.*
5. Add the *style=”position: running(header)”*attribute to the *div.*
6. Find the *@page { @top-left { content: "" }}* within the style definitions and replace the double quotes with *element(header).*
7. Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want. Your code may look like the one below if you use a PNG image: **Code example when using the PNG image type**<div th:fragment="HeaderFragment" xmlns:th=[http://www.thymeleaf.org](http://www.thymeleaf.org)><style>@page { @top-left { content: element(header) }}</style> 
 <div style="position: running(header)"> 
 <img src=" data:image/png;base64," width="72" height="22" alt="Logo"/> 
 </div> 
</div>

The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the exported document with the logo in the header.

##### Example of adding a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image

Let’s say you want to use a logo in the footer of the exported PDF file pages. In this example, we will use an absolute URL as the image source.

To add a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image

1. Open the <template folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the footer tag.
3. Add the img tag with <img src"http(s)://path_to_image" alt="<alt text>" >
4. Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the image the way you want. Your code may look like this (the CSS is inline for simplicity): Code example[IMAGE alt='Logo' src='https://www.3ds.com/assets/3ds-navigation/3DS_corporate-logo_blue.svg'] 
 
]]>

The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To make changes to the footer or header of exported documents, you will need to modify the following files:</p><ul><li>For the header - <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/header.html</em></li><li>For the footer - <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em></li></ul><p>You can add a logo to the header or footer in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Encode the logo image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the <em>img src</em> attribute.</li><li><p>Add a URL to the remote image as the value of the <em>img src</em> attribute.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e209f687-ce41-4ec5-9e89-bf3cf62f58e2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The URL must be absolute, relative URLs do not work.</li><li>The URL must be accessible from the server where Web Application Platform is deployed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Example of adding a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string</h3><p>Let’s say you want to use a logo in the header of the exported PDF file pages.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="602dab10-7658-4e4e-83be-83dd8a42ce2c">Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or use your own code/tools to make the conversion.</li><li data-uuid="4ed2a51f-5927-41ec-b1be-913bd811db66">Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/header.html</em> file.</li><li data-uuid="43fa63c5-19d5-4918-a84a-cfd017291ff0">Find the closing <em>style </em>tag.</li><li data-uuid="dd982a6e-a907-4e33-8119-c3bab9d9145a"><span>Below this tag, create a <em>div</em> with an <em>img</em> tag, where <em>&lt;img src=&quot;{data:image/&lt;image type&gt;;base64,&lt;Image as a Base64 encoded string&gt; }&quot; alt=&quot;&lt;alt text&gt;&quot;&gt;.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="dc3c7f52-7ca3-42a7-9a0a-d2643068f254"><span>Add the <em>style=”position: running(header)” </em>attribute to the <em>div.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="0b9a1b00-151a-401b-a188-c7db23f1930a"><span>Find the <em>@page { @top-left { content: &quot;&quot; }}</em> within the style definitions and replace the double quotes with <em>element(header).</em></span></li><li data-uuid="2ade40e2-1b10-438f-a4e3-26fd263a204a"><p>Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want. Your code may look like the one below if you use a PNG image:</p><table class="fixed-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 1090.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="background-color: rgb(241,242,244);"><strong>Code example when using the PNG image type</strong></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>&lt;div th:fragment=&quot;HeaderFragment&quot; xmlns:th=<a href="http://www.thymeleaf.org">http://www.thymeleaf.org</a>&gt;</span></p><p><span>        &lt;style&gt;</span></p><p><span>               @page { @top-left { content: element(header) }}</span></p><p><span>        &lt;/style&gt;<br />        &lt;div style=&quot;position: running(header)&quot;&gt;<br />               &lt;img src=&quot; data:image/png;base64,&quot; width=&quot;72&quot; height=&quot;22&quot; alt=&quot;Logo&quot;/&gt;<br />   &lt;/div&gt;<br />&lt;/div&gt;</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p></li></ol><p>The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the header.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logo_in_header.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An example of the exported document with the logo in the header.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of adding a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image</h3><p>Let’s say you want to use a logo in the footer of the exported PDF file pages. In this example, we will use an absolute URL as the image source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>footer</em> tag.</li><li>Add the <em>img</em> tag with <em>&lt;img src&quot;http(s)://path_to_image&quot; alt=&quot;&lt;alt text&gt;&quot; &gt;</em></li><li><p>Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the image the way you want. Your code may look like this (the CSS is inline for simplicity):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a2fd153-356b-40bf-bdf4-30fa95b7174a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Code example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<footer class="footer"
      xmlns:th="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
   
<div style="padding: 0 0 5px 5px; float: left; height: 25px;">
      <img src="https://www.3ds.com/assets/3ds-navigation/3DS_corporate-logo_blue.svg" 
           alt="Logo" style="width: auto;
           height:100%;
           object-fit: cover;">
   </div>   
   <div th:text="|${pagination.currentPageNumber}/${pagination.documentPagesSize}|" class="footer-text">
   </div>
</footer>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logo_in_footer.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063550 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00294: (2026x) Adding and configuring LDAP server

- page_id: `247063550`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063550/2026x+Adding+and+configuring+LDAP+server
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:43:58.061+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Settings application > (2026x) Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As an example of the process of adding and connecting to the LDAP server, the connection timeout is defined in milliseconds (ms) and specifies the period of response waiting time from the LDAP server.

##### Adding LDAP server

To add an LDAP server

1. Go to **Setting** application > **LDAP management** page.
2. In the right bottom corner of the page click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Create LDAP configuration** page opens.
3. Enter all required data and click the **Save**button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding and configuring a new LDAP server.

The table below describes the components of the **Create LDAP configuration** page.

| UI Component | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Configuration name | Enter the connection name of the LDAP server. Duplicate names are allowed. |
| Domain name | This is a short name identifier for the LDAP server.This field value is to be used in cases when a username conflict is detected during login operation. If the conflicting user is an internal Teamwork Cloud user, use the predefined twc domain to solve the conflict and proceed to log in as an internal user. |
| Server address | Enter the server IP address/hostname. This is a mandatory field and is editable once created. You will get an error message if you enter a duplicate server IP address or hostname. |
| Port | If you need to change the default port number. |
| Connect timeout (ms) | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the system to system to successfully authenticate a single server (5000 is the default value). If authentication fails, the system will query the next server in the queue. This field is required. |
| Read timeout (ms) | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the system to system to successfully query User DN before requiring a similar authentication service (10000 is the default value). This field is required. |
| Bind as | A mode of bind specifying whether a user connects to the LDAP server with a specific username or anonymously for finding the Distinguished Name (DN) of a user corresponding to the user trying to log into the Teamwork Cloud system.If you select Anonymous, the Username and Password are not required and the system username and password will be disabled. |
| LDAP status | The option to enable a connection with the LDAP server. When disabled users within LDAP will not be able to sign in. |
| Username | The DN of a user to connect to the LDAP server and perform queries. |
| Password | The system password to connect to the LDAP server and perform queries. |
|  | Click to save the LDAP server configuration properties. The function of this button is the same as that of the Save button on the Edit LDAP Configuration page. |
| Specific for authentication data |  |
| Search base | The authentication methods. It defines the location in the directory from which the LDAP search begins. |
| Authenticate using | Select LDAP query to search for users by LDAP query. This is the default option. Select User DN Template to search for users by User DN. |
| User DN | To store a template for mapping user authentication with LDAP servers using the LDAP distinguished names. |
| Users query | An LDAP query for searching, retrieving, and importing users, e.g., (&(cn={0})(objectClass=user)). Note that Users query and User groups query must be different. Both queries work only in the Search base scope. |
| User groups query | An LDAP query for searching, retrieving, and importing user groups, e.g., (&(cn={0})(objectClass=group)). Note that Users query and User groups query must be different. Both queries work only in the Search base scope. |
| Specific for encryption data |  |
| Encryption Protocol | The SSL and TLS are data encryption and authentication for a secure connection with the server. You can select None, SSL/TLS. Selecting None indicates you do not need to use an encryption protocol. |
| LDAP server certificate | The option to select a certificate file. The LDAP Server Certificate file is exported from the LDAP server to make a secure connection between the Teamwork Cloud Admin and LDAP server. Only files with the following extensions may be uploaded: crt, pem. |
|  | To select a certificate file (enabled if SSL/TLS is selected). |
|  | To remove the certificate file (enabled if either SSL/TLS is selected). |

****

**Authentication data**

The**LDAP query**authentication method is selected by default. The Active Directory LDAP attribute name and value should be set to**(sAMAccountName={0})**”. Besides**sAMAccountName**, you can use any attribute name, but it must be followed with “**={0}**”. The authentication information group should look like the following figure.

All LDAP users necessary to connect to Teamwork Cloud reside in *CN=Users*. The **Search Base** of this kind of LDAP server should be *CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com*. The pattern for the **Search Base** is {Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},{Grand_Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},…{n}.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

User DN Template

If the LDAP server is **OpenLDAP** or **ApacheDS**, the default attribute name is **uid**. If the LDAP server is set for **LDAP query**, the LDAP query for querying a user DN should be entered into the **Query** box. Click the following link for more information about the LDAP query [https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2254.txt](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2254.txt).

##### **Configuring encryption data**

The LDAP server connection is secured with SSL/TLS protocol (LDAPS) at default port number *636*. The Encryption Protocol must be SSL/TLS, and the LDAP server certificate file must be selected.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>Create LDAP configuration</strong> page opens.</p></li><li><p>Enter all required data and click the <strong>Save </strong>button.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="LDAP_configuration.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;">Adding and configuring a new LDAP server.</h6><p>The table below describes the components of the <strong>Create LDAP configuration</strong> page.</p><table class="wrapped fixed-width" style="width: 997.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 232.0px;" /><col style="width: 764.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th><strong>UI Component</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><strong>Configuration name</strong></td><td>Enter the connection name of the LDAP server. The pattern for the <strong>Search Base</strong> is {Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},{Grand_Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},…{n}.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ec18326-84e7-48d6-81c7-4976536d083c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Only users that are under the  Search Base will be able to log in using the <strong>User DN Template </strong>authentication method. The Encryption Protocol must be SSL/TLS, and the LDAP server certificate file must be selected.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064205 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00295: (2026x) Adding hyperlinks

- page_id: `247064205`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064205/2026x+Adding+hyperlinks
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:32:38.903+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing > (2026x) Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud supports hyperlinks to web pages, model elements, and document sections in all string-type values. You can also add cross-references to any part of a document section, including images and tables.

##### Adding hyperlinks to web pages

To add a hyperlink to a web page

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the Adress tab of an open dialog, specify the following information:
  1. In the URL box, enter the web page address to which you want to add a hyperlink.
  2. In the Text to display box, optionally type the text you want to use for the hyperlink. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_hyperlink.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to model elements

In a Cameo Collaborator document, you can hyperlinks to model elements for easier navigation in the document. Hyperlinks can be represented as element names, representation text, or any custom text. In addition, you have the ability to choose whether hyperlinks are updated when element names change.

To add a hyperlink to a model element

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Model element tab and specify the following information:
  1. In Text to display drop-down box, select one of the following options:
    - Name - the hyperlink is displayed as the element name.
    - Representation Text - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text.
    - Icon + Representation Text - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text with the element icon.
    - **Custom Text** - the hyperlink is displayed as your custom text. Type the hyperlink text in the additional **Text to display** box, which appears after selecting the **Custom Text** option. If you use custom text for the hyperlink, it cannot be updated when the element name changes. Therefore, the only available update mode (see the next step) is **Do not update**.
  2. In the Update mode drop-down box, select one of the following options:
    - Automatically updated - the element name or representation text used for the hyperlink is automatically updated when the element name changes.
    - Automatically checked - the hyperlink to an element is automatically checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.
    - Manually checked - the hyperlink to an element is manually checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.
    - Do not update - the hyperlink text stays the same regardless of any model changes.
  3. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the model element to which you want to add a hyperlink. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_model_element.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to document sections

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient way to incorporate references to any section of a Cameo Collaborator document.

To add a hyperlink to a document section or an element inside a section

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Document section tab and specify the following information:
  1. In the **Text to display** box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the section name is used instead.
  2. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section you want to add a hyperlink to. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_section.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to presentation elements

In addition to being able to cross-reference document sections, you can include hyperlinks to presentation elements within a section, like figures and tables.

To add a hyperlink to a presentation element inside a document section

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Document section tab and specify the following information:
  1. In the **Text to display** box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the presentation element name will be used instead.
  2. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section containing the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.
  3. Click the Next button.
  4. In the second step of the wizard, select the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_presentation_element.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Therefore, the only available update mode (see the next step) is <strong>Do not update</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Update mode</strong> drop-down box, select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Automatically updated</strong> - the element name or representation text used for the hyperlink is automatically updated when the element name changes.</li><li><strong>Automatically checked</strong> - the hyperlink to an element is automatically checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.</li><li><strong>Manually checked</strong> - the hyperlink to an element is manually checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.</li><li><strong>Do not update</strong> - the hyperlink text stays the same regardless of any model changes.</li></ul></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the model element to which you want to add a hyperlink.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_model_element.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to document sections</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient way to incorporate references to any section of a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To add a hyperlink to a document section or an element inside a section</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open dialog, go to the <strong>Document section</strong> tab and specify the following information:<ol><li><p>In the <strong>Text to display</strong> box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the section name is used instead.</p></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section you want to add a hyperlink to.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_section.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to presentation elements</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">In addition to being able to cross-reference document sections, you can include hyperlinks to presentation elements within a section, like figures and tables.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To add a hyperlink to a presentation element inside a document section</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open dialog, go to the <strong>Document section</strong> tab and specify the following information:<ol><li><p>In the <strong>Text to display</strong> box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the presentation element name will be used instead.</p></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section containing the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.</li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li>In the second step of the wizard, select the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_presentation_element.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064039 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00296: (2026x) Adding items to the table of contents

- page_id: `247064039`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064039/2026x+Adding+items+to+the+table+of+contents
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T13:48:56.508+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In PDF documents, the table of contents is generated automatically. For an item to be added to the table of contents, the following conditions must be met:

- The item must be a value of an HTML heading tag.
- The heading tag must be in the range from h1 to h6.
- The heading tag needs to have the toc-item class assigned, e.g., <h1 class="toc-item">New heading</h1>.
- The heading tag needs to have a unique ID attribute.

##### Example of including a new heading in the table of contents

Let’s assume you want to add a new level 2 heading to the Comments Summary page and include it in the table of contents.

To add a new heading and include it in the table of contents

1. Open the <template folder>/comment/comments-summary.html file.
2. Add the h2 heading tag with its text at the required place.
3. Add the toc-item class to the h2 element.
4. If you want the heading to be at the bottom of the Comments Summary page, add it as an h2 element before the last closing div. 1.3 New heading

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In PDF documents, the table of contents is generated automatically. For an item to be added to the table of contents, the following conditions must be met:</p><ul><li>The item must be a value of an HTML heading tag.</li><li>The heading tag must be in the range from h1 to h6.</li><li>The heading tag needs to have the toc-item class assigned, e.g., &lt;h1 class=&quot;toc-item&quot;&gt;New heading&lt;/h1&gt;.</li><li data-uuid="caf4b4eb-4535-4137-a858-8d121578a211"><span>The heading tag needs to have a unique ID attribute.</span></li></ul><h3><br />Example of including a new heading in the table of contents</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to add a new level 2 heading to the Comments Summary page and include it in the table of contents.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new heading and include it in the table of contents</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/comment/comments-summary.html</em> file.</li><li>Add the <em>h2</em> heading tag with its text at the required place.</li><li>Add the <em>toc-item</em> class to the <em>h2</em> element.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you want the heading to be at the bottom of the Comments Summary page, add it as an h2 element before the last closing div.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be6db61f-68ee-4439-aab5-fd9d74104bf4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[…
<h2 id="new-heading-1-3" class="toc-item">1.3 New heading</h2>

</div>
</body>
</html>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If document comments are exported using the updated template with the selected option to generate the table of contents, the new heading will be displayed on the Comments Summary page and included in the table of contents.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_summary_page_with_new_heading.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the Comments Summary page with a new heading.</h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="380" ac:width="712"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_of_contents_with_new_heading.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the table of contents with a new heading.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064154 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00298: (2026x) Adding media

- page_id: `247064154`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064154/2026x+Adding+media
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:33:33.583+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing > (2026x) Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you publish a Cameo Collaborator document for editing, you can decorate it by adding images and videos. Media files are added as new document paragraphs, allowing you to update them or change their location anytime.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To add media to a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing documents' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

#### TIP: Supported file formats

Supported file formats

Cameo Collaborator supports the following media file formats: **.jpg**, **.jpeg**, **.png**, **.svg**, **.gif**, **.mp4**, and **.mov**.

To add media to a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the **Add media** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to add media. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Add media pane, do the following:
  1. Optionally enter the media title in the Media title field. If you skip this step, the media file name will be used as the media title.
  2. If needed, change the media number in the Media number field.
  3. Drag the media file to the box at the bottom of the Add media pane, or click the box and select the file from your computer. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragging_media_file.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the Add media pane.
6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 to add more media files or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

##### Changing media location

You can change the location of the media added to a Cameo Collaborator document by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change media location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Cut icon under the media whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_media.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the media. TipIf you want to move the image from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Navigation pane' space=''] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the image. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] to exit the editing mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

paragraphs, allowing you to update them or change their location anytime.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064365 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00299: (2026x) Adding roles

- page_id: `247064365`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064365/2026x+Adding+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:17.887+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Users application > (2026x) Managing users > (2026x) Managing user roles and assignments
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a role to a user or users group

1. On the Users application , click the user you want to assign a role to. The User pane will open.
2. Go to the Roles card and click Change button.
3. In the open screen, select the Add role tab and start typing a role name in the search box.
4. Select the role you want to add from the list.
5. When the role appears in the **Assigned roles** list, optionally set a custom scope for the role. Otherwise move to the next step. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a role to a user or users group</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Users application</strong>, click the user you want to assign a role to. The <strong>User </strong>pane will open.</li><li>Go to the <strong>Roles</strong> card and click <strong>Change</strong> button.</li><li>In the open screen, select the <strong>Add role</strong> tab and start typing a role name in the search box.</li><li>Select the role you want to add from the list.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the role appears in the <strong>Assigned roles</strong> list, optionally set a custom scope for the role. Otherwise move to the next step.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_roles.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063413 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00300: (2026x) Adding user to user groups

- page_id: `247063413`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063413/2026x+Adding+user+to+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:15.975+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Users application > (2026x) Managing user groups
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Multiple selections are activated when you click these icons. Multiple selections allow you to select several users and add them to a group or copy a role assignment from another user. When using multiple selections in a groups section, you can select more than one group and delete them instantly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Select more than one user to add to the group.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Multiple selections <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99c3c607-8969-48b1-bcfb-c94a81764be2">are activated when you click these icons. Multiple selections allow you to select several users and add them to a group or copy a role assignment from another user. When using multiple selections in a groups section, you can select more than on</ac:inline-comment-marker>e group and delete them instantly.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Group_users.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Select more than one user to add to the group.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064133 space=MCS version=6 -->
## PAGE 00301: (2026x) Admin Tools

- page_id: `247064133`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064133/2026x+Admin+Tools
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-01-08T09:02:01.726+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Admin Tools package is a set of Linux utility scripts that facilitate server-side tasks. You can use these scripts to manage services (start, stop, check status), archive log and configuration files, and check the installation environment. Furthermore, you can generate shortcuts to log and configuration files in a central location for troubleshooting and monitoring configuration changes.

#### INFO: Where can I find the Admin Tools package?

Where can I find the Admin Tools package?

The[Admin Tools package](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html)*utilities_<version>.zip*is included with 2024x and newer releases, but could be used with older versions.

###### Creating shortcuts

The **add-twc-shortcuts.sh** script creates symbolic links to key log and configuration files so everything can be accessed in one location. For older versions of Teamwork Cloud, the *Utilities/AdminTools*path is created in the installation path and all utilities are copied over as well. A system path entry is set optionally in /etc/profile.d/twc.sh (applied at the next login session). Elevated permission is required to execute this script.

**Shortcut files mapping for add-twc-shortcuts.sh**

| Shortcut name | Original filename |
| --- | --- |
| conf-auth | authserver.properties |
| conf-twc | application.conf |
| conf-webapp | webappplatform.properties |
| log-auth | authentication.log |
| log-cassandra | system.log |
| log-tomcat | catalina.out |
| log-twc | server.log |
| log-webapp | webapp.log or web-app.log |
| log-webapp-admin | admin.log |

###### Managing services

You can use this set of utility scripts to manage all Teamwork Cloud services. Elevated permission is required to execute these scripts.

Note that if add-twc-shortcuts.sh was executed and system path is set, these scripts can be executed anywhere.

| Script name | Details |
| --- | --- |
| twcstats.sh[network] | Check the status of all services. If service is running, output will display: ACTIVE.When run as root/sudo, service log will be generated for each failed or stopped service.If Cassandra is installed and running on the same server, nodetool status will be used to report on Cassandra mode.Add "network" option to check port bindings of each service. |
| twc-start.sh[db] | Start all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.Services will only be started if they are installed and not active.Add “db” option to start Cassandra service (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server). |
| twc-stop.sh[db] | Stop all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.Only services that are currently running will be stopped.Add “db” option to stop Cassandra service as well. A nodetool drain will be performed first before service is stopped (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server). |
| cassandra-stop.sh | Perform proper nodetool drain before stopping the Cassandra service. This script is called by twc-stop.sh. |

###### Archiving configuration files

Use the **backup-twc-configs.sh** script to create a backup package of the current *certificate/keystore* and all Teamwork Cloud configuration files. The default backup path is *<install_root>**/TWC_Backup*. Elevated permission may be required to access files and archive path.

###### Comparing configuration files

Use **compare-twc-config.sh** to compare the current configuration against a reference set of configurations. Reference set can be either be from a backup or the no-install package.

###### Checking installation environment

The **check-env.sh** script is a tool for checking the installation environment on a server. This script does not require elevated permission to execute. See the table below for a list of checks performed.

| check-env.sh |
| --- |
| TWC version installed |
| Java version(s) installed |
| OS and version |
| Execution permissions on /tmp & /home |
| SELinux setting |
| fapolicyd service status |
| FIPS mode setting |
| Cassandra version installed |
| Cassandra /data & /logs mount types |

###### Trimming webapps

Use the**webapp-trim.sh** script to control the number of webapps to run on the server. Run with the minimum set of webapps for troubleshooting. Optimize system resources by running only the webapps that users need.

| Command | Option | Details |
| --- | --- | --- |
| webapp-trim.sh |  | Only run webapps in the $RUN_LIST specified inside the script file. |
| min | For troubleshooting and fast startup, set to minimum webapps needed. |  |
| restore | Restore server to run all the webapps. |  |
| help | Display list of options for script. |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Admin Tools package is a set of Linux utility scripts that facilitate server-side tasks. You can use these scripts to manage services (start, stop, check status), archive log and configuration files, and check the installation environment. Furthermore, you can generate shortcuts to log and configuration files in a central location for troubleshooting and monitoring configuration changes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a66fa6c3-166e-4982-881d-decff7bb53a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Where can I find the Admin Tools package?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html">Admin Tools package</a><span> </span><em>utilities_&lt;version&gt;.zip </em><span>is included with 2024x and newer releases, but could be used with older versions.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Creating shortcuts</span></h4><p>The <strong>add-twc-shortcuts.sh</strong> script creates symbolic links to key log and configuration files so everything can be accessed in one location. For older versions of Teamwork Cloud, the <em>Utilities/AdminTools </em>path is created in the installation path and all utilities are copied over as well. A system path entry is set optionally in /etc/profile.d/twc.sh (applied at the next login session). Elevated permission is required to execute this script.</p><p><strong>Shortcut files mapping for add-twc-shortcuts.sh</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Shortcut name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Original filename</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>conf-auth</p></td><td><p>authserver.properties</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>conf-twc</p></td><td><p>application.conf</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>conf-webapp</p></td><td><p>webappplatform.properties</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-auth</p></td><td><p>authentication.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-cassandra</p></td><td><p>system.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-tomcat</p></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="076746b1-7ee9-460a-8e9f-5626af8ed573">catalina.out</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-twc</p></td><td><p>server.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-webapp</p></td><td><p>webapp.log or web-app.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-webapp-admin</p></td><td><p>admin.log</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Managing services</h4><p>You can use this set of utility scripts to manage all Teamwork Cloud services. Elevated permission is required to execute these scripts.</p><p>Note that if add-twc-shortcuts.sh was executed and system path is set, these scripts can be executed anywhere.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 921.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 145.0px;" /><col style="width: 775.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Script name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Details</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>twcstats.sh<br />[network]</p></td><td><p>Check the status of all services. If service is running, output will display: ACTIVE.</p><p>When run as root/sudo, service log will be generated for each failed or stopped service.</p><p>If Cassandra is installed and running on the same server, nodetool status will be used to report on Cassandra mode.</p><p>Add &quot;network&quot; option to check port bindings of each service.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>twc-start.sh<br />[db]</p></td><td><p>Start all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.</p><p>Services will only be started if they are installed and not active.</p><p>Add “db” option to start Cassandra service (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>twc-stop.sh<br />[db]</p></td><td><p>Stop all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.</p><p>Only services that are currently running will be stopped.</p><p>Add “db” option to stop Cassandra service as well. A nodetool drain will be performed first before service is stopped (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>cassandra-stop.sh</p></td><td><p>Perform proper nodetool drain before stopping the Cassandra service. This script is called by twc-stop.sh.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Archiving configuration files</h4><p>Use the <strong>backup-twc-configs.sh</strong> script to create a backup package of the current <em>certificate/keystore</em> and all Teamwork Cloud configuration files. The default backup path is <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span></em><em>/TWC_Backup</em>. Elevated permission may be required to access files and archive path.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Comparing configuration files</h4><p>Use <strong>compare-twc-config.sh</strong> to compare the current configuration against a reference set of configurations. Reference set can be either be from a backup or the no-install package.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Checking installation environment</h4><p>The <strong>check-env.sh</strong> script is a tool for checking the installation environment on a server. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This web interface allows you to access web applications andvisually[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2000x) Users application' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2000x) Roles application' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2000x) Assigning users to resources' space='']via the easy-to-use web browser interface.The following sections describe system security and access control options to allow or restrict access to resources.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The welcome screen of the Web Application Platform.
