<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064539 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00305: (2026x) Advanced services configuration

- page_id: `247064539`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064539/2026x+Advanced+services+configuration
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:35:54.778+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this section, we will review the various settings which you may have to adjust in order to establish communication between web services and Teamwork Cloud.

Change these settings only if you need to change the default installation.

##### Increasing open file limit on Linux

Use this method to increase the maximum number of files that the entire operating system can handle at once before installing Web Application Platform with services.

To increase the open file limit for the operating system

1. Open the /etc/sysctl.conf file with a text editor as a root user: text
2. Add or modify the following line with the new limit: text
3. Save and exit the editor.
4. Apply the changes immediately by running: text
5. To verify the change, run: text

##### Connecting to remote authentication and Teamwork Cloud servers

###### Configuring remote authentication server

To configure remote authentication server

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/ webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate **Authentication server properties**and change the values as necessary: bash Authserver accessIf you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.If you have configured authserver to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.

###### Configuring remote Teamwork Cloud server

To configure remote Teamwork Cloud server

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate **Teamwork Cloud server properties**and change the values as necessary: Teamwork Cloud accessPlease ensure these credentials for twc.admin.username and twc.admin.password match those of a user with administrative privileges.If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.If you have configured Teamwork Cloud to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values. If you change any of the configuration parameters, you will need to restart the WebApp service.

###### Configuring remote Zookeeper service discovery server

To configure remote Zookeeper service discovery server

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate **Platform service discovery related properties**and change the values as necessary: /conf/zoo.cfg by default
#zookeeper.acl.enable=false
#zookeeper.acl.username=zkuser
#zookeeper.acl.password=zkpassword
# One of: all, read, write, create, delete, admin. If not set, the all permission will be used
#zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]>

###### Configuring remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services

You can configure a remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services, such as Cameo Collaborator or Server-side simulation.

To configure remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate **FlexNet/DSLS server properties**and change the values as necessary:

###### Changing client port

To change remote FlexNet license server

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate **Teamwork Cloud server properties**and change the value as necessary:

##### Setting web server protocol (HTTPS to HTTP)

By default, in order to enforce a higher level of security, web services are accessed via HTTPS. In order to change the mode of operation to HTTP, various configuration changes must be made.

We do not recommend changing HTTPS to HTTP. Perform this only if necessary.

The default port for web services is 8443. In this example, we will make the changes necessary to run over HTTP on the default port of 8443.

The WebApp server configuration is located in *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml.*

Locate the following *connector executor*section:

```text
]]>
```

Edit the section as shown below:

```text
]]>
```

The modifications consist of changing the port from 8080 to 8443, and removing a redirect that would route to the handler on port 8443.

Since we have configured this connector to listen on port 8443, we now need to remove the existing connector handler on port 8443.

Locate the following *connector*section (text shown below is truncated):

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Comment out the entire section as shown:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

By default, due to security reasons, we have established a security policy requiring access to be encrypted. To disable this, we need to edit *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/conf/web.xml.* This section is located at the very bottom of the file.

The following section:

```text
webapp
        /*                    
                                  
                              
        CONFIDENTIAL
    
]]>
```

needs to be edited as follows:

```text
webapp
        /*                    
                                  
                              
        NONE
    
]]>
```

where *transport-guarantee*is changed to *NONE*.

Next, update the configuration files to reflect the change in protocol.

- In <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties : change addresses listed in authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist to HTTP.
- In <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties : change authentication.server.protocol to HTTP.
  - Optionally, set twc.rest.protocol to HTTP if the Teamwork Cloud service will also switch protocol.
- In <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf : set url for esi.auth to the HTTP address for authentication server (e.g. url="http://<authserver>:8443" )
  - Optionally, set ssl enabled to false esi.http if the Teamwork Cloud service will also switch protocol.

Restart all Teamwork Cloud services after changes are saved. Access web addresses via HTTP once services are restarted.

Please also see more detailed instructions at [https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-10.0-doc/ssl-howto.html](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-10.0-doc/ssl-howto.html).

##### Changing Web Application Platform port

The default port of Web Application Platform is 8443. You can change this port if needed by editing the **server.xml**file in *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/conf*. The example below reconfigures web services to run HTTPS via port 8444, instead of 8443.

1. Locate this code section: xmlserver.xml]]>
2. Replace the port number: xmlserver.xml]]> The only change is the definition of the port number, which changed from 8443 to 8444.
3. Update authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist in <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties to reflect the port change.
4. Update authentication.server.port in <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties to reflect the authentication port change.
5. Provide new authentication address for twcloud service in <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf. Under esi.auth , specify url like this example: application.conf:8444"]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this section, we will review the various settings which you may have to adjust in order to establish communication between web services and Teamwork Cloud.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" number of files that the entire operating system can handle at once before installing Web Application Platform with services.</p><p><span>To increase the open file limit for the operating system</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="887edda0-dbfe-49e8-a2c1-e475a24e4326">Open the /etc/sysctl.conf file with a text editor as a root user:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43fd841c-9af1-4b9c-a882-55d09c6750b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo nano /etc/sysctl.conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d9788c17-8f86-49b1-aecb-54b3e208fdee">Add or modify the following line with the new limit:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f4093b-085f-4440-a5a7-0454f7175e76"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[fs.file-max = 20000]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d1e03137-8403-42d1-b035-cb1dd370edce"><span>Save and exit the editor.</span></li><li data-uuid="55bdcb4f-cf46-45c8-a80d-8228a3cbb860"><span>Apply the changes immediately by running:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b8e512f-9d44-415b-b6b3-ef99778ca39b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo sysctl -p]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="02c2d140-49dd-43fb-9041-88393a5592e7"><span>To verify the change, run:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="586c01fa-b160-4e70-bcd0-964aebbaf7df"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat /proc/sys/fs/file-max]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Connecting to remote authentication and Teamwork Cloud servers</h3><h4>Configuring remote authentication server</h4><p>To configure remote authentication server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the webappplatform.properties file at <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/</em><em>webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li><p>Locate <strong>Authentication server properties </strong>and change the values as necessary:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c4c0b63-3e30-401f-9b54-9984d9356f0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Authentication server properties
#

# Specify the Authentication server location.
# IP address or domain name.
authentication.server.ip=127.0.0.1

# Specify the Authentication server port.
authentication.server.port=8443

# Specify rest protocol (http or https) depending on the Authentication server setup.
authentication.server.protocol=https]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b85cbe0a-e4c7-4459-8f92-4ceba4320df9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Authserver access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.</p><p>If you have configured authserver to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4>Configuring remote Teamwork Cloud server</h4><p>To configure remote Teamwork Cloud server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the webappplatform.properties file at <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li><p>Locate <strong>Teamwork Cloud server properties </strong>and change the values as necessary:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="baa347d2-8d18-4ca7-8891-b7891fb843b6"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Specify the Teamwork Cloud IP address or domain name.
twc.ip=127.0.0.1

# Specify rest protocol (http or https) depending on the Teamwork Cloud server setup.
twc.rest.protocol=https

# Specify the Teamwork Cloud rest API port.
twc.rest.port=8111]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd10c124-c17c-4cc2-a9f4-56979d4e358e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Teamwork Cloud access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please ensure these credentials for twc.admin.username and twc.admin.password match those of a user with administrative privileges.</p><p>If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.</p><p>If you have configured Teamwork Cloud to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae118da7-2d62-499b-9a5c-55a70414096a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change any of the configuration parameters, you will need to restart the WebApp service.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4>Configuring <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">remote Zookeeper service discovery server</span></h4><p>To configure remote Zookeeper service discovery server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the webappplatform.properties file at <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li><p>Locate <strong>Platform service discovery related properties </strong>and change the values as necessary:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6c1640b-8126-46cb-bc38-543e09cd0bc7"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Specify the Zookeeper server IP address.
zookeeper.server.ip=127.0.0.1
# Specify the Zookeeper server port.
zookeeper.server.port=2181
# Specify zk node where ZooKeeper registers applications
zookeeper.base.service.path=/services
# Enable or disable service discovery. Enabled if the property does not exist
service.discovery.enabled=true
# Indicates path of services. Specify / if all services are deployed on the same Tomcat.
service.uri=/
# Enables or disables ACLs of ZooKeeper nodes under zookeeper.base.service.path. To use this functionality,
# skipACL must be set to no in the ZooKeeper configuration file, which is <zookeeper-home>/conf/zoo.cfg by default
#zookeeper.acl.enable=false
#zookeeper.acl.username=zkuser
#zookeeper.acl.password=zkpassword
# One of: all, read, write, create, delete, admin. If not set, the all permission will be used
#zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4>Configuring remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services</h4><p>You can configure a remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services, such as Cameo Collaborator or Server-side simulation.</p><p>To configure remote FlexNet license server for Web Application Platform services</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the webappplatform.properties file at <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li><p>Locate <strong>FlexNet/DSLS server properties </strong>and change the values as necessary:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab0781f4-b443-4d90-a64e-9bdb8b4ea849"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Specify if you want to use the FlexNet or DSLS framework (the default value is FlexNet).
license.framework=FlexNet

# Specify the FlexNet/DSLS license server IP:Port address.
# If the value of the 'license.dsls.server.mode' property is ORGANIZATION_DEFINED, address will be read from the DSLicSrv.txt file.
license.server=]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4>Changing client port</h4><p>To change remote FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the webappplatform.properties file at <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li><p>Locate <strong>Teamwork Cloud server properties </strong>and change the value as necessary:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd374c1a-0621-406a-94c3-b67498bf836d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Specify the Teamwork Cloud client API port.
twc.client.port=3579]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Setting web server protocol (HTTPS to HTTP)</h3><p>By default, in order to enforce a higher level of security, web services are accessed via HTTPS. In order to change the mode of operation to HTTP, various configuration changes must be made.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47507e1d-b9e9-470e-9f5b-6d474a568910"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not recommend changing HTTPS to HTTP. Perform this only if necessary.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The default port for web services is 8443. In this example, we will make the changes necessary to run over HTTP on the default port of 8443.</p><p>The WebApp server configuration is located in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml.</em></p><p>Locate the following <em>connector executor </em>section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a1f076d-284b-4b8c-bea2-f3b1be2c80a2"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector executor="tomcatThreadPool"
           port="8080" protocol="HTTP/1.1"
           connectionTimeout="20000"
           redirectPort="8443" />]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Edit the section as shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05f7d689-46fc-4bb0-8d8a-7df422367a48"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector executor="tomcatThreadPool"
           port="8443" protocol="HTTP/1.1"
           connectionTimeout="20000" />]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The modifications consist of changing the port from 8080 to 8443, and removing a redirect that would route to the handler on port 8443.</p><p>Since we have configured this connector to listen on port 8443, we now need to remove the existing connector handler on port 8443.</p><p>Locate the following <em>connector </em>section (text shown below is truncated):</p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>Comment out the entire section as shown:</p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector_2.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>By default, due to security reasons, we have established a security policy requiring access to be encrypted. To disable this, we need to edit <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/conf/web.xml.</em> This section is located at the very bottom of the file.</p><p>The following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ad9adc2-d46c-496c-9bcb-8a61cae4240a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<security-constraint>                             
    <web-resource-collection>                      
        <web-resource-name>webapp</web-resource-name>
        <url-pattern>/*</url-pattern>                    
    </web-resource-collection>                              
    <user-data-constraint>                          
        <transport-guarantee>CONFIDENTIAL</transport-guarantee>
    </user-data-constraint>
</security-constraint>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>needs to be edited as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e86d9e78-6202-4bd6-bf06-607fcadea8bc"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<security-constraint>                             
    <web-resource-collection>                      
        <web-resource-name>webapp</web-resource-name>
        <url-pattern>/*</url-pattern>                    
    </web-resource-collection>                              
    <user-data-constraint>                          
        <transport-guarantee>NONE</transport-guarantee>
    </user-data-constraint>
</security-constraint>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>where <em>transport-guarantee </em>is changed to <em>NONE</em>.</p><p>Next, update the configuration files to reflect the change in protocol.</p><ul><li>In <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties</em>: change addresses listed in <em>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist </em>to HTTP.</li><li>In <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em>: change <em>authentication.server.protocol </em>to HTTP.<ul><li>Optionally, set <em>twc.rest.protocol </em>to HTTP if the Teamwork Cloud service will also switch protocol.</li></ul></li><li>In <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf</em>: set <em>url </em>for <em>esi.auth </em>to the HTTP address for authentication server (e.g. <em>url=&quot;http://&lt;authserver&gt;:8443&quot;</em>)<ul><li>Optionally, set <em>ssl enabled </em>to <em>false esi.http </em>if the Teamwork Cloud service will also switch protocol.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br />Restart all Teamwork Cloud services after changes are saved. Access web addresses via HTTP once services are restarted.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ee51b4f-70b6-4a8b-863e-6412285ecda4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please also see more detailed instructions at <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-10.0-doc/ssl-howto.html">https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-10.0-doc/ssl-howto.html</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Changing Web Application Platform port</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The default port of Web Application Platform is 8443. You can change this port if needed by editing the <strong>server.xml </strong>file in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/conf</em>. The example below reconfigures web services to run HTTPS via port 8444, instead of 8443.</span></p><ol><li>Locate this code section:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92185101-e657-4d9e-bd47-6f6e39e4c40f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">server.xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector port="8443" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
                   sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
                   maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
            <SSLHostConfig sslProtocol="TLS"
                           protocols="TLSv1.2"
                           honorCipherOrder="true"
                           certificateVerification="none"
                           ciphers="..." >]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Replace the port number:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16f02cbb-07b8-48cd-9fed-3565c057ae08"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">server.xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector port="8444" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
                   sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
                   maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
            <SSLHostConfig sslProtocol="TLS"
                           protocols="TLSv1.2"
                           honorCipherOrder="true"
                           certificateVerification="none"
                           ciphers="..." >]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br />The only change is the definition of the port number, which changed from 8443 to 8444.</li><li>Update <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist </strong>in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties </em>to reflect the port change.</li><li>Update <strong>authentication.server.port</strong> in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties </em>to reflect the authentication port change.</li><li>Provide new authentication address for twcloud service in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf.</em> Under <strong>esi.auth</strong>, specify <strong>url </strong>like this example: <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24494788-9c14-47c4-bf77-e1b380a5659e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Authentication Server address
esi.auth {
        # Authentication Server base URL.
        url = "https://<server-address>:8444"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064035 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00306: (2026x) API reference

- page_id: `247064035`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064035/2026x+API+reference
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T09:24:17.586+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table provides a list of variables you can use in your templates.

| Variable Name | Description | Usage Scope |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Document information |  |  |
| documentInfo.name | The name of the document. | Header, footer |
| documentInfo.date | The last publishing date of the document. | Header, footer |
| documentInfo.author.userName | The name of the user who published the latest version of the document. | Header, footer |
| documentInfo.author.fullName | The full name of the user who published the latest version of the document. | Header, footer |
| documentInfo.version | Version from which the document was published. | Header, footer |
| Information about the project from which the document was published |  |  |
| projectInfo.name | The name of the project name | Header, footer |
| projectInfo.date | The last project modification date. | Header, footer |
| projectInfo.author.userName | The name of the user who was last to modify the project | Header, footer |
| projectInfo.author.fullName | The full name of the user who was last to modify the project | Header, footer |
| projectInfo.version | Project version | Header, footer |
| projectInfo.branchName | Name of the project branch | Header, footer |
| Cover page information |  |  |
| coverPage.image | The logo image as a byte array. The image is available if the document was published using the SysML for Cameo Collaborator template with a configured image. | Cover page |
| coverPage.documentInfo | Information about the document. Check documentInfo for specific fields available for use, like coverPage.documentInfo.name | Cover page |
| coverPage.projectInfo | Information about the project from which the document was published. Check projectInfo for specific fields available for use, like coverPage.projectInfo.name | Cover page |
| coverPage.author.userName | The name of the user who exported the document. | Cover page |
| coverPage.author.fullName | The full name of the user who exported the document. | Cover page |
| coverPage.dateExported | Document export date. | Cover page |
| coverPage.documentType | Document export type: DOCUMENT or COMMENTS | Cover page |
| coverPage.branchIcon | Base 64 encoded branch icon image in the SVG format | Cover page |
| exportFormat | Document export format: HTML or PDF | Cover page |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Usage Scope</strong></p></th></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="3"><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Document information</strong></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>documentInfo.name</p></td><td><p>The name of the document.</p></td><td><p>Header, footer</p></td></tr><tr name of the project name</p></td><td><p>Header, footer</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>projectInfo.date</p></td><td><p>The last project modification date.</p></td><td><p>Header, The image is available if the document was published using the <em>SysML for Cameo Collaborator</em> template with a configured image.</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.documentInfo</p></td><td><p>Information about the document. Check <em>documentInfo</em> for specific fields available for use, like coverPage.documentInfo.name</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.projectInfo</p></td><td><p>Information about the project from which the document was published. Check  <em>projectInfo</em> for specific fields available for use, like coverPage.projectInfo.name</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.author.userName</p></td><td><p>The name of the user who exported the document.</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.author.fullName</p></td><td><p>The full name of the user who exported the document.</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.dateExported</p></td><td><p>Document export date.</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>coverPage.documentType</p></td><td><p>Document export type: DOCUMENT or COMMENTS</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span>coverPage.branchIcon</span></p></td><td><p><span>Base 64 encoded branch icon image in the SVG format</span></p></td><td><p><span>Cover page</span></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>exportFormat</p></td><td><p>Document export format: HTML or PDF</p></td><td><p>Cover page</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064754 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00307: (2026x) App bar

- page_id: `247064754`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064754/2026x+App+bar
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-03-31T10:45:47.948+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The app bar is located on the top of a published model portal. You can perform the following actions from the app bar:

| App bar icon | Icon description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to show or hide the . |
|  | Click and select a Cameo Collaborator document view to open it. This icon appears next to the current document view name if the document has multiple views. |
|  | Click and type a search phrase in the document search box to that matches or includes the entered text (e.g., element names). |
|  | Click to see which historical version of the document is currently open. This icon is shown only in read-only historical versions of documents. |
|  | Click to show or hide the . |
|  | Click and select a web app to open it (e.g., Resources, Users, Roles, etc.). |
|  | Click and select Sign out to sign out of the current user account. |
|  | Click and select the action you want to perform:/- to switch from the Model view to the Document view mode or vice versa. - to enable or disable hyperlinks to a modeling tool for all paragraphs in the current document. - to enable or disable email notifications about new comments or comment replies in the current document. - to see detailed Cameo Collaborator document and project information.Help - to open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud documentation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The app bar is located on the top of a published model portal. You can perform the following actions from the app bar:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>App bar icon</th><th>Icon description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_navigation_pane.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to show or hide the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Navigation pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigation pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click and select a Cameo Collaborator document view to open it. This icon appears next to the current document view name if the document has multiple views.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="document_search.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click and type a search phrase in the document search box to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Searching in a document" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[find the content]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that matches or includes the entered text (e.g., element names).</span></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="history.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click to see which historical version of the document is currently open. This icon is shown only in read-only historical versions of documents. </span></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to show or hide the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[comments pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="plugins.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click and select a web app to open it (e.g., Resources, Users, Roles, etc.).</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sign_out.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fda79bd4-2c56-4b07-a7d5-aa3f95ee2ce8">Click and select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fda79bd4-2c56-4b07-a7d5-aa3f95ee2ce8">Sign out</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fda79bd4-2c56-4b07-a7d5-aa3f95ee2ce8"> to sign out of the current user account.</ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Click and select the action you want to perform:</p><ul><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document view modes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>/<strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document view modes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>- to switch from the Model view to the Document view mode or vice versa.</li><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening items in a modeling tool"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Show/hide paragraph links to model editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable hyperlinks to a modeling tool for all paragraphs in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subscribing to email notifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subscribe to/unsubscribe from email notifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable email notifications about new comments or comment replies in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting document information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document information]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to see detailed Cameo Collaborator document and project information.</li><li><strong>Help</strong> - to open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud documentation.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064351 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00308: (2026x) Applying the license

- page_id: `247064351`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064351/2026x+Applying+the+license
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-26T20:05:44.288+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) License management
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using the FlexNet license server

mcs

FlexNet license server installation and licensing

Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.

The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, **1101** will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.

- You need to import a floating license key of Magic Collaboration Studio to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.
- An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license.
- Magic Collaboration Studio will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.
- After applying a license, Magic Collaboration Studio permanently consumes one user connection.

To apply Magic Collaboration Studio license using FlexNet license server

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp .
2. Sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. In the **FlexNet** tab, e nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . If you want to use the [Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&version=2026x), follow this format:*server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port*
6. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click **Confirm.**
7. After applying the license, in the License information table, you will see the license that is used.

If your license server only has a single license edition for Magic Collaboration Studio, it will be automatically applied.

If your Magic Collaboration Studio is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Magic Collaboration Studio will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.

Whenever a Magic Collaboration Studio node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.

If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. Auser with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role)can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.

You can see information about your current license on the **Server license** page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Changing the license' space=''] to change or update your license.

twc

- To install the FlexNet License Server, please see FlexNet license server installation and licensing .
- You need to import a floating license key of Teamwork Cloud to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.
- An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Teamwork Cloud license.
- Teamwork Cloud will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.
- After applying a license, Teamwork Cloud permanently consumes one user connection.

Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Teamwork Cloud license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.

The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, **1101** will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.

To apply a Teamwork Cloud license using FlexNet license server

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp .
2. Sign in to Teamwork Cloud using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. In the **FlexNet** tab, e nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . If you want to use the [Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&version=2026x), follow this format:*server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port*
6. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click **Confirm.**
7. After applying the license, in the License information table, you will see the license that is used.

If your license server only has a single license edition for Teamwork Cloud, it will be automatically applied.

If your Teamwork Cloud is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Teamwork Cloud will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.

Whenever a Teamwork Cloud node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.

If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. Auser with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role)can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.

You can see information about your current license on the **Server license** page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the license' space=''] to change or update your license.

##### Using DSLS

To install DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server), see [DSLS installation and licensing](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=2026x).

You can configure the falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc license using the user interface of Teamwork Cloud Admin.

To apply the license using Teamwork Cloud Admin

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp . 8443 is the default port.
2. Sign in to falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu, select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. Choose the **DSLS** tab.
6. In the **DSLS**tab, select one of the following:
  1. **Organization defined**, and then click **SHOW LICENSES**. You do not need to set the address, as it is already defined in the DSLicSrv.txt file by the organization. The API will checkout licenses from servers defined in that file:On Windows: *C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses*On Linux and Mac: */var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses*
  2. **Custom**. Enter the IP address of the License server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . The address provided in the **address**line is used to connect to the licensing server. You can apply the triad license server for both **Organization defined** and **Custom**. If you want to use the Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server), follow one of these formats:*server-name1:port,server-name2:port,server-name3:port**server-name1:port>server-name2:port>server-name3:port*
7. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click Confirm.
8. After applying the license, you will see the used license in the License information table.

**Edition:**with DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. See [Product trigrams](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=product-trigrams&version=2026x) for the specific trigrams denoting each product.

The API will checkout licenses from servers defined in that file:</p><ul class="alternate"><li>On Windows: <em>C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses</em></li><li>On Linux and Mac: <em>/var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li><li><span><strong>Custom</strong>. </span>Enter the IP address of the License server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click <strong>SHOW LICENSES</strong><span>. T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">he address provided in the <strong>address </strong>line is used to connect to the licensing server.<br /></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c5af9f4-efbd-4f18-b091-decfe9922583"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply the triad license server for both <strong>Organization defined</strong> and <strong>Custom</strong>. If you want to use the Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server), follow one of these formats:</p><ul><li><em>server-name1:port,server-name2:port,server-name3:port</em></li><li><em>server-name1:port&gt;server-name2:port&gt;server-name3:port</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ac1e4fbb-8c18-4f45-a717-523e6c2d3d64"><span>If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click </span><strong>Confirm.</strong></li><li data-uuid="075ca460-bf18-4dae-9061-bd483c9c5dfb">After applying the license,<span> you will see the used license </span>in the <strong>License information</strong><span> table.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97b15c0f-31ec-4dc9-8c16-a8bdbebd4cfe"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Edition: </strong>with DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=product-trigrams&amp;version=2026x">Product trigrams</a> for <span>the specific trigrams denoting each product.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063445 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00309: (2026x) Assigning roles

- page_id: `247063445`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063445/2026x+Assigning+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:45:25.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Roles application > (2026x) Managing roles
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Roles application, you can assign a predefined or custom role to users or user groups as described below. When assigning roles, you can also specify the role scope (resources the role applies to) for the users or user groups the role is assigned to.

#### NOTE: Assigning roles

Assigning roles

Only users with the Resource Manager role can assign roles to users and user groups.

##### Assigning a role to a user or user group

To assign a role to a user or user group

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_role_assignments.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, select the **Add user/group** tab.
3. In the search bar, start typing a user or user group name and select the desired name from the search result list. The selected user or user group should be added to the role assignments screen. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

If you want to assign multiple roles to a user at once, you can do it in the Users application. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding roles' space=''].

##### Removing users or user groups from a role

You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the**Users**application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.

To remove a user/group from a role

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the **Find user/group** tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Roles application, you can assign a predefined or custom role to users or user groups as described below. When assigning roles, you can also specify the role scope (resources the role applies to) for the users or user groups the role is assigned to.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e636726a-ab4c-4978-991c-1dd728210913"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Assigning roles</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only users with the Resource Manager role can assign roles to users and user groups.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assigning a role to a user or user group </h3><p>To assign a role to a user or user group </p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:</span><ul><li>Select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the <strong>Role assignments</strong> card.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_role_assignments.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change role assignments</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p>When the role assignments screen opens, select the <strong>Add user/group</strong> tab.</p></li><li><p>In the search bar, start typing a user or user group name and select the desired name from the search result list. The selected user or user group should be added to the role assignments screen.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_users_to_role.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes. <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cb4036c-b122-48ba-a4de-224a721dc6a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to assign multiple roles to a user at once, you can do it in the Users application. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding roles" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removing users or user groups from a role</h3><p>You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the<strong> Users </strong>application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a user/group from a role</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:</span><ul><li>Select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the <strong>Role assignments</strong> card.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change role assignments</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the role assignments screen opens, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_user_from_role.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ce73e6d-eeaa-45cf-817a-ce374e9f0aeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064875 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00310: (2026x) Assigning users to resources

- page_id: `247064875`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064875/2026x+Assigning+users+to+resources
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:41:34.463+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Resources application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.

##### Assigning users to resources

To assign users to a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_card.png']
2. In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign. The selected user is added and displayed in the **Assigned users/groups** list.
3. Click the **Add new role** box and select the role you want to add. The assigned role is displayed on the bottom of the user card. Changing roles of assigned usersIf you want to change the roles of the user who is already assigned to the resource, select the **Find user/group** tab and use the search box to find the user whose roles you want to change. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. If needed, repeat steps 2 and 3 to assign more users to the resource.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the assigned users. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_screen.png']

The added users are assigned to the resource and given the selected roles in the resource scope.

##### Removing users from resources

You can use the Resources application to remove users assigned to a specific resource.

To remove users from a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to remove users from and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button.
2. In the resource assignments screen, select the Find user/group tab and use the search box to find the user you want to remove.
3. In the bottom right corner of the user card, click [ATTACHMENT filename='delete_user.png'] to remove the user.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assigning users to resources</h3><p>To assign users to a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:<ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select <strong>Change assignments</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the resource details pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Resource assignments</strong> card click the <strong>Change</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignments_card.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign.
````
