ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool reloadseeds]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Otherwise, Teamwork Cloud service may not start.</span></p><h3>Modifying the seed node</h3><p><span>If a seed node was added, removed, or replaced from a cluster, the seed node list will need to be updated. </span></p><p><span>Use the following command to check what seeds are currently configured for the cluster:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a0f40e3-7083-4696-a166-d4538ec1ab4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool getseeds]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Update the <em>seeds</em> list configuration, under <em>seed_provider</em> of <em>cassandra.yaml</em>, for every node in the cluster. Use the following command to apply the seed list change on a live node. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea76ee70-4443-4395-8f3f-22a6771277a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool reloadseeds]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Otherwise, the changes will not be applied until each node is restarted.</span></p><h3>Syncing nodes</h3><p><span>The Cassandra repair tool is used to fix data inconsistencies between nodes. If a down node was replaced, repair will stream differences in the data from out-of-sync data between nodes. </span></p><p><span>For incremental repair of the current node, use the command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f00da33-99f1-45f2-93c5-7e17da232a29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool repair]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Incremental repairs should be run regularly on each node. Apache recommends doing this every 7 days.</span></p><p><span>For a full repair of all data, use the command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d2520ec-1407-4bb4-97cf-c122c67befc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool repair --full]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A new node to the cluster should bootstrap and sync data from other nodes. If a new node has not streamed data from other nodes on the cluster, use the following command to initiate the process:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f54edcd-5f1e-4d59-b4c7-e6502f3af87c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool rebuild]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>For additional information, refer to the <a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/4.1/index.html">official Cassandra documentation</a>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063631 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00323: (2026x) Cassandra Data Migration

- page_id: `247063631`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063631/2026x+Cassandra+Data+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:33.572+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides guidance and procedures for migrating falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcdata stored in Apache Cassandra to a new server or cluster. The procedures outlined here provide a clean method for migrating data between databases without carrying over Cassandra configuration artifacts from the source server, such as the cluster name and number of tokens.

The migration procedures use two utility scripts provided with the falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcinstallation. These scripts can be found in *<install_root>/Utilities/AdminTools/db-tools* of your installation.

***truncate_tables.sh***

Truncate all tables for keyspaces specified in the script. By default, this script will truncate all tables in the product *twc* keyspace. The script can be used to truncate the database remotely by setting *PRIVATE_IP*.

***stream_sstables.sh***

Stream the *twc* keyspace table data to a new Cassandra database using the sstableloader tool. Provide target node address and full path to source data (where *twc* folder is located) as commandline options. Source table data must be stored under "*twc*" directory and locally accessible by the script. Use *localhost* or 127.0.0.1 as the target address if streaming to a local node.

**Prerequisites**

- Local execution access to *cqlsh*is required for both scripts.
- Target node(s) with newly-installed Cassandra and empty database
- Network connection between source and target nodes is needed for remote data streaming and/or transfer.
- Elevated system permission may be required to stream data.
- Gawk Linux package

##### Cluster to cluster (same size)

Migrating data between clusters with the same number of nodes involves direct node-to-node streaming of the data. This procedure also applies to single-node data migration (one to one). Configure the target cluster with a different cluster name than the source cluster. Before attempting data migration, make sure all target cluster nodes are up and normal. Perform the steps below for each pair of source and target nodes.

**On source node**

1. Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).
2. Perform nodetool drain.
3. Stop the cassandra service.
4. Create a copy of the twc directory (default: /data/data/twc ) and move to target node.

**On target node**

1. Install Apache Cassandra.
2. Start the cassandra service.
3. Install falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc .
4. Configure falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc with Cassandra cluster information, including replication factor.
5. Start the twcloud service to create an initial twc keyspace and tables.
6. Stop the twcloud service.
7. Execute truncate_tables.sh to truncate all twc keyspace tables.
8. Execute stream_sstables.sh to stream the source data to the target node.

##### Cluster to single node

Before attempting data migration, make sure the target node has sufficient storage capacity. The target node will initially be loaded with data from all source nodes. Database size will reduce after duplicate data is cleaned up with scheduled compaction.For example, if the source cluster has 3 nodes and 5GB of data on each node, then the target node will initially have up to 15GB of data after data is streamed from each source node.

**On source nodes**

1. Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).
2. Perform nodetool drain.
3. Stop the cassandra service.
4. Create copy of the twc directory (default: /data/data/twc ) and move to target node.

**On target node**

1. Install Apache Cassandra.
2. Configure with different cluster name in cassandra.yaml.
3. Start the cassandra service.
4. Install falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc .
5. Start the twcloud service to create an initial twc keyspace and tables.
6. Stop the twcloud service.
7. Execute truncate_tables.sh to truncate all twc keyspace tables.
8. Execute stream_sstables.sh to stream source data to target node.

After data has finished streaming, the target node will initially contain duplicate data from all source cluster nodes. The duplicate data will eventually be cleaned up with scheduled compaction. To force compaction immediately, run the following command:

```bash

```

##### Single node to cluster

To migrate data from one node to a cluster, data only needs to be streamed to one node in the cluster. All other nodes in the cluster will replicate the migrated data. Before attempting data migration, make sure all target cluster nodes are up and normal. Select a target node to perform data migration on.

**On source node**

1. Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).
2. Perform nodetool drain.
3. Stop the cassandra service.
4. Create a copy of the twc directory (default: /data/data/twc ) and move to target nodes.

**On selected target node**

1. Install Apache Cassandra.
2. Start the cassandra service.
3. Install falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc .
4. Configure falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc with Cassandra cluster information, including replication factor.
5. Start the twcloud service to create an initial twc keyspace and tables.
6. Stop the twcloud service.
7. Execute truncate_tables.sh to truncate all twc keyspace tables.
8. Execute stream_sstables.sh to stream source data to target node.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12d78814-53d9-40b9-a831-0427b516fd7d">This</ac:inline-comment-marker> page provides guidance and procedures for migrating <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="846e459b-4cd8-4b23-b59c-81cb5f8bfb38" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9dfe864f-aa44-425c-b298-c602306a9b73" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>data stored in Apache Cassandra to a new server or cluster. Provide target node address and full path to source data (where <em>twc</em> folder is located) as commandline options. Source table data must be stored under &quot;<em>twc</em>&quot; directory and locally accessible by the script. Use <em>localhost</em> or 127.0.0.1 as the target address if streaming to a local node.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Prerequisites</strong></p><ul><li><span>Local execution access to <em>cqlsh </em>is required for both scripts.</span></li><li><span>Target node(s) with newly-installed Cassandra and empty database</span></li><li><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4acbe6b8-681f-4763-b0f7-449941cf0d9e">Network connection between source and target nodes is needed for remote data streaming and/or transfer.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li><span>Elevated system permission may be required to stream data.</span></li><li><span>Gawk Linux package</span></li></ul><h3>Cluster to cluster (same size)</h3><p><span>Migrating data between clusters with the same number of nodes involves direct node-to-node streaming of the data. This procedure also applies to single-node data migration (one to one). Configure the target cluster with a different cluster name than the source cluster. Perform the steps below for each pair of source and target nodes.</span></p><p><strong>On source node</strong></p><ol><li>Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</li><li>Perform nodetool The target node will initially be loaded with data from all source nodes. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Database size will reduce after duplicate data is cleaned up with scheduled compaction. </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">For example, if the source cluster has 3 nodes and 5GB of data on each node, then the target node will initially have up to 15GB of data after data is streamed from each source node.</span></p><p><strong>On source nodes</strong></p><ol><li>Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</li><li>Perform nodetool drain.</li><li>Stop the cassandra service.</li><li>Create copy of the <em>twc</em> directory (default: <em>/data/data/twc</em>) and move to target node.</li></ol><p><strong>On target node</strong></p><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Install Apache Cassandra.</span></li><li>Configure with different cluster name in <em>cassandra.yaml.</em></li><li>Start the cassandra service.</li><li>Install <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cecfdcdf-8321-46ae-b0d9-9f54b699b4de" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3db4dced-69d8-4bca-8591-798e4bce99dc" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</li><li>Start the twcloud service to create an initial <em>twc</em> keyspace and tables.</li><li>Stop the twcloud service.</li><li>Execute <em>truncate_tables.sh</em> to truncate all <em>twc</em> keyspace tables.</li><li>Execute <em>stream_sstables.sh </em>to stream source data to target node.</li></ol><p>After data has finished streaming, the target node will initiall<span>y contain duplicate data from all source cluster nodes. The duplicate data will eventually be cleaned up with scheduled compaction. To force compaction immediately, run the following command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d52f9556-75bf-4135-bbd2-9ece19b49776" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool compact twc]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Single node to cluster</h3><p><span>To migrate data from one node to a cluster, data only needs to be streamed to one node in the cluster. All other nodes in the cluster will replicate the migrated data. Before attempting data migration, make sure all target cluster nodes are up and normal. Select a target node to perform data migration on.</span></p><p><strong>On source node</strong></p><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="501df605-b4ca-4408-a102-c0ceded0baab">Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Perform nodetool drain.</li><li>Stop the cassandra service.</li><li>Create a copy of the <em>twc</em> directory (default: <em>/data/data/twc</em>) and move to target nodes.</li></ol><p><strong>On selected target node</strong></p><ol><li>Install Apache Cassandra.</li><li>Start the cassandra service.</li><li>Install <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cfb7b6bf-23e8-487c-bccb-30d7725bfcf2" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="55e06bfa-489b-4309-8d3b-e36557614388" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</li><li>Configure <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9a884408-e576-4aba-a291-07d238f73778" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="adb5486e-b72a-441d-9619-4ab53574631c" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>with Cassandra cluster information, including replication factor.</li><li>Start the twcloud service to create an initial <em>twc</em> keyspace and tables.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="34f71cf1-5991-4388-9010-d05ac5983598">Stop the twcloud service.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Execute <em>truncate_tables.sh </em>to truncate all <em>twc</em> keyspace tables.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">Execute </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">stream_sstables.sh </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">to stream source data to target node.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064428 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00324: (2026x) Changing appearance of custom comment fields

- page_id: `247064428`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064428/2026x+Changing+appearance+of+custom+comment+fields
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:22.630+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom comment fields are rendered in the *comment/comment-fragment.html* file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:

```text
]]>
```

##### Hiding custom comment fields

You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.

To hide custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.

##### Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields

Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.

To change the appearance of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below: ****
 ****
]]>

##### Changing the color of the custom comment field chip

You can change the default color of custom comment fields

To change the color of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/styles.html file.
2. Find the *style* tag with *th:fragment="CommentStyles"*, locate the *main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip*tag within, and change the property *background-color: #336699;* to any other desired color code: table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
 background-color: #336699;
 …
 }
…
]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Custom comment fields are rendered in the <em>comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d35c102-e4b7-4bf3-8dd8-348ccdb1ad03"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<div th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <div th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}"
        class="comment-field-chip">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </div>
</div>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Hiding custom comment fields</h3><p>You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To hide custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.</li></ol><h3><br />Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the appearance of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d1faf7-eac6-42da-b85e-54d5c5b04554"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<ul th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <li th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </li>
</ul>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the color of the custom comment field chip</h3><p>You can change the default color of custom comment fields</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the color of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/styles.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommentStyles&quot;</em>, locate the <em>main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip </em>tag within, and change the property <em>background-color: #336699;</em> to any other desired color code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bd950fc-25a4-4805-8549-6a37c6336a61"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommentStyles">
   table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
            background-color: #336699;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064564 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00325: (2026x) Changing font color

- page_id: `247064564`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064564/2026x+Changing+font+color
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-08T09:13:00.191+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS *color* property.

However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.

##### Example of changing the font color for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).

To change the font color for the whole document

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *color: rgb(90, 90, 90);* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
 …
 }
…
]]>

If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.

##### Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer

Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.

To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *.footer* class definition and change the color property value to *blue*. .footer {
 color: blue;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS <em>color</em> property.</p><p>However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color for the whole document</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>color: rgb(90, 90, 90);</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81175f14-144a-4477-83e0-a76157a56f7e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_font_color.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the style tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>.footer</em> class definition and change the color property value to <em>blue</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d0c6117-27fb-43b9-8a82-cf5ed71f297c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   .footer {
            color: blue;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_pagination_font_color.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064568 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00327: (2026x) Changing font size

- page_id: `247064568`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064568/2026x+Changing+font+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:25.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard *font-size* CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the *<template_folder>/common/styles.html* template.

Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.

##### Example of changing the font size for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.

To change the font size for the whole document to 15px

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *font-size: 15px;* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 font-size: 15px;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard <em>font-size</em> CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> template.</p><p>Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.</p><h3><br />Example of changing the font size for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font size for the whole document to 15px</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>font-size: 15px;</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bb7ad3-f7c4-477d-89bc-1aa3755e7bf5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            font-size: 15px;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064522 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00328: (2026x) Changing server or service address

- page_id: `247064522`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064522/2026x+Changing+server+or+service+address
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:24.003+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.

###### Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment

Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.

| Configuration file | Parameter |
| --- | --- |
| authserver.properties | authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist |
| webappplatform.properties | authentication.server.ip |
| service.url |  |

###### Updating server address for all deployments

If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.

| Configuration file to edit | Default path |
| --- | --- |
| cassandra.yaml | /etc/cassandra/conf |
| application.conf | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration |
| authserver.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |
| webappplatform.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |

###### Additional configuration for multi-server deployment

You may need additional configuration changes if services are deployed on different servers.

| Configuration file | Remote service | Parameter to check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| application.conf | cassandra | contact-points in esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver |
| webapp (authentication) | url in esi.auth |  |
| webappplatform.properties | twcloud | twc.ip |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.</span></p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuration file</span></th><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Parameter</span></th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authserver.properties</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</span></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">webappplatform.properties</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.server.ip</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">service.url</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Updating server address for all deployments</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuration file to edit</span></th><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Default path</span></th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">cassandra.yaml</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>/etc/cassandra/conf</em></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration</em></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authserver.properties</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">webappplatform.properties</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em></span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Additional configuration for multi-server deployment</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You may need additional configuration changes if services are deployed on different servers.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Configuration file</strong></span></th><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Remote service</strong></span></th><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Parameter to check</strong></span></th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">application.conf</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">cassandra</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">contact-points in esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">webapp (authentication)</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">url in esi.auth</span></td></tr><tr><td>webappplatform.properties</td><td>twcloud</td><td>twc.ip</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064815 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00329: (2026x) Changing SSL certificates

- page_id: `247064815`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064815/2026x+Changing+SSL+certificates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:03:20.882+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.

##### Changing the SSL certificate file

You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.

To change the SSL certificate file

1. Go to the <web_app_platform_installation_directory>\conf directory and open to edit the server.xml file.
2. In the server.xml file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password: ImportantIf you havecreated the SSL certificatefile as described in the above section, the **certificateKeystorePassword** property value must be the same as the **-storepass** and **-keypass** property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.
  - certificateKeystoreFile="<path_to_certificate_file>"
  - certificateKeystorePassword="<password>"
3. Save and close the server.xml file.
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.</p><h3><br />Changing the SSL certificate file</h3><p>You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the SSL certificate file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;web_app_platform_installation_directory&gt;\conf</em> directory and open to edit the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>In the <em>server.xml</em> file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password:<br /><ul><li><pre>certificateKeystoreFile=&quot;&lt;path_to_certificate_file&gt;&quot;</pre></li><li><pre>certificateKeystorePassword=&quot;&lt;password&gt;&quot;<br /><br /></pre></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="027c18b8-3066-4e11-9adb-e8dab5278eae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have </span>created the SSL certificate<span> file as described in the above section, t</span>he <strong>certificateKeystorePassword</strong> property value must be the same as the <strong>-storepass</strong> and <strong>-keypass</strong> property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064654 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00330: (2026x) Changing the font for the whole document

- page_id: `247064654`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064654/2026x+Changing+the+font+for+the+whole+document
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:31:15.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the font for the whole document, you need to do the following:

1. Specify the font in the HTML templates.
2. .
3. If you want the font of the table of contents to be other than the default Arial font, specify it in the properties file.

##### Example of changing the document font to Noto Sans

Let’s assume you want to use the Google *Noto Sans* font as the main font of the document.

To change the default font to Noto Sans

1. Change the font-family in the templates of the document-exporter:
  1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html .
  2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
  3. Locate the body tag within and change the font-family value to "Noto Sans" .
  4. Add a generic font family, e.g., sans-serif, to the end of the list so that an alternative font is selected if the main font is unavailable. body {
 font-family: "Noto Sans", sans-serif;
 …
 }
…
]]>
2. Install the font on the operating system where the document-exporter web application is deployed.
3. Open the *<web_app_platform_install_root>/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties*file and set*Noto Sans*as the value of the*fonts.toc.default*property.

##### Installing fonts

Some publicly available fonts have their own installation scripts and instructions. If this is the case, follow the official installation instructions.

Fonts must be embeddable. You can configure the export to avoid embedding for most of the fonts, but it is not recommended because all users who open the exported document need to have these fonts installed on their computers.

###### Example of installing the Noto Sans font

Let's say you need to install the Noto Sans font.

To install the Noto Sans font on Linux

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Copy the extracted font files to the /usr/share/fonts/noto directory.
4. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
5. Execute the following command to check if the new Noto sans font is installed:

To install the Noto Sans font on Windows

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Select and right-click the extracted font files, then select Install .
4. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the Noto Sans font is installed.

###### Example of installing a custom/proprietary font

Let's assume you need to install a custom font, e.g., *MyCompanyFont*, available as a .tiff file, e.g., *MyCompanyFont.tiff.*

**

To install a custom font on Linux

1. Copy the font file to the /usr/share/fonts/mycompany directory.
2. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
3. Execute the following command to check if the *MyCompanyFont* font is installed:

To install a custom font on Windows

1. Right-click the MyCompanyFont.tiff file and select Install .
2. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the MyCompanyFont font is installed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

            …
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064369 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00331: (2026x) Changing the license

- page_id: `247064369`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064369/2026x+Changing+the+license
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-26T20:55:26.134+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) License management
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using the FlexNet license server

mcs

You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.

To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server

1. In a web browser, go to http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp to open the authentication page.
2. Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account with the Server Administrator ****role
3. Open the Settings application.
4. In the left-side menu select the Server license .
5. In the License information card, click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license will open.
6. Click the **Change** button. You cannot undo your action after clicking the **Change** button.
7. If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the Server license field.
8. Click the Show Licenses button.
9. Select a new license key and click the Confirm button.

After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the **License information** card.

twc

Changing a license or updating it through the **Server license** section on the **Settings application**requires a user with a Server Administrator role. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### License information in Server settings.

The **License Information** section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use. Click **Change License**button only if you want to change your license because it will cause the current license to be deleted and allow you to select one of the available licenses and use it.

###### Getting a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account or user with a Server Administrator role.
2. Using the App bar, navigate to the Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.
4. Click **Update**. You cannot undo your action after clicking **Update**.
5. Your current license server address appears in the License Server box. Click the Show Licenses button: [ATTACHMENT filename='show_license.png'] Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form: [ATTACHMENT filename='applying_license.png']
6. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm button. Your new license will be applied and appear in the Server License section.

###### Getting a new Teamwork Cloud license from another license server

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from another license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account.
2. Using App bar, navigate to Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete your current license will open.
4. Click the Yes button.
5. Type the address and port of another license server in the Server license box.
6. Click the Show License button. All available license keys on the new server license will appear in tabular form.
7. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm . Your new license will be applied and appear in the License Information section.

##### Using DSLS

mcs

If you use DSLS, follow the steps for[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values to update the license.

twc

Use the**application.conf**file in your Teamwork Cloud installation directory to change Teamwork Cloud license. Follow the procedure for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Using the FlexNet license server</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7365524-1738-4457-bc1b-8080998ca0b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b05ed720-80cf-4802-846e-276fdca0dfda"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="952533ac-4789-4af3-8bb5-a2290759d233">In a web browser, go to <em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em> to open the authentication page.</li><li data-uuid="d9177934-3c63-4321-b13f-3e01105bb18f">Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">with the </span>Server Administrator<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <strong> </strong>role</span></li><li data-uuid="64c8e489-d445-4091-9dca-499e15ea95fe">Open the <strong> Settings </strong>  application.</li><li data-uuid="dcec6f21-3cd2-458e-814b-05e3d6379b72">In the left-side menu select the <strong>Server license</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="79a4959f-4b3f-40b9-9c81-c7daf23e7a67">In the <strong>License information</strong> card, click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_license.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">License information in Server settings.</h6><p>The <strong>License Information</strong> section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use.
````
