A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.</li><li data-uuid="bda2d3f8-258f-489d-babf-aa806295c3f0"><p>Click <strong>Update</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b036a481-38b5-4332-b822-89925f29ffb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot undo your action after clicking <strong>Update</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="175e4c38-3878-442a-8604-d65f2ac25af9">Your current license server address appears in the <strong>License Server</strong> box. Click the <strong>Show Licenses </strong>button:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_license.png" /></ac:image><br /><br />Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="applying_license.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="62615c4f-abee-4e5b-b2af-6262415bd063">Select a new license key from the table and click <strong>Confirm </strong>button. Your new license will be applied and appear in the <strong>Server</strong> <strong>License</strong> section.</li></ol><h4>Getting a new Teamwork Cloud license from another <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">license server</span></h4><p>To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from another <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">license server </span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="14db1272-8508-4f0c-87d3-670eb62c429d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Accessing web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log into the Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an Administrator account.</li><li data-uuid="6d17f4b1-f27d-457a-8bf0-64c0d9ad7fd1">Using App bar, navigate to <strong>Settings</strong> application.</li><li data-uuid="c8f90c23-bfc7-4d29-b798-891a9b53bc2c">Click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete your current license will open. </li><li data-uuid="911bffa2-c6ec-43c6-9805-b3336ae0c933">Click the <strong>Yes </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="d5842375-105e-4917-9916-3a85214d8d53">Type the address and port of another license server in the <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Server license</span></strong> box.</li><li data-uuid="58cb15f9-47c1-4505-ad12-2404277ba19f">Click the <strong>Show License </strong>button. All available license keys on the new server license will appear in tabular form.</li><li data-uuid="c94bfb78-04b0-47ab-bf3f-593730625ac2">Select a new license key from the table and click <strong>Confirm</strong>. Your new license will be applied and appear in the <strong>License Information</strong> section.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Using DSLS</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d81afad4-6105-4627-9a5f-ae95d1ad91b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you use DSLS, follow the steps for</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying the license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and change the appropriate property values to update the license.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e35b337-2db4-453a-88bc-b21fb2f561a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use the<span> </span></span><strong>application.conf</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span>file in your Teamwork Cloud installation directory to change Teamwork Cloud license. Follow the procedure for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying Teamwork Cloud license using DSLS]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and change the appropriate property values.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623472 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00332: (2026x) Changing the title of the table of contents

- page_id: `261623472`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/261623472/2026x+Changing+the+title+of+the+table+of+contents
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T13:01:38.315+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define the table of contents in the c*ommon/table-of-contents.html* template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:

- The title
- The indentation of items
- The color

##### Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document

Let’s assume you want to change the default title *Table of Contents* for a document to *Index.*

To change the default title*Table of Contents*

1. Open the *<template folder>/common/table-of-contents.html* file.
2. Find the *h1* element with a value of *Table of Contents*within the html *body*element, and change its value to *Index*: textIndex]]>

If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title *Index* would appear as the title of the table of contents:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a modified table of contents title.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can define the table of contents in the c<em>ommon/table-of-contents.html</em> template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="849acf33-e70b-47d9-970a-8a0212b0ddda"><span>The title</span></li><li data-uuid="378925af-387a-4367-b1a0-8eddbf7c7492"><span>The indentation of items</span></li><li data-uuid="1cd0283b-2fba-4acb-af23-ce5d1d18203a"><span>The color</span></li></ul><h3><span>Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document</span></h3><p><span>Let’s assume you want to change the default title <em>Table of Contents</em> for a document to <em>Index.</em></span></p><p><span>To change the default title<em> Table of Contents</em></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3acd9f8a-c9d9-4f45-9165-07853fe84e58"><span>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/table-of-contents.html</em> file.</span></li><li data-uuid="36272b8c-0f75-46f1-abdb-db2af36aed43"><span>Find the <em>h1</em> element with a value of <em>Table of Contents </em>within the html <em>body </em>element, and change its value to <em>Index</em>:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4fbc681-0c3f-400d-83c0-a7ee6fe6b6e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[…
<body>
   <h1>Index</h1>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span>If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title <em>Index</em> would appear as the title of the table of contents:</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_exported_table_of_contents.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a modified table of contents title.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064806 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00333: (2026x) Changing Web Application Platform property values

- page_id: `247064806`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064806/2026x+Changing+Web+Application+Platform+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:38.484+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the *webappplatform.properties* file. Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using no-installer' space=''], to learn more about each property.

To change Web Application Platform property values

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. Change the desired property values.
3. Restart the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file. Click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using no-installer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, to learn more about each property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change Web Application Platform property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Change the desired property values.</li><li>Restart the Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064834 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00334: (2026x) Checking current version and user count

- page_id: `247064834`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064834/2026x+Checking+current+version+and+user+count
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:41.850+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.

To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information

1. Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.
2. Go to http(s)://<cc_for_twc_host>:<cc_for_twc_port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info .

The website displays all licence information, such as licence type, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information</p><hr /><ol><li>Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.</li><li>Go to <em>http(s)://&lt;cc_for_twc_host&gt;:&lt;cc_for_twc_port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em>.</li></ol><p><br />The website displays all licence information, such as licence <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4430fa4-f2e4-49a6-a0d3-6698f17baa5b">type</ac:inline-comment-marker>, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063664 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00335: (2026x) Checking LDAP connection

- page_id: `247063664`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063664/2026x+Checking+LDAP+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:37.968+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Settings application > (2026x) Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.

To check the connection to a LDAP server

1. On the **LDAP Management** page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select **Check connection**. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messages will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the connection to a LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP Management</strong> page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Check connection</strong>. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messag<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d9608cd-f9a4-45af-af63-ab4980ff04c2">es will appear.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064515 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00336: (2026x) Collaborator View Diagram

- page_id: `247064515`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064515/2026x+Collaborator+View+Diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:39:29.961+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Publishing documents > (2026x) Document templates > (2026x) Working with custom document templates > (2026x) Creating a custom template
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.

##### The Document element

A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog when [publishing a project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x) Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface).

##### The View element

A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Enabling the Single Tree View' space=''].

The main properties of a View element are:

- Skip if Empty - if set as true , a published project does not display views without data.
- Document View - if set as true , this particular view of a document is presented as in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Enabling the Document View' space=''].
- **Allow To Switch Property View Mode** - if set as *true*, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Model View** or **Document View**.

##### The Viewpoint element

A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''].

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_template_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Document element</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when</span> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x) Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface">publishing a project</a>.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The View element</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Enabling the Single Tree View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabling the Single Tree View mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The main properties of a View element are:</p><ul><li><strong>Skip if Empty</strong> -<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, a published project does not display views without data.</span></li><li><strong>Document View</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> - </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, this particular view of a document is presented as in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Enabling the Document View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Allow To Switch Property View Mode</strong> - if set as <em>true</em>, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image>, and select <strong>Model View</strong> or <strong>Document View</strong>.</span></li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Viewpoint element<br /></span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064607 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00337: (2026x) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram

- page_id: `247064607`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064607/2026x+Collaborator+Viewpoint+Method+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:39:49.396+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Publishing documents > (2026x) Document templates > (2026x) Working with custom document templates > (2026x) Creating a custom template
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal.

Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.

##### Collect operators

In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.

##### Filter operators

Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the "FilterBy..." operator needs to have the "CollectBy..." operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.

##### Sort operators

Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.

##### Table Structure operators

Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.

##### Template operators

After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed. Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal. </p><p>Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoint_method_diagram_pallet.png" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.</span></h6><h3>Collect operators</h3><p>In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.</p><h3><br />Filter operators</h3><p>Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the &quot;FilterBy...&quot; operator needs to have the &quot;CollectBy...&quot; operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.</p><h3><br />Sort operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.</span></p><h3><br />Table Structure operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_structure_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.</h6><h3>Template operators</h3><p>After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed.  Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064699 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00338: (2026x) Comments pane

- page_id: `247064699`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064699/2026x+Comments+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:38:02.438+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['comments-pane', 'filtering-comments', 'sorting-comments', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comments pane of a published model portal.

The comments pane can be shown or hidden.

To show/hide the comments pane

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show-hide_comments_pane.png'] in the title bar of a published model portal.

##### Navigating between comments and commented items

Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.

##### Searching for comments

The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.

To search for comments

1. Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.
2. Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:
  - Under the comment search box, select the Unresolved check box, to search only for unresolved comments.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **Unread** check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **High priority** check box, to search for comments with high priority.

When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">The comments pane of a published model portal.</h6><p>The comments pane can be shown or hidden.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the comments pane</p><hr /><ul><li style="text-align: left;">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png" /></ac:image> in the title bar of a published model portal.</li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Navigating between comments and commented items</h3><p>Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commented_items_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image> </p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="graphical_comments_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Searching for comments</h3><p>The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for comments</p><hr /><ol><li>Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.</li><li>Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:<br /><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Unresolved</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a"> check box, to search only for unresolved comments.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>Unread</strong> check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>High priority</strong> check box, to search for comments with high priority.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247050151 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00339: (2026x) Committing changes

- page_id: `247050151`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247050151/2026x+Committing+changes
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-14T12:15:32.915+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Teamwork Cloud when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.

To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud

1. In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).
2. In the **Uncommitted changes** dialog, enter the commit message in the **Commit message** field. This step is optional.
3. In the same dialog, Click the **Commit** button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud. Discarding model changesIf you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the **Discard** button in the **Uncommitted changes** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Teamwork Cloud when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog, enter the commit message in the <strong>Commit message</strong> field. This step is optional.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the same dialog, Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d191a22c-cd4f-426f-ba5e-fded8b0c3c7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Discarding model changes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the <strong>Discard</strong> button in the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063321 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00340: (2026x) Common Access Card

- page_id: `247063321`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063321/2026x+Common+Access+Card
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T10:35:03.244+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration > (2026x) Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Basic configuration

In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in Web Application Platform.

Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:

```text

```

The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (**authentication.certificate.username.template**), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (**authentication.certificate.displayname.template**).

Both of these values default to using (CN)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Configuring username

```text

```

The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).

For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:

Template: *(CN)*, username: *JohnDoe*

Template: *(O)-(CN)*, username: *MyCompany*-*JohnDoe*

Template: *CERT_(CN)*, username: *CERT_JohnDoe*

##### Configuring the value displayed in the login button

To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the **authentication.certificate.displayname.template** property:

```text

```

For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is *CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB*, and the display template is *(CN) CERTIFICATE*, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.

For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Authentication by certificate' space=''].

##### Configuring truststore

CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.

Certificate verification should be enabled in the *WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml* file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on [this page](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig). E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:

- certificateVerification with the value optional or required (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).
- truststoreFile - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.
- truststorePassword - truststore password.
- truststoreType - e.g. "JKS" or "PKCS12".

##### Certificate revocation list

The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url** or **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file**, and point it to the location of the revocation list.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Basic configuration</h3><p>In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Web Application Platform.</span></p><p>Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed921e8-f752-494e-ab99-43a4e73b0620"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.enabled=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (<strong>authentication.certificate.username.template</strong>), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (<strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong>).</p><p>Both of these values default to using (CN)</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><span>Configuring username</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20ae9f6-f5bc-4ee9-b38d-e2f895e5e10d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.username.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name) </span>. </p><p>For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(CN)</em>, username: <em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(O)-(CN)</em>, username: <em>MyCompany</em>-<em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>CERT_(CN)</em>, username: <em>CERT_JohnDoe</em></p><h3><span>Configuring the value displayed in the login button</span></h3><p>To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the <strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong> property:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94afc71-b451-4bb8-bec3-e194fdeb8601"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is <em>CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB</em>, and the display template is <em>(CN) CERTIFICATE</em>, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.</p><p>For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Authentication by certificate" /></ac:link>.  </p><h3>Configuring truststore</h3><p>CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.</p><p>Certificate verification should be enabled in the <em>WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml</em> file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig">this page</a>. E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:</p><ul><li><strong>certificateVerification</strong> with the value <em>optional</em> or <em> required </em>  (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).</li><li><strong>truststoreFile</strong> - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.</li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> - truststore password.</li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> - e.g. &quot;JKS&quot; or &quot;PKCS12&quot;.</li></ul><h3>Certificate revocation list</h3><p>The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</strong> or <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</strong>, and point it to the location of the revocation list.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f3c881-22bb-4a81-85d3-737159d3974d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url=http://someserver.somedomain.com/revocation.lst
authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file=/opt/local/revovcation.lst]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063171 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00341: (2026x) Comparing elements in different versions

- page_id: `247063171`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063171/2026x+Comparing+elements+in+different+versions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T10:44:05.489+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.

##### Opening comparison screen

To compare elements in different document versions

1. Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.
2. At the top right, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Compare versions.**The version comparison screen opens.

##### Structure of comparison screen

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

- Document version drop-down. In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the Cameo Collaborator document versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the Document version drop-down list.
- Comparison mode selection. In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison.
  - Fade: this mode allows you to transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences. Fade mode is only available for diagrams. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Vertical: select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Horizontal: this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Filtering: when comparing tables, you can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Project version drop-down. When the project is changed from within the document, only the project version updates, not the document version. To compare different project versions, use the project version switcher to switch between project versions. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_project_version_comparer.png'] You can only select the project version for documents that are published for editing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.</p><h3>Opening comparison screen</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To compare elements in different document versions</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.</span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">At the top right, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Managing webhooks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Compare versions. </strong>The version comparison screen opens.</span></li></ol><h3>Structure of comparison screen</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_vertical.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><strong>Document version drop-down. </strong>In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the Cameo Collaborator document versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the <strong>Document version </strong>drop-down list.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong>Comparison mode selection.</strong> In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison. <ul><li><strong>Fade:</strong> this mode allows you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf66a76-5e62-4833-a39b-320a874ddc81"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Fade mode is only available for diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_fade.gif" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2dac002c-7218-4fcb-8912-b584b5ec30db">Vertical: </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></strong><span /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_comparison.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Horizontal:</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_comparison_horizontal.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Filtering: </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">when comparing tables, you can click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter.png" /></ac:image> next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering__compared_tables.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="3a8b4298-2023-40a2-95ac-50ad78fbd471"><strong>Project version drop-down. </strong>When the project is changed from within the document, only the project version updates, not the document version. To compare different project versions, use the project version switcher to switch between project versions.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_project_version_comparer.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccbef4a0-99e9-4c35-9b97-62934bdbc164"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only select the project version for documents that are published for editing. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247048660 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00342: (2026x) Conditional user groups

- page_id: `247048660`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247048660/2026x+Conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-29T12:24:08.200+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Users application > (2026x) Creating users and user groups
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In certain security-related authentication scenarios, user access rights must be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Teamwork Cloud allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values by using the conditional user groups feature.

When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To allow users to create conditional user groups, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2000x) Enabling conditional user groups' space='']**Conditional user groups** option must be enabled in the Settings application.
- To define the conditions for conditional user groups, SAML attributes must already be configured on the SAML page of the Settings application.

To create a conditional user group

1. Go to the Users application
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2000x) Creating users and user groups' space=''] or select to edit an existing user group.
3. On the group details pane, turn on the **Conditional user group** switch.
4. In the **Condition** box, enter the condition users must meet to be granted the group permissions.Available attributes are displayed below the **Condition**box. . When defining the condition for a conditional user group, make sure to use SAML attributes in the same way they are configured on the **SAML** page of the Settings application.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the user group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a conditional user group.

##### Condition syntax

###### Configuring SAML attributes

Conditions for conditional user groups accept only the SAML attributes configured onthe Settings application's**SAML**pageused for SAML integration.

To configure SAML attributes for conditional user groups

1. Open the Web Application Platform's Settings > SAML page .
2. Fill in the**SAML user attributes for conditional user groups**field with SAML attributes you want to use for conditional groups.
3. Save the configuration.

###### Condition operators

You can define the conditions for conditional user groups using the configured SAML attributes and the following operators:

- Boolean operators: AND; OR
- Equality operators: ==; !=
- Grouping of statements: ()

#### TIP: Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

(loginLocation == "Dallas" AND clearance == "Secret") OR department == "ITservices"

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261619813 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00344: (2026x) Configuring authentication server for SAML integration

- page_id: `261619813`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/261619813/2026x+Configuring+authentication+server+for+SAML+integration
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-27T13:06:38.499+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration > (2026x) Authentication server > (2026x) SAML integration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Configure the authentication server for SAML integration as described below.

To configure the authentication server parameters for SAML integration

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Settings application' space=''].
2. In the left-side menu of the Settings application page, select **SAML** to open the SAML configuration page.
3. In the **Configuration** tab of the **SAML configuration** page, turn on the **Enable SAML authentication** switch. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the values of the and click the **Save** button.
5. Wait for the message confirming that the SAML configuration was saved successfully.
6. Select the **Identity provider metadata** tab and upload the Identity Provider metadata by doing one of the following:
  - Click the **Upload** button and select the XML file with the Identity Provider metadata.
  - Copy the XML file's content directly into the **Identity provider metadata** tab.
7. Click the **Save** button to save the uploaded metadata. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. If the authentication server is deployed separately from Web Application Platform, restart the authentication server.

##### SAML parameters

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Entity ID | The entity ID of the Service Provider. It is generated automatically. | - |
| Name ID format | The name ID format that contains the username of the authenticated user. | urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:X509SubjectName |
| Name ID attribute | The SAML user attribute to map to the Teamwork Cloud username instead of the SAML user name ID, which is used by default. | - |
| Signature algorithm | SAML integration requests a signature algorithm. | RSA-SHA256 |
| AllowCreate Name ID policy | If enabled, adds the AllowCreate attribute to the NameIDPolicy parameter in the SAML request. | Switched on |
| Disable forced authentication | Sets ForceAuthn to true or false in the AuthnRequest in SAML-based authentication. Change it carefully, as you won't be able to log in with another user after logging out if this switch is turned on. This flag is only used in case the Identity Provider does not support single logout. | Switched off |
| AuthN contexts | (Optional) If AuthN contexts need to be used, fill in one AuthN context per line. | - |
| AuthN contexts comparison type | If AuthN contexts are used, select the AuthN Context comparison type. | exact |
| SAML button title | The button title displayed for the SAML user on the login page. | SAML |
| Show SAML authentication detailed error | If enabled, a detailed SAML authentication error text is displayed for users. | Switched off |
| Login RelayState format | The RelayState format for the authentication request. | uuid |
| Logout RelayState format | The RelayState format for the logout request. | uuid |
| SAML user attributes for conditional user groups | A list of SAML attributes (one attribute per line) that can be used while defining conditions for conditional user groups. . | - |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064832 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00345: (2026x) Configuring data logging

- page_id: `247064832`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064832/2026x+Configuring+data+logging
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:41.564+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the default data logging settings, including the logging level, for Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud as described below.

Web Application Platform uses the Apache Log4j logging framework. To learn more, see [https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/).

To configure the data logging

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the log4j2.properties file.
2. Depending on your needs do one or both of the following:
  - To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:
    - logger.webappplatform.name
    - logger.webappplatform.level
    - logger.webappplatform.additivity
    - logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref
  - To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:
    - appender.file.type
    - appender.file.name
    - appender.file.fileName
    - appender.file.filePattern
    - appender.file.layout.type
    - appender.file.layout.pattern
    - appender.file.policies.type
    - appender.file.policies.time.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.size
    - appender.file.strategy.type
    - appender.file.strategy.max
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Standard logging levels

Standard logging levels

The Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.

##### How to turn off logging

You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.

To turn off logging

1. Locate the logger you want to turn off.
2. Change the logging level of that logger to "off".
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Turning logging back on

Turning logging back on

To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

bold;"><strong>appender.file.name</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.fileName</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.filePattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.pattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.time.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.size.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.size.size</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.strategy.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.strategy.max</strong></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Restart the Java application server.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90211fe4-01f9-4daf-bd9d-7d841f113e69"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Standard logging levels</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>How to turn off logging</h3><p>You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn off logging</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ae5340b-b346-494e-aae4-c61cb3d5c452">Locate the logger you want to turn off.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Change the logging level of that logger to &quot;off&quot;.</p></li><li>Restart the Java application server.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5eea956c-4dba-4676-ba50-11ebe8d5b40e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Turning logging back on</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064804 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00346: (2026x) Configuring email notifications for services

- page_id: `247064804`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064804/2026x+Configuring+email+notifications+for+services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:38.156+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.

To enable email notifications

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file. In the *webappplatform.properties* file:The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., **mail.from.default=info@company.com**. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.
3. Specify the following properties under the *Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail* property group:
  - mail.host
  - mail.port
  - mail.username
  - mail.password
  - mail.protocol
  - mail.encoding
  - mail.from.default
4. If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the Mailing engine TLS secure properties property group (otherwise, go to the next step):
  - mail.smtp.auth
  - mail.smtp.ssl.trust
  - mail.smtp.ssl.enable
  - mail.smtp.starttls.enable
5. Restart Web Application Platform.

##### Examples

Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable email notifications</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e24de815-3242-4ef4-8043-eedd63e07e43"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., <strong>mail.from.default=info@company.com</strong>. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1a2e445c-946c-4d9e-a039-9ae91fa1be05">Specify the following properties</ac:inline-comment-marker> under the <em>Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail</em> property group:</p><ul><li><strong>mail.host</strong></li><li><strong>mail.port</strong></li><li><strong>mail.username</strong></li><li><strong>mail.password</strong></li><li><strong>mail.protocol</strong></li><li><strong>mail.encoding</strong></li><li><strong>mail.from.default</strong></li></ul></li><li>If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the <em>Mailing engine TLS secure properties</em> property group (otherwise, go to the next step):<br /><ul><li><strong>mail.smtp.auth</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.trust</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.enable</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.starttls.enable</strong></li></ul></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><h3><br />Examples</h3><p>Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f464f036-bdd7-435e-918f-ae22f87c6b74"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Without SSL/TLS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=25
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41b4a47d-ca2e-419a-95f9-a0c6cfb6f9ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">With SSL</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=465
mail.smtp.auth=true
mail.smtp.ssl.enable=true
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=anonymous
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c12e1bc5-a63e-487a-b4f4-bff3cd089500"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">With TLS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=587
mail.smtp.auth=true
mail.smtp.starttls.enable=true
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=anonymous
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988832 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00347: (2026x) Configuring Grafana

- page_id: `303988832`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/303988832/2026x+Configuring+Grafana
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-27T16:29:15.351+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Monitoring Stack > (2026x) Manual installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Prerequisites**

- Fully deployed monitoring and telemetry nodes
- Grafana dashboard JSON file for Teamwork Cloud

##### Configure Grafana

The following information is provided as example guidance. Grafana interface and exact procedure will differ by version.

1. Configuring Grafana monitoring dashboard
  1. Log in to http://MONITORINGNODE_IP:3000 - you will be displayed the Grafana Login Screen - default credentials are admin/admin. Upon logging in, you will be prompted to change the admin password.
  2. You will be presented with the following screen: [ATTACHMENT filename='grafana.png']
  3. Open the Data Sources page by clicking the gear icon in the bottom left of the webpage and then clicking **Data Sources.**Then, click **Add data source:** [ATTACHMENT filename='Add_data_source.png']
  4. Select InfluxDB: [ATTACHMENT filename='influxdb.png']
  5. Create the data sources, enter the information as in the following screenshots, and press**Save & Test**for each. After the data source gets created, click the**Data Sources**link to continue adding data sources. [ATTACHMENT filename='influx_connector1.png']
  6. Click **Save & test**, then go to the next step. [ATTACHMENT filename='influx_connector2.png']
  7. Now that the data sources have been added, select the option to import a dashboard [ATTACHMENT filename='Import_dashboard.png']
  8. To upload .json file, click the Upload .json File [ATTACHMENT filename='upload_json.png']
    - Select the provided [ATTACHMENT filename='Teamwork Cloud Dashboard.json'].
  9. At this point, you will be presented with the following screen, in which you will need to map the Telegraf data source: [ATTACHMENT filename='map_telegraf_source.png']
  10. Map the data sources as shown below and click Import [ATTACHMENT filename='import_twc_dashboard.png']
  11. Click the star next to Teamwork Cloud Dashboard at the top of the screen to save this as a favorite in Grafana. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
