<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064526 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00348: (2026x) Configuring Java heap space

- page_id: `247064526`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064526/2026x+Configuring+Java+heap+space
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-04-29T12:50:54.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The page instructs on how to manually set the memory limits for Cassandra's Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

##### Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra

To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, you must explicitly set the standard JVM arguments, **-Xms** (initial) and **-Xmx** (maximum), in the appropriate JVM options file jvmXX-server.options.

- Default file location: /etc/cassandra/conf/jvmXX-server.options
- You may need to add the -Xms/-Xmx lines if missing, or edit the existing.
- Replace jvmXX with your installed Java version (read requirements for deployed version).

The JVM Heap Size (-Xmx) must never exceed 50% of the total physical system RAM.

For example, when Cassandra is running on a dedicated server, and the server has 64 GB of RAM, you might set the heap (Xmx) to 16 GB and not more than 32 GB, even if the server has more than 64 GB of RAM.

This statement is technically accurate and hits the following key points:

1. **50% Rule:** It correctly uses the 50% rule (32GB is 50% of 64GB) as the **absolute maximum**.
2. **Best Practice:** It suggests the best-practice range by using 16GB as a likely setting.
3. **Maximum Cap:** It reinforces that the 32GB limit is a ceiling, even if the total physical RAM is much larger than 64GB.

**Edit jvmXX-server.options and add the lines regarding your need:**

Example:

```text

```

Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory. The automatic heap size calculation is capped to 15872 MB (≈15.5 GB) when using the modern G1 Garbage Collector (which is the default). 
This logic is highly conservative, which is why manual tuning of the heap (-Xmx) is the recommended best practice for high-performance servers.

The formula details are outlined below:

- For the system memory 16 GB: use 8 GB.
- For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 15.5 GB.
- For the system memory 64 GB: use 15.5 GB.

##### Configuring Java heap space for falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc

###### On Linux

- If you start falseMagic Collaboration Studiomcs falseTeamwork Cloudtwc from the executable twcloud file, you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).

###### On Windows

- If you start falseMagic Collaboration Studiomcs falseTeamwork Cloudtwc from the executable file twcloud.exe , you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).
- If you start falseMagic Collaboration Studiomcs falseTeamwork Cloudtwc from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Teamwork Cloud\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

##### Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform

Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.

###### On Linux

- Heap settings are located in <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh JVM_OPTS="-server -XX:+UseParallelGC -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M"

###### On Windows

- The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The page instructs on how to manually set the memory limits for Cassandra's Java Virtual Machine (JVM). </p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra</h3><p>To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, you must explicitly set the standard JVM arguments, <strong>-Xms</strong> (initial) and <strong>-Xmx</strong> (maximum), in the appropriate JVM options file jvmXX-server.options.</p><ul><li data-uuid="93e7f37d-e1ee-40be-9b32-cc08d7939c5d">Default file location: /etc/cassandra/conf/jvmXX-server.options</li><li data-uuid="41950ee9-6518-4a22-af3e-ee6de123b2ba">You may need to add the <span class="base"><span class="mord">-Xms</span><span class="mord">/</span><span class="mord">-Xmx</span></span> lines if missing, or edit the existing.</li><li data-uuid="a8b1909f-f0a9-43b7-9b62-80a7aee10dff">Replace jvmXX with your installed Java version (read requirements for deployed version).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc79f9e4-25fc-49e7-bc1d-d9d4687febd6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The JVM Heap Size (-Xmx) must never exceed 50% of the total physical system RAM.</p><p>For example, when Cassandra is running on a dedicated server, and the server has <span class="mord"><span class="mord mathbf">64</span> GB</span> of RAM, you might set the heap (<span class="mord">Xmx</span>) to <span class="mord"><span class="mord mathbf">16</span> GB</span> and not more than <span class="mord"><span class="mord mathbf">32</span> GB</span>, even if the server has more than <span class="mord"><span class="mord mathbf">64</span> GB</span> of RAM.</p><p>This statement is technically accurate and hits the following key points:</p><ol><li data-uuid="558ffad5-1674-46e1-ae2b-a5d5a2033369"><p><strong>50% Rule:</strong> It correctly uses the <span class="mord">50%</span> rule (<span class="base"><span class="mord">32</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span> is <span class="mord">50%</span> of <span class="base"><span class="mord">64</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span>) as the <strong>absolute maximum</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="f01909b8-f213-43d4-aab2-891f86b6b933"><p><strong>Best Practice:</strong> It suggests the best-practice range by using <span class="base"><span class="mord">16</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span> as a likely setting.</p></li><li data-uuid="58f5c7b8-c261-49f8-8f35-aff61731fdbc"><p><strong>Maximum Cap:</strong> It reinforces that the <span class="base"><span class="mord">32</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span> limit is a ceiling, even if the total physical RAM is much larger than <span class="base"><span class="mord">64</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span>.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><strong>Edit jvmXX-server.options and add the lines regarding your need:</strong></p><p>Example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1a425cd-3ea9-4b22-83f1-f1a7afaa0f23"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Xmx16G
-Xms8G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory. The automatic heap size calculation is capped to 15872 MB (≈15.5 GB) when using the modern G1 Garbage Collector (which is the default).<br />This logic is highly conservative, which is why manual tuning of the heap (-Xmx) is the recommended best practice for high-performance servers.</p><p>The formula details are outlined below:</p><ul><li data-uuid="60b73b45-9b90-4173-859d-19edd7d3594b">For the system memory 16 GB: use 8 GB.</li><li data-uuid="868fb455-34d6-4e36-b18a-7625be616154">For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 15.5 GB.</li><li data-uuid="a8527b12-0a7e-4d92-8016-7aa68bb7dd09">For the system memory 64 GB: use 15.5 GB.</li></ul><h3>Configuring Java heap space for <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8a57d23-4f78-47bd-affb-a3fb1bdcf863"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> from the executable <strong>twcloud</strong> file, you can configure the <strong>-Xmx</strong> parameter from the file <strong>jvm.options </strong>(located in the same folder).</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li data-uuid="2b20a6df-162a-49ca-9416-d5634ca715d4">If you start <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99671454-888f-4301-85ec-00742f15a2c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="baeeb417-bf46-472d-8668-8f03e6587203"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.</li></ul><p><br />To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Teamwork Cloud\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform</h3><p>Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.</p><h4>On Linux</h4><ul><li data-uuid="2aac6d2c-0f49-4e20-a527-54777713f500">Heap settings are located in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh</em><br />JVM_OPTS=&quot;-server  -XX:+UseParallelGC  -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M&quot;</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li data-uuid="ecd3b3d4-c497-4996-b63b-e21ee8e9866a">The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064830 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00349: (2026x) Configuring memory usage

- page_id: `247064830`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064830/2026x+Configuring+memory+usage
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:48:05.861+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the Java application server the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., Apache Tomcat). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.

##### Configuring memory usage on Windows

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable

1. In the Java application server home directory (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the *bin* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>\bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.bat file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following line to the *setenv.bat* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service

1. Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.
2. Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.
3. In the command-line interface, run the following command: .exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]> ImportantMake sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''].

You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to *...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. Then right-click the *Options* file and select **Modify**. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.

##### Configuring memory usage on Linux

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.

To configure memory usage on Linux

1. In the Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the *bin* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>/bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.sh file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following lines to the *setenv.sh* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server</span> the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Tomcat</span>). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.</p><h3><br />Configuring memory usage on Windows</h3><p>If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server home directory (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the <em>bin</em> folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.bat</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following line to the <em>setenv.bat</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfe54299-58bd-43d1-873d-f2abeba1da4e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[set JAVA_OPTS=-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -Xms32G -Xmx32G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="628e3463-1c92-43ed-9b2e-58cfd7f7b909"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the command-line interface, run the following command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35224c3c-500a-4a71-961d-face327d14db"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[.\bin\tomcat<version>.exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99097b00-b699-4350-ab39-3e067b9b8468"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Make sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.</span></li><li><span>The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p>You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to <em>...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>. Then right-click the <em>Options</em> file and select <strong>Modify</strong>. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.<br /><br /></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Configuring memory usage on Linux</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage on Linux</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the <em>bin</em> folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;/bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.sh</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following lines to the <em>setenv.sh</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56ce7ef-6c14-4709-aa21-88aa98e3e9fa"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xms32G"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xmx32G"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0ccbed-452d-4f51-ac7b-804ac3680242"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064742 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00350: (2026x) Configuring or changing Administrator user password

- page_id: `247064742`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064742/2026x+Configuring+or+changing+Administrator+user+password
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:33.692+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you may need to configure the Administrator user password.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

It is recommended to set up and use the default Administrator user. However, you can also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating users and user groups' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Users application' space=''] of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Admin web applications' space=''].

To set up or change the Administrator user password

1. Open the Users application and select the Administrator user. If you previously used Teamwork Cloud with a custom Administrator password and have only recently installed Web Application Platform, skip this step and proceed to step 5
2. In the **User** pane, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Change password**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Change password** pane, enter the current Administrator password, then enter and confirm the new password in the appropriate boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. Use the console of the machine where the server is deployed to open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/ *webappplatform.properties* file.
6. Change the twc.admin.password property value to the same password you set in the Users application.
7. Restart Web Application Platform (webapp service).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

However, you can also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating users and user groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new user]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Users application" /></ac:link> of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Admin web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064826 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00351: (2026x) Configuring Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS

- page_id: `247064826`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064826/2026x+Configuring+Web+Application+Platform+to+work+with+Teamwork+Cloud+via+TLS
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:40.708+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a secureconnection between the Teamwork Cloud server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Enabling secure connection between client and server' space=''].

To configure Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file do the following:
  1. Uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below. twc.client.keystore.path=twc.client.keystore.password=]]> Replace the placeholders between <> with the actual values.The **twc.client.keystore.password** property is only required if a keystore is password-protected.
  2. Change the value of the **twc.client.port** property from 3579 to 10002.
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If a secure</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> connection between the Teamwork Cloud server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b2f9679-a9d7-4821-850a-d34d420e710e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Enabling secure connection between client and server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[a secure connection between Teamwork Cloud and the client is enabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To configure Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file do the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> Uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d37d7-5ff7-45e8-8539-b8c1101f26c0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[twc.client.tls=true
twc.client.keystore.type=<keystore_type>
twc.client.keystore.path=<full_path_to_keystore_file>
twc.client.keystore.password=<keystore_password>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5491380-e569-4a08-afbf-ab41fff48a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Replace the placeholders between &lt;&gt; with the actual values.</li><li>The <strong>twc.client.keystore.password</strong> property is only required if a keystore is password-protected.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Change the value of the <strong>twc.client.port</strong> property from 3579 to 10002.</span></p></li></ol></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064828 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: (2026x) Configuring Zookeeper access control using ACLs

- page_id: `247064828`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064828/2026x+Configuring+Zookeeper+access+control+using+ACLs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:40.998+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform uses Zookeeper to coordinate the work of its services (e.g., Resources, Cameo Collaborator, Reports, etc.). Zookeeper stores the data that services must share in a hierarchical structure called a Zookeeper data tree.By default, anyone with access to the Zookeeper server can read or change the data tree nodes.However, you can limit access to Zookeeper, by configuring it to useACLs (Access Contol Lists) which provide fine-grained control over who can access and modify data stored in Zookeeper. [Learn more about Zookeeper ACLs>>](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/r3.1.2/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl)

To enable and configure access to Zookeeper using ACLs

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the file, uncomment and specify the following properties: zookeeper.acl.password=zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]> The valid values of the **zookeeper.acl.user.permissions** property are **all**, **read**, **write**, **create**, **delete**, **admin**. If the property is not specified, the **all** value is used.
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

After enabling access to Zookeeper using ACLs, you can define and/or change ACLs for Zookeeper nodesand this waydetermine which entities have access to specific nodes within the ZooKeeper distributed file system.

For example, you can configure Zookeeper to use digest authentication by running the **addauth digest user:password** command. Then you can authenticate yourself to the server using the**addauth**command in the ZooKeeper CLI. The**addauth**command sends the password hash to the ZooKeeper server, which validates it and grants you access to its nodes. Once you have authenticated yourself, you can use the ZooKeeper CLI to manage Zookeeper nodes. [Learn how to use Zookeeper CLI >>](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperCLI.html)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform uses Zookeeper to coordinate the work of its services (e.g., Resources, Cameo Collaborator, Reports, etc.). Z<span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">ookeeper stores the data that services must share in a hierarchical structure called a Zookeeper data tree</span><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">. </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">By default, anyone with access to the Zookeeper server can read or change the data tree nodes. </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">However, you can limit access to Zookeeper, by configuring it to use<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> ACLs (Access Contol Lists) which <span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">provide fine-grained control over who can access and modify data stored in Zookeeper. <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/r3.1.2/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl">Learn more about Zookeeper ACLs&gt;&gt;</a></span></span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">To enable and configure access to Zookeeper using ACLs</span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em><span> </span>directory and open for editing the<span> </span><em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the file, uncomment and specify the following properties:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7590b237-aa4b-4f35-9e0e-5efd20d836d1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[zookeeper.acl.enable=true
zookeeper.acl.username=<user_name>
zookeeper.acl.password=<password>
zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d059da5a-ebee-422e-8943-73ca154428e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The valid values of the <strong>zookeeper.acl.user.permissions</strong> property are <strong>all</strong>, <strong>read</strong>, <strong>write</strong>, <strong>create</strong>, <strong>delete</strong>, <strong>admin</strong>. If the property is not specified, the <strong>all</strong> value is used.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br />After enabling access to Zookeeper using ACLs, you can define and/or change ACLs for Zookeeper nodes<span> and this way </span><span>determine which entities have access to specific nodes within the ZooKeeper distributed file system.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">For example, you can configure Zookeeper to use digest authentication by running the <strong>addauth digest user:password</strong> command. Then you can authenticate yourself to the server using the </span><strong>addauth</strong><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);"> command in the ZooKeeper CLI. The </span><strong>addauth</strong><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);"> command sends the password hash to the ZooKeeper server, which validates it and grants you access to its nodes. Once you have authenticated yourself, you can use the ZooKeeper CLI to manage Zookeeper nodes. <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperCLI.html">Learn how to use Zookeeper CLI &gt;&gt;</a></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064764 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00353: (2026x) Content pane

- page_id: `247064764`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064764/2026x+Content+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:38:24.826+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['navigate-to-magicdraw', 'open-in-magicdraw', 'open-in-model-editor', 'zoom-in', 'zoom-out', 'hyperlinks', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content pane displays the standard properties, relations, and/or visual representation of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Navigation pane' space='']. If you select a diagram that has graphical comments, those comments are displayed on the content pane as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content pane of a published model portal.

##### Changing image size

On the content pane, images are displayed in their actual size. If an image is larger than the content pane, you can scroll to the desired part by clicking the image and dragging it. You can also zoom images in and out, as described below.

To zoom an image in or out

- If you want to zoom an image in, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the image.
- If you want to zoom an image out, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the image.

The number between the zoom in and zoom out buttons shows the percentage of the actual image size.

##### Navigating from items with hyperlinks

Some items displayed on the content pane have hyperlinks that help you navigate in a published document. If you hover the mouse pointer over an item with a hyperlink, it changes to [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Clicking that item navigates to its representation in the document. If an Item is represented in several views of a document, clicking the item opens the menu, allowing you to choose the representation to which you want to navigate. For example, if you click a Block symbol in a diagram, you can navigate to the characteristics table of that Block or its representation in different diagrams, as displayed below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### If an item with a hyperlink is displayed in several views of a document, clicking it opens a menu with navigation options.

##### Opening items in a modeling tool

If you have a modeling tool installed on your computer, you can open an item in a modleing tool (e.g., MagicDraw), right from a published document.

To open an item in a modeling tool

1. On the content pane, find the Open in Model Editor property of the item you want to open.
2. Click the item name displayed as the property value.

Clicking an item name (as displayed below) opens the related model in a modeling tool, such as MagicDraw, and selects the item in the model browser. If you click a hyperlink to a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image shows the hyperlink to the Climate Control Block in a modeling tool.

You can also enable hyperlinks for paragraphs in a published document.

To enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] in the app bar of a published model.
2. From the menu, select Show paragraph links to model editor .

When you enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool, the hyperlink icon is displayed next to each paragraph of a document, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A paragraph with a hyperlink icon that navigates to the paragraph related element in a modeling tool.

Clicking a hyperlink icon opens the related model in a modeling tool and selects the paragraph related model element in the model browser. If the related element is a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063838 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00354: (2026x) Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents

- page_id: `247063838`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063838/2026x+Contextualizing+Cameo+Collaborator+documents
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:43.529+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.

To learn more, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, refer to the following topics:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064268 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00355: (2026x) Converting users to internal or external

- page_id: `247064268`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064268/2026x+Converting+users+to+internal+or+external
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:10.340+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Users application
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two types of users in Teamwork Cloud: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.

To convert a user to internal

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the external user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to Internal. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select Convert to Internal .
2. When the Convert to Internal pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_roles.png'] to convert the user.

To convert a user to external

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to External.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select Convert to External .
2. When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click Convert . [ATTACHMENT filename='converting_users_to_external.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are two types of users in Teamwork Cloud: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to internal</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the external user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to Internal.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_internal.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to Internal</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Convert to Internal</strong> pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png" /></ac:image> to convert the user. </li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to external</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to External.</strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to External</strong>. </li></ul></li><li>When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click <strong>Convert</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_external.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064340 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00356: (2026x) Copying roles and assignments

- page_id: `247064340`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064340/2026x+Copying+roles+and+assignments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:16.156+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Users application > (2026x) Managing users > (2026x) Managing user roles and assignments
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the **User**pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the **Role Assignments** pane on the **User**pane.

To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another

1. On the User application, click the target user to open the User pane.
2. In the Roles card, click Change .
3. Select the Copy assignments tab.
4. In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.
5. Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied. [ATTACHMENT filename='copying_user_assignments.png']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the <strong>User </strong>pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the <strong>Role Assignments</strong> pane on the <strong>User </strong>pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>User </strong>application, click the target user to open the <strong>User </strong>pane. </li><li>In the <strong>Roles</strong> card, click <strong>Change</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Copy assignments</strong> tab.<strong><br /></strong></li><li>In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.</li><li>Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copying_user_assignments.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to save the changes. </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064390 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: (2026x) Creating a custom template

- page_id: `247064390`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064390/2026x+Creating+a+custom+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:19.648+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Publishing documents > (2026x) Document templates > (2026x) Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom templates allow you to customize the appearance and content of a Cameo Collaborator document. You can specify which model elements are published, what element information is displayed, and the format in which it is displayed.Acustom template can be stored in one of two ways depending on how you intend to use it:

- In a specific model - use this method if you intend to use the template to publish only one specific model.
- Stored as a separate .mdzip file - use this method if you intend to apply the template to multiple models.

You can create a custom document template as described below.

To create a custom document template

1. Open your modeling tool, and create a new model. Creating a template in the model to be publishedIf you want tostore your document template in the model you intend to publish, skip the first step and create a dedicated Package for the template inside the model. Such a template can only be used to publish the model it is stored in.
2. [Use the *Document View Collaborator Profile*](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Using+other+projects+in+a+project) in the model. Select it from the list in the *<install.root>\profiles* predefined location.
3. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Collaborator View Diagram' space='']:
  1. Right-click the route Package of the model (or the Package dedicated for storing the template) and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator View Diagram and enter the diagram name.
4. In the Collaborator View Diagram, create the Document element which is the root element of the template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template.
5. In the same diagram, create one or multiple View elements to represent different types of content.
6. Create Directed Composition relationships from the Document to each of the Views.
7. In the Collaborator View diagram, create the Viewpoint element for each of the Views created in step 5.
8. Create Conform relationships from each of the Views to its Viewpoint.
9. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''] for each Viewpoint element created in the previous step:
  1. In the Collaborator View Diagram (created in step 3), right-click the Viewpoint shape and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram and enter the diagram name.
10. In each Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram, use different types of operators (Collect, Filter, and Sort) to define how model information should be collected and displayed in each view of the document.
11. Save the model. If you have created the template as a separate file, it is recommended to save it in the modelLibraries folder of the installation directory of your modeling tool or another location dedicated for storing custom templates.

#### TIP: Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Although you can create a completely new custom template, it is highly recommended that you modify the copy of a predefined template instead. It is a quicker method since you can reuse the elements that are already created, and easily remove the elements that you don't need.

However,**DO NOT** change a predefined template. If you want to customize a predefined template, first create a copy of this template with a different name, and then modify the newly created template file.

To create a copy of a predefined template for customization

1. Open the desired predefined template from the modelLibriaries folder located in the installation directory of your modeling tool.
2. Clear the **Standard/System Profile** checkbox in the **Project Properties** dialog since it is not recommended to change system files:
  1. In the main menu, go to File > Project Properties .
  2. In the bottom left corner of the Project Properties dialog, click the More button.
  3. In the Shared Packages tab of the dialog, clear the Standard/System Profile check box.
  4. Click OK to close the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_properties dialog.png']
3. In the main menu, select **File** >**Save Project As**, enter a new name for your template, and click the **Save** button.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Custom templates allow you to customize the appearance and content of a Cameo Collaborator document. Y</span>ou can specify which model elements are published, what element information is displayed, and the format in which it is displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span>A<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> custom template can be stored in one of two ways depending on how you intend to use it:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In a specific model - use this method if you intend to use the template to publish only one specific model.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Stored as a separate .mdzip file - use this method if you intend to apply the template to multiple models.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a custom document template as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom document template</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your modeling tool, and create a new model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b867e22-5c2d-4676-94a7-f535b817408c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Creating a template in the model to be published</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you want to </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">store your document template in the model you intend to publish, skip the first step and create a dedicated Package for the template inside the model. Such a template can only be used to publish the model it is stored in.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,51,0);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Using+other+projects+in+a+project">Use the <em>Document View Collaborator Profile</em></a> in the model</span>. Select it <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">from the list in the <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em> predefined location.</span></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Collaborator View Diagram" /></ac:link>:</p><ol><li>Right-click the route Package of the model (or the Package dedicated for storing the template) and in the menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, click <strong>Collaborator View Diagram</strong> and enter the diagram name.</li></ol></li><li>In the Collaborator View Diagram, create the Document element which is the root element of the template. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template.</span></li><li>In the same diagram, create one or multiple View elements to represent different types of content.</li><li>Create Directed Composition relationships from the Document to each of the Views.<span style="color: rgb(160,0,41);"><br /></span></li><li>In the Collaborator View diagram, create the Viewpoint element for each of the Views created in step 5.</li><li>Create Conform relationships from each of the Views to its Viewpoint.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link> for each Viewpoint element created in the previous step:<ol><li>In the Collaborator View Diagram (created in step 3), right-click the Viewpoint shape and in the menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, click <strong>Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram</strong> and enter the diagram name.</li></ol></li><li>In each Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram, use different types of operators (Collect, Filter, and Sort) to define how model information should be collected and displayed in each view of the document.</li><li>Save the model. If you have created the template as a separate file, it is recommended to save it in the <em>modelLibraries</em> folder of the installation directory of your modeling tool or another location dedicated for storing custom templates.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c64cc23b-5d2d-443f-9a79-6ce9f9b9c0a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Customizing the copy of a predefined template</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Although you can create a completely new custom template, it is highly recommended that you modify the copy of a predefined template instead. It is a quicker method since you can reuse the elements that are already created, and easily remove the elements that you don't need. </p><p>However,<strong> DO NOT</strong> change a predefined template. If you want to customize a predefined template, first create a copy of this template with a different name, and then modify the newly created template file.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a copy of a predefined template for customization</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the desired predefined template from the <em>modelLibriaries</em> folder located in the installation directory of your modeling tool.</li><li><p>Clear the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> checkbox in the <strong>Project Properties</strong> dialog since it is not recommended to change system files:</p><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the bottom left corner of the <strong>Project Properties</strong> dialog, click the <strong>More</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Shared Packages</strong> tab of the dialog, clear the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> check box.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_properties dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li><p>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Save Project As</strong>, enter a new name for your template, and click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063768 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00358: (2026x) Creating a webhook

- page_id: `247063768`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063768/2026x+Creating+a+webhook
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:41.470+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Settings application > (2026x) Working with webhooks
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.

Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.

##### Creating resource-scoped webhooks

To create a resource-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Resource**as the **Webhook scope**. With this webhook scope, you can also select **Commit**or **Tagged commit**event. For the option of **Tagged commit**, enter the commit tags to listen for: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. For the webhook to work, keep the option **Enabled** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the **Resource**or **Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Once you select the resources or categories, click **Create**to create your webhook .

##### Creating model element-scoped webhooks

To create a model element-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Model element**as the **Webhook scope.** Note that **Tagged commit**is not available for this option: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. For the webhook to work, keep the option **Enabled** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the**Resource**or**Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **Next**.
10. In the third step of the wizard, select the **Branch scope**of the webhook. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. Click **Next.**
12. In the next step select the **Element scope.**You can also check the **Select recursively**box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If **Select recursively**is left unchecked, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. After making your selections, click **Create**to finish creating your new webhook.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9485d426-80a4-47ba-929b-f7272e1bb022"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating resource-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a resource-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Resource </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope</strong>. With this webhook scope, you can also select </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Commit </strong>or <strong>Tagged commit </strong>event.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the option of <strong>Tagged commit</strong>, enter the commit tags to listen for:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_webhook_creation.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enabled</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with basic authentication. </strong>If you choose this option, enter the username and password:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_url_username_and_password.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the second step of the wizard, select the <strong>Resource scope </strong>of the webhook. You can select either the <strong>Resource </strong>or <strong>Category </strong>option and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_resource_select.png" /></ac:image> <br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Once you select the resources or categories, click <strong>Create </strong>to create your webhook</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li></ol><h3>Creating model element-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a model element-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Model element </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope.<br /></strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Note that <strong>Tagged commit </strong>is not available for this option:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_webhook_step_1.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enabled</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with basic authentication. </strong>If you choose this option, enter the username and password:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_with_authentication.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the second step of the wizard, select the <strong>Resource scope </strong>of the webhook. You can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">select either the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">Resource </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">or </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">Category </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">option</ac:inline-comment-marker> and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_scope_search.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the third step of the wizard, select the <strong>Branch scope </strong>of the webhook.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_branch_scope_selection.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next.</strong></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the next step select the <strong>Element scope.<br /></strong>You can also check the <strong>S<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">elect recursively </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If <strong>Select recursively </strong>is left un</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">checked</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_element_scope.jpg" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After making your selections, click <strong>Create </strong>to finish creating your new webhook.</span></li></ol>
````
