<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063718 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00359: (2026x) Creating and editing graphical comments

- page_id: `247063718`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063718/2026x+Creating+and+editing+graphical+comments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-11T15:09:30.231+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Working with comments > (2026x) Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['creating-comments', 'adding-comments', 'creating-graphical-comments', 'adding-graphical-comments', 'editing-comments', 'editing-graphical-comments', 'creating-a-graphical-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating graphical comments

You can create graphical comments for images in a published document, such as diagrams, maps, and matrices. Later, graphical comments can be as many times as necessary. Graphical comments allow you to draw on images using various shapes, as well as add text.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To create a graphical comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the image you want to comment on. The **Create graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw as many different shapes as you want. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing textual informationTo edit the textual information of a graphical comment, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the shape palette. When the comment form opens, change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click **Done** to save the changes.
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment creation.

The graphical comment is created and displayed both on the content pane and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Comments pane' space='']. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Comments pane' space=''] between graphical comments displayed on the content pane and comments displayed on the comments pane by clicking them.

##### Editing graphical comments

You can edit the drawn shapes and/or the textual information of a graphical comment.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To edit the textual information of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
4. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
5. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
6. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save your changes, or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] to cancel comment editing.

To edit the drawing of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit drawing**. The **Edit graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw new shapes or select and edit existing shapes, as shown below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment editing.

##### Using the shape palette

When , use the shape palette on the top of the **Create graphical comment** screen to draw various shapes and even write text on a diagram. The following table describes all the icons found in the shape palette:

| Shape palette icon | Icon name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Color | Click the color icon to select one of the six available colors for the shapes you will draw. You can draw shapes using multiple colors in one graphical comment. You can also select a shape and change its color at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Text shape | Select the text shape icon, click any area of a diagram, and type the desired text. You can select and resize or move the text shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Line shape | Select the line shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a line. You can select and move the line shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Arrow shape | Select the arrow shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an arrow. You can select and move the arrow shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Freeform shape | Select the free form shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw the desired shape. You can select and resize or move the free form shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Rectangle shape | Select the rectangle shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a rectangle. You can select and resize or move the rectangle shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Ellipse shape | Select the ellipse shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an ellipse. You can select and resize or move the ellipse shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Select | Click this icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to move, resize or rotate it. |
|  | Delete | Click the delete icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to delete it. |
|  | Comment form | Click this icon to open the comment form, where you can change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click Done to save the changes.If a document has custom comment templates set, you can use them in the comment form. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Creating graphical comments</span></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="59a7532a-88c7-41fb-91f6-19176de7044f">You can create graphical</ac:inline-comment-marker> comments for images in a published document, such as diagrams, maps, and matrices. Later, graphical comments can be <ac:link ac:anchor="Editing graphical comments"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[edited]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as many times as necessary. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Graphical comments allow you to draw on images using various shapes, as well as add text.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3e502d0-7654-4a01-8392-b68075c8e9b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create graphical comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p><p>To create a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening a resource" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Cameo Collaborator document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_comment.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>On the content pane, select the image you want to comment on. The <strong>Create graphical comment</strong> screen opens.</p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Using the shape palette"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use the shape palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the top of the screen to draw as many different shapes as you want.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_graphical_comment.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba91ed5c-d689-4e31-b12d-469aea72c1bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing textual information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>To edit the textual information of a graphical comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_form.png" /></ac:image> on the shape palette. When the comment form opens, change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click <strong>Done</strong> to save the changes.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>When you finish, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment creation.</p></li></ol><p><br />The graphical comment is created and displayed both on the content pane and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[comments pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can easily <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating between comments and commented items"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> between graphical comments displayed on the content pane and comments displayed on the comments pane by clicking them.<br /><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Editing graphical comments</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can edit the drawn shapes and/or the textual information of a graphical comment.</span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d064af16-fe97-4a6e-87bd-85d611a57e0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create graphical comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li>You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To edit the textual information of a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save your changes, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment editing.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the drawing of a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit drawing</strong>. The <strong>Edit graphical comment</strong> screen opens.</p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Using the shape palette"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use the shape palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the top of the screen to draw new shapes or select and edit existing shapes, as shown below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="working_with_shapes.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>When you finish, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment editing.</p></li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Using the shape palette</h3><p>When <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating graphical comments"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating a graphical comment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, use the shape palette on the top of the <strong>Create graphical comment</strong> screen to draw various shapes and even write text on a diagram. The following table describes all the icons found in the shape palette:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 1049.94px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 91.4062px;" /><col style="width: 95.2188px;" /><col style="width: 862.312px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Shape palette icon</th><th>Icon name</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="color.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Color</td><td colspan="1">Click the color icon to select one of the six available colors for the shapes you will draw. You can draw shapes using multiple colors in one graphical comment. You can also select a shape and change its color at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="text_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Text shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the text shape icon, click any area of a diagram, and type the desired text. You can select and resize or move the text shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Line shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the line shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a line. You can select and move the line shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="arrow_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Arrow shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the arrow shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an arrow. You can select and move the arrow shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="free_form_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Freeform shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the free form shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw the desired shape. You can select and resize or move the free form shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectangle_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Rectangle shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the rectangle shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a rectangle. You can select and resize or move the rectangle shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ellipse_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Ellipse shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the ellipse shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an ellipse. You can select and resize or move the ellipse shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Select</td><td colspan="1">Click this icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to move, resize or rotate it.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Delete</td><td colspan="1">Click the delete icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to delete it.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_form.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Comment form</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click this icon to open the comment form, where you can change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click <strong>Done</strong> to save the changes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64d681cf-e075-4a49-9cd2-fa0827fa60de"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a document has <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Creating+and+editing+textual+comments#Creatingandeditingtextualcomments-Usingcustomcommenttemplate">custom comment templates</a> set, you can use them in the comment form.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063739 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00360: (2026x) Creating and editing textual comments

- page_id: `247063739`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063739/2026x+Creating+and+editing+textual+comments
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-23T13:24:42.347+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Working with comments > (2026x) Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['create-a-textual-comment', 'creating-textual-comments', 'creating-comments', 'editing-comments', 'edit-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating textual comments

You can create textual comments for any element, including standard properties and relations. Textual comments can be as many times as necessary.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- Commenting is not disabled when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface' space='']

To create a textual comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Select the item you want to comment. Commentable items are highlighted in yellow when hovered over with a mouse pointer, as shown below. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **New comment** pane on the right side of the document portal, do the following:
  1. In the Title box, enter the comment title.
  2. In the Comment area, optionally enter the comment text.
  3. If needed, turn the High priority switch on to set the comment priority to high. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the new comment pane to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to discard the comment.

The textual comment is created and displayedin the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Comments pane' space='']. The commented item in the content pane is highlighted in yellow. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Comments pane' space=''], as shown below. If an item has more than one comment, clicking it opens the list of comments, allowing you to choose the comment you want to navigate to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating from comments to commented items and vice versa.

##### Editing textual comments

You can edit any textual comment displayed on the comments pane of a published model portal, as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can edit textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model

To edit a textual comment

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment and select **Edit**. Displaying comment related actionsIf you do not see the icons of comment-related actions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment.
2. When the Edit comment pane (displayed below) opens on the right side of the document portal, do one or more of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_coment_pane.png']
  1. Edit the comment title.
  2. Edit the comment text.
  3. Click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

After completing the above steps the comment is updated and displayed on the comments pane.

##### Using custom comment template

If the project you are commenting on has a custom comment template, you can use this template when creating a comment. Simply select the template from the Comment customization template drop-down to access custom selection lists, input fields, and other elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a custom comment template.

For information on setting up a custom comment template, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063840 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00361: (2026x) Creating and managing document sections

- page_id: `247063840`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063840/2026x+Creating+and+managing+document+sections
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:34:20.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing > (2026x) Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create sections in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing documents' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

##### Creating document sections

Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a document section

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Create section icon at the top of a section. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_section.png']
4. In the Section name box of Create section pane, enter the section name.
5. Click the Place section drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Below - to create a section below the open section.
  - Above - to create a section above the open section.
  - Inner section - to c reate a subsection within the open section, positioned one level deeper in the navigation tree hierarchy. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
7. Repeat steps 3 to 6 to create more sections or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

The newly created section is empty and only has a name. Now you can fill it with content, like [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating paragraphs' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding media' space=''].

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing element values' space=''].

##### Reordering document sections

You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.

To change the position of a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change. The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below. If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section. [ATTACHMENT filename='manage_sections_dialog.png']
4. Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the Save button on the bottom right corner of the window.

##### Deleting document sections

You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

To delete a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete. The sections you can delete are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the section to delete it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c35c5514-54cf-43ec-9dd3-3c7b1451a485"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create sections in a Cameo Collaborator document:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Publishing documents" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The document must be published for editing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.</li><li>You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating document sections</h3><p>Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a document section</p><hr /><ol><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening a resource" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Cameo Collaborator document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click the <strong>Create section</strong> icon at the top of a section.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_section.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Section name</strong> box of <strong>Create section</strong> pane, enter the section name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Place section</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Below</strong> - to create a section below the open section.</li><li><strong>Above</strong> - to create a section above the open section.</li><li><strong>Inner section</strong> - to c<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">reate a subsection within the open section, positioned one level deeper in the navigation tree hierarchy.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_section_pane.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image>.</span></li><li>Repeat steps 3 to 6 to create more sections or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_editing.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.</li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);"><br />The newly created section is empty and only has a name. Now you can fill it with content, like <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating paragraphs" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[paragraphs]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding media" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[media]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7122eb52-fa79-4921-9ffc-3bd7d1ff55d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Editing element values" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Reordering document sections</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c00ab45-348e-436e-8c93-1d495d622bc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To change the position of a document section</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Manage sections</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eee084be-a0ca-4fa5-a198-18768a70a2df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd465b6f-2dee-4836-aead-8aa43fb44212"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_sections_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the <strong>Save</strong> button on the bottom right corner of the window.</li></ol><h3>Deleting document sections</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To delete a document section</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Manage sections</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01796be9-a5a3-415e-b9d7-bfaf352b64fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The sections you can delete are highlighted in the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_icon.png" /></ac:image> next to the section to delete it.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064857 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00362: (2026x) Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates

- page_id: `247064857`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064857/2026x+Creating+Cameo+Collaborator+comment+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-02-09T14:06:19.438+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Working with comments
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Profile Diagram to customize Cameo Collaborator comment templates according to your needs or specific project requirements. Add additional input fields and properties, such as version selection check boxes or a Jira issue input field, which users can see in the web interface comments panel.

Comment customization is only available for comments stored in the project.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment template

1. Open the project where you want to create a custom Cameo Collaborator comment template.
2. Create a Profile Diagram. To see the Profile Diagram option when creating a new diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog to enable the Expert mode.
3. In the Containment tree, navigate to Project Usages > Model [Document View Collaborator Profile] > Document View Collaborator Profile > Stereotypes .
4. Find the *«* CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype and drag it into the Profile Diagram you created.
5. Create a profile in your Profile Diagram. 
In this part of the process, with the « CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype applied and an empty profile created, your diagram should look something like this: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Create a stereotype element inside the newly created profile element. This is the stereotype that defines your custom comment template.
7. In the name field of the stereotype, enter the name for your custom template . This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.
8. Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. The metaclass of the new stereotype changes to Comment: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click the Create Property button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_property.png'] to add a property . Supported property types: TypeMultiplicityResultString0Not requiredString0..1Not requiredString0..*Not requiredString1RequiredString1..*RequiredStringDefaultRequiredBoolean-ShownEnumeration0Not shownEnumeration0..1List, value is not requiredEnumeration0..*Check box, value is not requiredEnumeration1List, value is requiredEnumeration1..*Not shownEnumerationDefaultList, value is required **Explanation:**
  - If you add a S tring type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as an **input field;**
  - If you add a Boolean type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as a **switch**(with a true/false value);
  - If you add an Enumeration type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as either a **selection list**or **check box(es)**, depending on the multiplicity.
10. Once you've finished editing the diagram, commit the project to the server. Now, users can use the template you created when adding comments in Cameo Collaborator.

**Example**In our custom template, let‘s display check boxes with the label **Version**and the following values: 
*2024x* 
*2024x Refresh1* 
*2024x Refresh2*

and provide an input field for a Jira issue URL.

To do this, we need to create an Enumeration type element and set the check box values as its properties:[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then, we add a property, e.g. Version, to the «Project X custom comments»**stereotype, set its type to Versions*,*and its multiplicity to 0..* to display check boxes*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

To add an input field for a Jira issue URL, we add a Jira Issue URL****property and set its type to String: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After we commit the project to the server, users can see the****Project X custom comments**template with check boxes and Jira Issue URL input field in the Cameo Collaborator web interface: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2c57aa02-d0a2-4934-9ad0-2f19c1f37be6">Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. The metaclass of the new stereotype changes to Comment:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stereotype_generalization_link_in_cc_comments_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Click the Create Property button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="9"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Customization Element Properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d95cfa0c-5583-4174-ab9a-3ae54735819d">a property</ac:inline-comment-marker>. Supported property types:<br /><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 31.3248%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 31.7952%;" /><col style="width: 35.0904%;" /><col style="width: 33.1145%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;vertical-align: top;" scope="col">Type</th><th style="text-align: center;" scope="col">Multiplicity</th><th style="text-align: center;" scope="col">Result</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;">String</td><td style="text-align: center;">0</td><td style="text-align: center;">Not required</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064846 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00363: (2026x) Creating comment backup

- page_id: `247064846`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064846/2026x+Creating+comment+backup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:42.653+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Administration Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to create a comment backup for a specific document. You can save the backup as a plain text file for future use, e.g, system recovery.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

In order to create a comment backup and import comments from the backup file, the comments must be stored in a document (not in the project).

To create the comment backup for a Cameo Collaborator document

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP GET request with the following URL and headers:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/export. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating comment backup' space=''] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating comment backup' space=''] .
2. Save the retrieved data as a plain text file.

The text file with retrieved data is your comment back up. You can use that backup to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document published from the same project.

To import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document from the backup file

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP POST request with the following URL, headers, and body:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/import. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating comment backup' space=''] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating comment backup' space=''] .
  - Body: the data you retrieved from an HTTP GET request when exporting comments from the document. When you specify the body of the HTTP request, select the raw data type and JSON data format.
2. Reload the document to which you have imported comments.

After completing the above steps and reloading the document, the imported comments should be visible in the comments pane.

##### How to find document and branch IDs

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to form a URL with document and branch IDs. The following steps explain how to find these IDs.

To find document and branch IDs

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.
3. Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.

##### How to find HTTP request headers

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.

To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request.
3. Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

GET request with the following URL and headers:<br /><ul><li><p>URL: https://&lt;host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/&lt;uuid&gt;/branch/&lt;uuid&gt;/comments/export. <ac:link /></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li>Save the retrieved data as a plain text file.</li></ol><p><br />The text file with retrieved data is your comment back up. You can use that backup to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document published from the same project.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document from the backup file</p><hr /><ol><li>Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP POST request with the following URL, headers, and body:<br /><ul><li><p>URL: https://&lt;host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/&lt;uuid&gt;/branch/&lt;uuid&gt;/comments/import. <ac:link ac:anchor="How to find document and branch IDs"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn to find document and branch IDs (UUIDs)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating comment backup" /></ac:link> you need to add to the URL.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73ca9b74-843b-4a67-af37-6c07aec460a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (&lt;&gt;) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. <ac:link ac:anchor="How to find HTTP request headers"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn to find headers for the HTTP request]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating comment backup" /></ac:link>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Body: the data you retrieved from an HTTP GET request when exporting comments from the document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b620528-a373-4ef7-9ee5-3c4ad96a180c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you specify the body of the HTTP request, select the raw data type and JSON data format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Reload the document to which you have imported comments.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps and reloading the document, the imported comments should be visible in the comments pane.<br /><br /></p><h3>How to find document and branch IDs</h3><p>When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to form a URL with document and branch IDs. The following steps explain how to find these IDs.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find document and branch IDs</p><hr /><ol><li>In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.</li><li>Select the &quot;comments&quot; HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.</li><li>Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.</li></ol><h3><br />How to find HTTP request headers</h3><p>When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers</p><hr /><ol><li>In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.</li><li>Select the &quot;comments&quot; HTTP request.</li><li>Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063948 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00364: (2026x) Creating comments and replies

- page_id: `247063948`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063948/2026x+Creating+comments+and+replies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:48.309+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Working with comments > (2026x) Working with comments as model elements
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When Cameo Collaborator comments are stored as model elements, you can create new textual comments and replies either directly in the diagram where the commented element is displayed or in the Containment tree.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in a diagram

1. Open the diagram where the element that you want to create a comment for is displayed.
2. In the diagram palette, click Collaborator Comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a comment.
3. Enter comment text on the shape of the comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to save it.
4. Create an Anchor from the comment to the commented element.
5. Find the new comment in the Containment tree of your project and move it to the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype. This Package name is typically the same as the project. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. Then place the newly created comment in this Package. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in that Package to be valid and visible in a published document.

The Cameo Collaborator Comment will be visible in a published document once you commit changes to the server and refresh the document. If you want to create comments in the Containment tree of your model, you can do it as described below.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of your model, right-click the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype and select **Create Element** > **Comment**. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in such a Package to be valid and visible in a published document.
2. Type the text of the comment and press Enter.
3. Right-click the created comment and select Refactor > Convert To > More Elements > Collaborator Comment .
4. Open the Specification window of the new Cameo Collaborator comment, select the specification cell of the Annotated Element property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
5. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the element the comment is created for and click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] to add it to the selected elements area.
6. Click OK and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This figure shows Cameo Collaborator comments in a diagram and the Containment tree.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When Cameo Collaborator comments are stored as model elements, you can create new textual comments and replies either directly in the diagram where the commented element is displayed or in the Containment tree.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the diagram where the element that you want to create a comment for is displayed.</li><li>In the diagram palette, click <strong>Collaborator Comment</strong> and click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a comment.</li><li>Enter comment text on the shape of the comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to save it.</li><li>Create an Anchor from the comment to the commented element.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the new comment in the Containment tree of your project and move it to the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype. This Package name is typically the same as the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c7c7892-4b75-4f11-9c34-f8ffa251669d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Collaborator Comments Package</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the <span>«CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. Then place the newly created comment in this Package. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in that Package to be valid and visible in a published document.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>The Cameo Collaborator Comment will be visible in a published document once you commit changes to the server and refresh the document. If you want to create comments in the Containment tree of your model, you can do it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree of your model, right-click the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Comment</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94313c6f-4b4d-44c0-930c-c33f5a866914"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Collaborator Comments Package</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in such a Package to be valid and visible in a published document.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Type the text of the comment and press Enter.</li><li>Right-click the created comment and select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong> &gt;<strong> Collaborator Comment</strong>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the new Cameo Collaborator comment, select the specification cell of the <strong>Annotated Element</strong> property, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the element the comment is created for and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add.png" /></ac:image> to add it to the selected elements area.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> and close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_comments_in_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This figure shows Cameo Collaborator comments in a diagram and the Containment tree.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064817 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00365: (2026x) Creating element values

- page_id: `247064817`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064817/2026x+Creating+element+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:40.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can create values for stereotype tags and textual properties right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool or any workarounds to add new values to your model.

To create a value for a stereotype tag

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. On the bottom of the **Edit** pane, click the **Add new value** button.
4. When a new value card is added, enter the desired value.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a stereotype tag in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a value for a textual property

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, enter the desired value in the value card.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a textual property in a Cameo Collaborator document.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064656 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00366: (2026x) Creating indexing configurations

- page_id: `247064656`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064656/2026x+Creating+indexing+configurations
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-03-16T12:39:23.784+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) Settings application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.

This chapter explains how to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To enable this functionality, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] . Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.
- Only users with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) User roles and permissions' space=''] can create indexing configurations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating indexing configurations.

The following procedure outlines the steps to

create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations. To c

To create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations

1. In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select **Indexing management**.
2. Select tab **Global Element Usage Search**at the top.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.
4. In the open wizard, make sure that the indexing configuration type **Global Element Usage Search** is selected.
5. Select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click **Next**. NoteYou can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.
6. Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click **Next**. Note on ScopeThe scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the **Select all** check box at the top of the resource tree.Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] page.
7. In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:
  - Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.
  - Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.
  - By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the Commit tag box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag. [ATTACHMENT filename='by_specific_commit_tag.png']
8. Click the Create button.

The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.</span></p><p>This chapter explains how to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="990ba527-bbea-4862-999a-2b1d12717355"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable this functionality,</span> you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install Elasticsearch and configure Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Only users with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) User roles and permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the Server Administrator and Index Manager roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can create indexing configurations.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_index_configurations.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Creating indexing configurations.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3402d835-e6f6-4002-a281-4c720c17e866"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following procedure outlines the steps to </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations. To c</span>reate an indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2CM" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="731289fa-611b-4eb2-8f42-724ac6459384"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select <strong>Indexing management</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="f15035e6-19bb-40be-9940-1693845ea4fa"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select tab <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Global Element Usage Search </strong>at the top.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="9975bbe4-62b7-468c-ab39-d124e68b9938"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.</span></li><li data-uuid="7345e99b-5b77-4e82-8fb6-f322d1b26371"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the open wizard, make sure that the indexing configuration type <strong>Global Element Usage Search</strong> is selected.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="3ec87a05-d7a3-4bf2-8f99-277161760718"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfbd7a1e-d206-44ab-a6ec-2d3b816d3ba4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4c7c85ba-9c5f-44fd-b595-ccd87cf17915"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d70c606b-3f79-40cb-825c-4a775dac23ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note on Scope</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the <strong>Select all</strong> check box at the top of the resource tree.</li><li>Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Systemrequirements"><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[System requirements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="89a718a6-7bda-42bf-aebf-d65a3fa80b7a">In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:<ul><li>Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.</li><li>Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.</li><li>By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the <strong>Commit tag</strong> box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="by_specific_commit_tag.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="19dc20d4-ac4a-4fb8-aa6a-62e72be4ecf5">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064191 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00367: (2026x) Creating paragraphs

- page_id: `247064191`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064191/2026x+Creating+paragraphs
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:33:56.335+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Editing and contextualizing > (2026x) Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published for editing, you can add new paragraphs to it with text, images, and hyperlinks to model elements or other document sections.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create paragraphs in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Publishing documents' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To create a paragraph

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the **Create paragraph** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to insert a paragraph. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Create paragraph** screen, enter the paragraph text. Text formattingUse the text formatting toolbar on the top of the **Create paragraph** screen to change the text font, size, color, alignment, etc. You can also use the toolbar to add hyperlinks, tables, and images to the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_paragraph_content.png']
5. On the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the paragraph.
6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 to create more paragraphs or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

##### Editing paragraphs

You can edit the content of a paragraph created in a Cameo Collaborator document the same way you edit textual element values. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing element values' space=''].

##### Changing paragraph location

You can change the location of a paragraph created in Cameo Collaborator by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change a paragraph location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Cut icon under the paragraph whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_paragraph.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the paragraph. TipIf you want to move the paragraph from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Navigation pane' space=''] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='pasting_paragraph.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] in the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If a Cameo Collaborator document is published for editing, you can add new paragraphs to it with text, images, and hyperlinks to model elements or other document sections.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c35c5514-54cf-43ec-9dd3-3c7b1451a485"><ac:parameter /></li><li>On the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the paragraph.</li><li>Repeat steps 3 to
