````html
<p>Data Manager is a Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the <em>Utilities </em>directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae5d4027-111e-4c93-aebc-36998ed902f8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database. </span></p><p><span>Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8fbe328-b9e7-4409-a2ab-22d34beb8d51"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fea76924-d46d-4cf2-a970-8dbd9cda34a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> distribution.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Running Data Manager</span></p><p>To run Data Manager</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Stop <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d9b61db-f329-47ab-a1c8-d3bab099979c"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7937a3ba-df47-4a4a-93ce-a0b73e86c070"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span> services to prevent users from accessing the database.</span></li><li>Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the <em>nodetool status </em>command to verify the database status is <em>Up </em>and <em>Normal</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run a Data Manager command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="655ef0b3-b022-4798-b388-ef762bed4164"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bfde1fd-b089-473b-a3a4-457533e77dcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fdd2c79-2b42-429a-8d1b-ecd3f55d5a12"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute with the <em>-help </em>option to ensure Data Manager is working:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4b00d65-6501-4b5b-96c2-8b290708d7b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[./datamanager -help]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After finishing all Data Manager steps, start <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad16296c-23a5-42bb-b02f-38749aa18a4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3de94fdb-c4d7-4479-9bdc-b46177ec06a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span> services to resume user access.</p></li></ol><h3>Data Manager configuration</h3><p>Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under <em>configuration</em>.</p><h4>Database access</h4><p><span>For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the </span><strong>contact-points </strong>field for <strong>datastax-java-driver</strong><span><strong> </strong>in application.conf.</span></p><h4><span>Cassandra Authentication</span></h4><p><span>Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than <em>AllowAllAuthenticator</em>, you will need to modify the </span><strong>auth-provider</strong><span><strong> </strong>fields in<em> </em>application.conf accordingly.</span></p><h4><span>Logs</span></h4><p><span>Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.</span></p><h3>Data Manager commands</h3><p>The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Server tool command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><p>-help</p></td><td>Displays Help information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks</p></td><td>Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-rbs, --recalculate-blob-size</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd1424db-54b5-492f-a9b5-f407e645da84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-dk, --drop-keyspace &lt;keyspace=value&gt; --silent-drop</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drops persistent keyspace to free up the storage space.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2db5e6d8-2752-4a8e-b143-6b0bc330ee17">Add <strong>--silent-drop </strong>to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.</li><li data-uuid="e786c66f-80a5-4ddf-920d-8c154418b27c"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the <strong>nodetool</strong> utility command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c312e9-91b9-486b-8369-34c5278b1154"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ nodetool clearsnapshot -- <keyspace>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-dr,--delete-resource &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3b9b41-1672-4a1d-90f7-c850b049f90a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drb,--delete-branch &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt; includeChildBranch=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildBranch</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="1009f89b-53ee-4f1d-93f1-8679e88b6756">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong>, and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="0de3873c-832b-4c86-8cf9-ae191f64802a">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>, </em>the branch including its child branches will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d1bcbc-2d3c-48ad-8f9f-00ddb873487f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22febd0d-99ad-4f01-af62-a1f821385f84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drr,--delete-revision &lt;resourceID=value revision=&lt;revision1,revision2&gt; includeChildRevision=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildRevision</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="4a8c0db5-5c17-40d9-9305-f7ddd0d4cadd">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong><em>, </em>and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="f66c065e-1044-4d68-808e-0e56729f41dc">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>,</em> the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8a1990-5cc3-4fae-bcea-8b43d2cdd8d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-du,--delete-user &lt;username&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.</p><ul><li data-uuid="ee1ccc86-f955-4910-9aeb-4f9e7c7fd8ef">To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.</li><li data-uuid="0dbfade5-9524-4834-85b6-9d74acf09110"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the username consists of two words (for example, <em>dan smith</em>) add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0302fbce-a9ca-4057-9ea5-680c8447cf0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag &lt;-resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; --categoryID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="502cc4e7-3c14-41c1-a0bf-cdaf58b591d0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lb,--list-branch &lt;resourceID=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Lists all branches of the given resource.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lc,--list-cluster-ids</p></td><td>Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;resourceID=&lt;value&gt; branchID=&lt;value&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div ac:macro-id="f66c2956-ce83-46d5-8347-1cc052f3f753"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lcat,--list-categories</p></td><td>Lists all the categories and their IDs.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-li,--list-index</p></td><td>Lists all available data that have indexes.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lr,--list-resources</p></td><td>Lists all available resources.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td>Lists all resources that are marked as deleted.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lrv,--list-revision &lt;resourceID=value branchID=value&gt;</p></td><td>Lists all revisions in the given branch.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lu,--list-users &lt;--no-info&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7236b80d-5f13-4722-89a9-f1395659fa85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add <strong>--no-info</strong> to show only the usernames.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ram,--reset-administrator</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resets admin password, status, and permissions.</p><ul><li data-uuid="1c58e0a1-427d-4978-b697-6b1cb3ee1eed">If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.</li><li data-uuid="dc027045-4955-4cc4-abf4-f5cb005916fe">If the Administrator account exists in the database:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="eda0546a-d631-484f-989f-1ca95e4312d2"><ul><li>If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li>If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li></ul></li></ul><ul class="alternate"><li data-uuid="adce4c4b-0f36-4f5e-9fd1-e7575e6d8a0b">The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rc,--recreate-cluster-id</p></td><td><p>Recreates a cluster ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ri,--rebuild-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to rebuild all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-urd,--unmark-deleted-resources &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vb,--validate-blob &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL&gt;</p></td><td>Validates blob information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-ve,--validate-eobject &lt;resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the <strong>resourceID=*</strong> option to validate all resources.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-version</p></td><td>Displays the version of Data Manager.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-vi,--verify-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to verify all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vr,--validate-rbac &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-vrc,--validate-resource-commit &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information.</td></tr><tr><td>-col,--cleanup-object-locks</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f91ddea-a561-4aad-b051-6f88b57e6abf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>How to safely execute this command:</p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="c05597e9-d55e-486f-aaee-d6c9ead79757">Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.</li><li data-uuid="c66d34f8-03db-4a78-83e2-267607b7f7c4">After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool repair</strong> which you can find in the <em>Cassandra/bin</em> directory. Once <strong>nodetool repair </strong>is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li><li data-uuid="3bbd747d-4184-4a4a-a31c-1d6f5babeabf">DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool compact</strong>. After running the command in <strong>nodetool compact </strong>terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rcc,--rebuild-change-count &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e9397cb-2a34-4dc4-9979-ab9aa5e39b0d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40487135-04e4-41b7-8e01-40229da20b94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.</p><p>Example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8e66b4dd-700c-4c8e-9730-b36ed7912f70">datamanager -rcc</li><li data-uuid="53c5dd82-583c-4218-9ba8-47f2b4477e7b">datamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637</li><li data-uuid="a2a69910-2ccc-459a-b5e2-e0a969c7f3d1"><span>datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-crb,--clean-resource-blobs &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional <strong>resourceID</strong> argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-cu,--change-username &lt;username=value newUsername=value&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Changes the given username and generates a new password.<br />                                                                                                             </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85862fb-7945-425b-a8d6-70450e7e40d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, &quot;, around the username. Example:</p><p>datamanager -cu username=&quot;John Old&quot; newUsername=&quot;Andrew New&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ll,--list-ldap</p></td><td><p>Lists all LDAP realm information.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rlu, --remap-ldap-users</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d17ead5-c338-4444-8707-2824840f8d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vlu,--validate-ldap-users &lt;saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-udb,--unmark-deleted-branches &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The <strong>delete</strong> command to delete resources and users works as follows.</p><h4>Deleting selected resource </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe1732a-965d-4141-8318-eb14b852a461"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> <resource ID> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the resource. </li><li>Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories). </li><li>Branch information. </li><li>Commit information. </li><li>Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed). </li><li>Locking information.</li></ul><h4>Deleting selected user </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89fa469-6680-4099-8b73-5b9199426522"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-du,--delete-user <username>,<username> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).</li><li>Session (the existing user session will be removed).</li><li>Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).</li><li>User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdb4f757-4a39-4a2e-b0bc-bd228aefce15"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes on deleting usernames</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For usernames consisting of two words (such as <em>dan smith</em>), add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username, as shown:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac17643d-503e-4234-bc3c-db295661ad2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the modeling tool's history panels, the word <em>deleted</em> will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deleting selected branch </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a7bd5f-fc36-4868-bcb6-59a08022d27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch</li><li>Commit information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Locking information of each object locked in the branch</li><li>Branch information of the branch</li><li>If the branch has child branches, depending on the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong><strong> </strong>value, the branch will be deleted or not: <em> </em><br /><ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <em>False </em>and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch </strong>value is set to <em>True, </em>then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Deleting revisions </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7deaa30e-49cc-4711-9f3b-1d2227ceb92f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the revision</li><li>Object information created/modified at the revision</li><li>Commit information of the revision</li><li>Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to</li><li>Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch</li><li>If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong><strong> </strong>value, the revision will be deleted or not: <ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <em>False, </em>and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision </strong>value is set to <em>T</em><em>rue,</em> then<em> </em>revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063113 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00377: (2026x) Data markings support

- page_id: `247063113`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063113/2026x+Data+markings+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:24:35.016+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMPTWRT'] to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects.The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classificationor sensitivity. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.

For information on how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space=''].

The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.

The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.

Data markings are supported in tables as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings displayed in a table banner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMPTWRT" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ac:link> to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects. </span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">or sensitivity</span>. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81888686-7ea9-4d43-8e16-47dacf2e4a5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_element_names.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_diagrams.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.</h6><p>Data markings are supported in tables as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_tables.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings displayed in a table banner.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063868 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00378: (2026x) Deleting a comment

- page_id: `247063868`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063868/2026x+Deleting+a+comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:45.075+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Working with comments > (2026x) Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.

To delete a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the bottom of the comment and select Delete .

#### TIP: Useful to know

Useful to know

- Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.
- You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="541e0626-073b-4cd7-8106-6c818482f294"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful to know</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.</li><li>You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

To delete a comment

1. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Comments pane' space=''], find the comment you want to delete.
