<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063623 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00380: (2026x) Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center

- page_id: `247063623`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063623/2026x+Deploying+Cassandra+Multi-Data+Center
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T10:37:25.385+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Installation > (2026x) Advanced installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc2022x Refresh2 and later versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcusers. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example.

Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.

##### Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

###### Live backup/disaster recovery

Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwccan also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.

In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation.

###### ****Geographical location

For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center. All of the data centers would form one Cassandra cluster, and all of them would have access to the latest data.

In this scenario, performance would mainly be affected by the database write operations. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.

##### Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for the product

This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc.

To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

1. 
2. Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.
3. 
4. 
5. For an existing database, .
6. Start falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcservices and verify operation.

##### Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers

Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. Each Cassandra data center has three nodes. When performing the initial installation and configuration of Cassandra nodes, make sure the version of Cassandra is consistent across all nodes. Also, make sure to synchronize clocks across all nodes.

Start by updating the *cassandra.yaml* configuration file.

Set a unique cluster name for all nodes. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.

```text

```

Set the seed provider for all data centers. Assign at least two seed nodes for each data center. Specify the address for all seeds. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.

```text

```

Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is *SimpleSnitch*, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file when *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* is set.

```text

```

Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: [https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html)

Edit the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.

For the primary data center:

```text

```

For the backup data center:

```text

```

Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run *nodetool status* to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.

For an existing Cassandra cluster, run *nodetool repair* and *nodetool cleanup* after the above configurations have been made.

###### Configuring the product for Multi-DC

Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the *application.conf* file for Multi-DC.

In the *esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace* section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL" for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).

```text

```

- When *is-multi-dc* is set to *true*, the *replication-factor* parameter is ignored.
- The *dc-replication-factors* configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcwill use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use *ALTER KEYSPACE* to change the replication factor.

| Keyspace Consistency Setting | Write Consistency Level | Read Consistency Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL | ALL | ONE |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL | ALL | LOCAL_ONE |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | EACH_QUORUM |
| FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL | LOCAL_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |

In the *esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic* section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.

```text

```

Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in *cassandra.yaml*

```text

```

###### Configuring Cassandra keyspace

If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.

Use *cqlsh*, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.

```bash

```

Run *nodetool repair* after keyspace modification to update all nodes.

To verify replication in *cqlsh*:

```bash

```

These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcwill create the keyspace during initial startup.

```bash

```

##### Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices

- The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers
- At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center
- Clocks should be synchronized for all node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86345e6e-735a-447d-b4e8-d9fdba67a802"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="862e922d-5b21-483c-8de6-99c9179daeea"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>2022x Refresh2 and later versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a58336ea-3ccd-489c-8230-11d0a97055c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdacbbe9-5f04-45a5-9db9-ff735843fb45"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>users. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster</span></h3><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Live backup/disaster recovery</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24beefff-6554-43ce-8f63-774ba9105016"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="048db3eb-258a-451d-99d8-a35777751882"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>can also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation. </span></p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong> </strong>Geographical location</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center. All of the data centers would form one Cassandra cluster, and all of them would have access to the latest data. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In this scenario, performance would mainly be affected by the database write operations. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for the product</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59f5eded-17c1-4e8e-9feb-9d6c39e38b39"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a042008-40b8-424a-9799-f5b12a05d3df"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure Cassandra cluster with more than one data center.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li>Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.</li><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring the product for Multi-DC"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure the product for Multi-DC.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring authentication in Web Application Platform for Multi-DC"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure authentication in Web Application Platform for Multi-DC.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For an existing database</span>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Cassandra keyspace"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the “twc” keyspace for Multi-DC setup]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Start <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c4035ce-62fb-49b9-8fa6-e6d806420835"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adcfccf4-2a0b-4d7e-89af-1ea2614b3b4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>services and verify operation.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. Each Cassandra data center has three nodes. When performing the initial installation and configuration of Cassandra nodes, make sure the version of Cassandra is consistent across all nodes. Also, make sure to synchronize clocks across all nodes.  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Start by updating the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> configuration file. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Set a unique cluster name for all nodes. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e767c7-e04a-4920-9421-eb5ba65a2644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cluster_name: 'MDC Cluster']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Set the seed provider for all data centers. Assign at least two seed nodes for each data center. Specify the address for all seeds. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f21423e-fe82-4b53-b0a4-2354e7fe0f05"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seed_provider:
    - class_name: org.apache.cassandra.locator.SimpleSeedProvider
      parameters:
          - seeds: "10.0.0.1, 10.0.0.2, 10.0.1.1, 10.0.1.2"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is <em>SimpleSnitch</em>, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using <em>GossipingPropertyFileSnitch</em> for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the <em>cassandra-rackdc.properties</em> file when <em>GossipingPropertyFileSnitch</em> is set. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc269aa0-2fed-430b-a612-84d77f41ce2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[endpoint_snitch: GossipingPropertyFileSnitch]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: <a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html">https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html</a></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Edit the <em>cassandra-rackdc.properties</em> file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For the primary data center:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f9ec96e-2868-4b44-99ed-f47bc4134781"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dc=datacenter1
rack=rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For the backup data center:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec0d8678-6728-4ff6-99b5-3e3aa70a6088"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dc=datacenter2
rack=rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run <em>nodetool status</em> to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For an existing Cassandra cluster, run <em>nodetool repair</em> and <em>nodetool cleanup</em> after the above configurations have been made.</span></p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuring the product for Multi-DC</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the <em>application.conf</em> file for Multi-DC. </span></p><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the <em>esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace</em> section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL&quot; for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c8b0f69-b6b7-4080-b030-dccd9259860e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[is-multi-dc = true
strategy-class = "NetworkTopologyStrategy"
consistency = "FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL"
dc-replication-factors {
     datacenter1=3
     datacenter2=3
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">When <em>is-multi-dc</em> is set to <em>true</em>, the <em>replication-factor</em> parameter is ignored. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The <em>dc-replication-factors</em> configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a415054b-af0e-4dd1-bd02-50d1fce1f6a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac581463-9e77-4f02-91c9-5c097c98c061"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>will use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use <em>ALTER KEYSPACE</em> to change the replication factor.</span></li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Keyspace Consistency Setting</span></p></th><th><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Write Consistency Level</span></p></th><th><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Read Consistency Level</span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ALL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ONE</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ALL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">LOCAL_ONE</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the <em>esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic</em> section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5434990-4ea4-4c49-91c3-f17b93684643"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[load-balancing-policy.local-datacenter = datacenter1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in <em>cassandra.yaml</em></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="882ea537-923b-4bdf-86fd-cb7e8f683dee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[contact-points = ["10.0.0.1:9042","10.0.0.2:9042"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuring Cassandra keyspace</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use <em>cqlsh</em>, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e609f3d-bc25-49be-afb6-538060dec874"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALTER KEYSPACE twc WITH replication = {'class': 'NetworkTopologyStrategy', 'datacenter1' : 3, 'datacenter2' : 3};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Run <em>nodetool repair</em> after keyspace modification to update all nodes.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To verify replication in <em>cqlsh</em>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd6ba8d6-7cf0-4045-a8cd-b57634c6bb58"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[select * from system_schema.keyspaces;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c1c7f95-aa3b-4ef4-91d9-2e6f4112a205"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="232eedaf-df70-4ca2-bece-63420312c855"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>will create the keyspace during initial startup.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c7f50b5-4d1c-4b90-90f0-d972fed3e78a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[CREATE KEYSPACE twc WITH replication = {'class': 'NetworkTopologyStrategy', 'datacenter1' : 3, 'datacenter2' : 3}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices</span></h3><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Clocks should be synchronized for all node</span></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064537 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00381: (2026x) Deployment configuration

- page_id: `247064537`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064537/2026x+Deployment+configuration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-22T09:24:58.479+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The configuration settings described in this section are optional. You only need to configure them if the default setting is not suitable for your environment. The falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcsystem will load the configuration file every time it starts. Therefore, changes to the configuration file will be applied once you restart it.

The falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcservice configuration file (*application.conf)* is located in *<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf.*This file is in HOCON format ("Human-Optimized Config Object Notation"). For more information about HOCON file format, visit the following link [https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax](https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax).

To configure Cassandra authentication, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Hardening Cassandra' space=''].

##### Setting a password for authentication with the Authentication Server

Teamwork Cloud Admin uses an Authentication Server to authenticate users. Teamwork Cloud Admin needs a password to access /token endpoint of the Authentication Server. You need to specify the same password that is entered in the *webappplatform.properties* file ****(the **authentication.client.secret** parameter). The default value is **CHANGE_ME**.

```text

```

##### Changing a log file location

You can configure the logging system of falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcby using *logback.xml*.

To change the name or location of the log files in *logback.xml*, locate <file> fields. When changing the log file name and location, you need to be sure that the user account for your Operating System (OS) that you will use to start falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwchas the write permission in the newly configured location. To learn more about logback configuration, see [http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html](http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html).

##### Changing the client port

The default port number of falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcfor the client to connect to is **3579**. However, the port number is changeable. If you would like to change the default port number, for example, to 3580, you can configure this in the falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcconfiguration file, *application.conf.* Search for the content shown below, and edit as follows:

```text

```

Please note that the modeling tool, such as MagicDraw, is considered a client of falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc. Consequently, if you change this setting, you have to specify the port number while connecting from the modeling tool.

##### Setting a public IP

If falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcis deployed in the environment where the client will connect to the server via a public IP (NATed to a private IP), set the parameter **server-broadcast-host** to the public IP address and **host** to the private IP. Search for the content in *application.conf* and edit as follows:

```text

```

##### Configuring secure SSL communication between falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcand Cassandra

You can set up secure communication between falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcand Cassandra as described below.

To set up SSL communication between falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcand Cassandra

1. Configure application.conf :
  1. Locate and open the application.conf file.
  2. Search for the following section: /Certs/keystore.p12"
 keystoreType = ""
 keystorePassword = ""
 truststorePath = "/Certs/keystore.p12"
 truststoreType = ""
 truststorePassword = ""
 }
}]]>
  3. Configure the properties, as shown in the example above: 
**enabled** = *true***keystorePath** = *<install_root>/Certs**/keystore.p12***keystoreType** = *<keystore_type>***keystorePassword** = *<keystore_password>***truststorePath** = *<install_root>/Certs**/keystore.p12***truststoreType** = *<truststore_type>***truststorePassword** = *<truststore_password>*
  4. Save the changes.
2. Configure *cassandra.yaml*:
  1. Locate and open the *cassandra.yaml*file.
  2. Configure the following properties: 
**client_encryption_options**: 
enabled: *true*optional:*false* If*enabled* and *optional* are set to true, encrypted and unencrypted connections are handled. **keystore**: *<path_to_keystore>/keystore.p12***keystore_password**: *<keystore_password>***require_client_auth**: *false*
  3. If r equire_client_auth is set to true , set truststore and truststore_password : truststore : <path_to_truststore>/keystore.p12 truststore_password : <truststore_password> The **require_client_auth** setting is optional – it adds an extra layer of security by requiring the client to present its own certificate to the server (mutual TLS). However, this setting does not affect whether encryption is used for communication: if **require_client_auth**is set to *false*, the server does not require the client to present a certificate, but the communication is still encrypted as long as SSL/TLS is enabled.
  4. Use these advanced defaults: protocol : TLS algorithm : SunX509 store_type : PKCS12

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5636081d-0dc9-4394-bb64-6f48a6008088">The configuration settings</ac:inline-comment-marker> described in this section are optional. You only need to configure them if the default setting is not suitable for your environment. The <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df87ac48-e1a1-49f9-b36e-3cdbe51aee68"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03a824af-0e7a-4dab-bcb0-11899b275be2"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>system will load the configuration file every time it starts. Therefore, changes to the configuration file will be applied once you restart it.</p><p>The <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64e4d5f3-9dbb-428a-91db-3ffd133b371b"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92ff2c42-b253-465b-af5c-57fe2cd92c9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>service configuration file (<em>application.conf)</em> is located in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf. </em>This file is in HOCON format (&quot;Human-Optimized Config Object Notation&quot;). For more information about HOCON file format, visit the following link <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax">https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9b2b143-f82f-4f1d-a043-74b5ef04ca4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To configure Cassandra authentication, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Hardening Cassandra" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a password for authentication with the Authentication Server</h3><p>Teamwork Cloud Admin uses an Authentication Server to authenticate users. Teamwork Cloud Admin needs a password to access /token endpoint of the Authentication Server. You need to specify the same password that is entered in the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">webappplatform.properties</span></em> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e6ad671d-3f49-44ee-8255-860643be0576">file</ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong> </strong>(the <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong> parameter). The default value is <strong>CHANGE_ME</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ec852e3-c3af-4242-866d-b502f6a8761b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">authserver.properties</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.console {
....
#
 # The console client password parameter.
 client {
 pswd = "CHANGE_ME"
 }
...
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Changing a log file location</h3><p class="Standard">You can configure the logging system of <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fd7d634-4269-4612-bcc1-3ef192fcc09b"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b95dc49-aa79-45ee-8af6-57d16f14736a"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>by using <em>logback.xml</em>.</p><p class="Standard">To change the name or location of the log files in <em>logback.xml</em>, locate &lt;file&gt; fields. When changing the log file name and location, you need to be sure that the user account for your Operating System (OS) that you will use to start <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f47ede20-ee0e-4b01-a94e-93c219d4f702"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55ab3d43-798a-4eb1-b245-089bede7819f"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>has the write permission in the newly configured location. To learn more about logback configuration, see <a href="http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html">http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html</a>.</p><h3><span>Changing the client port</span></h3><p class="Standard">The default port number of <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e233c3eb-5f20-4307-bb67-5c3513f1d96a"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ce3a6c0-7455-4bb7-8f4f-d91ef2666dc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>for the client to connect to is <strong>3579</strong>. However, the port number is changeable. If you would like to change the default port number, for example, to 3580, you can configure this in the <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c995279-4f88-4e86-8c89-e9c2563ca95f"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="162925c3-44aa-4ad4-afae-3b60b6b38fc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>configuration file, <em>application.conf. </em> Search for the content shown below, and edit as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60d6afce-0f48-4698-92e2-f938c6735f91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net
	{
		connectors = [
			{
				# The ip address to bind to (for client connection).
				# The default value is bind to all IPs.
				host = "0.0.0.0"
				
				# Port for client application to connect to. The default value is 3579.
				# This is corresponding to the port specified when connecting from client such as MagicDraw. 
				port = 3580]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="Standard">Please note that the modeling tool, such as MagicDraw, is considered a client of <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="050300b1-d0b7-4a75-847e-3abd8ddb5e36"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0320717f-dfc9-409f-8bc3-0848182c7681"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>. Consequently, if you change this setting, you have to specify the port number while connecting from the modeling tool.</p><h3>Setting a public IP</h3><p>If <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a76c8dd7-3284-4e7e-85fa-38a593d6a52b"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b420250-7abd-4ef2-a201-69119648cce3"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>is deployed in the environment where the client will connect to the server via a public IP (NATed to a private IP), set the parameter <strong>server-broadcast-host</strong> to the public IP address and <strong>host</strong> to the private IP. Search for the content in <em>application.conf</em> and edit as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="651c7dc5-daf9-4c7d-aad4-53e077a72453"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net
	{
		connectors = [
			{
				# The ip address to bind to (for client connection).
				# The default value is bind to all IPs.
				host = "0.0.0.0"
				
				# Port for client application to connect to. The default value is 3579.
				# This is corresponding to the port specified when connecting from client such as MagicDraw. 
				port = 3579
				
				protocol = "raw"
			}
		]
		
		# To support deploying Teamwork Cloud in private network, but client connects from public network,
		# Specify IP address that will be used by client to connect to this Teamwork Cloud from public network.
		# This value will be default to esi.net.host if it is empty.
		# server-broadcast-host is not recommended to use.
		server-broadcast-host = "PUBLIC_IP_ADDRESS"
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Configuring secure SSL communication between <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43551333-1e35-4a74-8ded-40b72333fa65"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc75801f-6ba3-4c50-9a22-e9a8826b1517"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>and Cassandra</h3><p>You can set up secure communication between <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a5f0393-03d2-450e-b789-f355129c16b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89af5daf-3d2b-4e35-9288-61ea55a61bcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>and Cassandra as described below.</p><p>To set up SSL communication between <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f655e6ed-2f5b-4674-b430-35cd07cf41c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d360764-5e4c-4672-bdf6-8d9c6dc1ccac"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>and Cassandra</p><hr /><ol><li>Configure <em>application.conf</em>:<ol><li>Locate and open the <em>application.conf</em> file.</li><li><p>Search for the following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c0d712b-9728-492f-bf52-d223edbdda83"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Secure connection with SSL between Cassandra and Teamwork Cloud
esi.security {
    cassandra {
        enabled = true
    	keystorePath = "<install_root>/Certs/keystore.p12"
    	keystoreType = "<keystore_type>"
    	keystorePassword = "<keystore_password>"
    	truststorePath = "<install_root>/Certs/keystore.p12"
        truststoreType = "<truststore_type>"
        truststorePassword = "<truststore_password>"
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Configure the properties, as shown in the example above:<br /><strong>enabled</strong> = <em>true<br /></em><strong>keystorePath</strong> = <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/Certs</em><em>/keystore.p12<br /></em><strong>keystoreType</strong> = <em>&lt;keystore_type&gt;<br /></em><strong>keystorePassword</strong> = <em>&lt;keystore_password&gt;<br /></em><strong>truststorePath</strong> = <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/Certs</em><em>/keystore.p12<br /></em><strong>truststoreType</strong> = <em>&lt;truststore_type&gt;<br /></em><strong>truststorePassword</strong> = <em>&lt;truststore_password&gt;</em></p></li><li>Save the changes.</li></ol></li><li><p>Configure <em>cassandra.yaml</em>:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate and open the <em>cassandra.yaml </em>file.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Configure the following properties:<br /><strong>client_encryption_options</strong>:<br />enabled: <em>true<br /></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">optional: </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">false</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ae01f62-5e93-4391-b9cb-ad67953781e2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If<em> enabled</em> and <em>optional</em> are set to true, encrypted and unencrypted connections are handled.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>keystore</strong>: <em>&lt;path_to_keystore&gt;/keystore.p12<br /></em><strong>keystore_password</strong>: <em>&lt;keystore_password&gt;<br /></em><strong>require_client_auth</strong>: <em>false</em></p></li><li>If <strong>r</strong><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="05045098-50bf-4bf1-9332-ec56391ac38a">equire_client_auth</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong>is set to <em>true</em>, set <em>truststore</em> and <strong>truststore_password</strong>:<br /><strong>truststore</strong>: <em>&lt;path_to_truststore&gt;/keystore.p12<br /></em><strong>truststore_password</strong>: <em>&lt;truststore_password&gt;<br /></em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60ada203-675f-4cbe-8e5c-2f67bb634424"><ac:rich-text-body>The <strong>require_client_auth</strong> setting is optional – it adds an extra layer of security by requiring the client to present its own certificate to the server (mutual TLS). However, this setting does not affect whether encryption is used for communication: if <strong>require_client_auth </strong>is set to <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">false</span></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, the server does not require the client to present a certificate, but the communication is still encrypted as long as SSL/TLS is enabled.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Use these advanced defaults:<br /><strong>protocol</strong>: <em>TLS<br /></em><strong>algorithm</strong>: <em>SunX509<br /></em><strong>store_type</strong>: <em>PKCS12</em></li></ol></li></ol>
````
