<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064329 space=MCS version=13 -->
## PAGE 00382: (2026x) Deployment example on Kubernetes

- page_id: `247064329`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064329/2026x+Deployment+example+on+Kubernetes
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-06-12T12:33:22.913+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Enterprise deployment
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This deployment example is not intended for production use. Use it for testing purposes only.

This chapter provides information on how to deploy the product for testing purposes. Keep in mind that the guidelines provided below are not considered the best practices and do not take into account security settings and password encryption. This deployment has been tested with a specific Kubernetes version, CNI, container runtime, and OS. If you use other solutions, use them at your own risk.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- Kubernetes cluster (for more information, refer to https://kubernetes.io/ ):
  - 1 virtual machine used as the control plane node and 3 nodes used as worker nodes The names of worker nodes must match their FQDN.
  - Control plane node: 4 CPU cores, 8GB RAM
  - Worker node: 16 CPU cores, at least 64GB RAM (depends on your deployment size)
- Kubernetes version: v1.28 or later
- Container runtime: Containerd (for more information, refer to https://containerd.io/ )
- CNI: Calico
- Load Balancer: MetalLB (the configuration instructions are provided later in this chapter) Most cloud providers do not support MetalLB.
- IP: 1 additional IP address with the DNS A record that points to this IP (IP from your network that is not used by your cluster nodes).
- Ingress: ingress-nginx (with sticky session support). Note that there are two nginx ingresses: ingress-nginx is maintained by the Kubernetes community, while nginx-ingress is maintained by F5 inc. The free F5 inc. version of nginx-ingress does not support “sticky sessions” which are required. Therefore, make sure you use ingress-nginx.
- A Host with Docker or Podman to build images and push them to the image registry.
- An image registry (repository) such as Harbor, Sonatype Nexus, Gitlab, or Docker registry server to push and pull the images you will build.
- The applications needed to run Teamwork Cloud and services:
  - Zookeeper (for more information, refer to https://zookeeper.apache.org/ )
  - Apache Artemis MQ (for more information, refer to [https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/](https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/))
  - Keda CRD (deployed with the Simulation web application)
  - MetalLB
  - HAProxy Ingress
  - Cassandra

See the compatible versions of the applications in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page.

The following figure displays the environment used in this deployment example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In this environment, the K8S cluster admin host (Linux virtual machine) is used to build images, run the image repository, and manage the Kubernetes cluster.

**In the workflow below:**

- [admin.example.com](http://admin.example.com) refers to the K8S cluster admin host and should be changed to your K8S cluster admin FQDN.
- [ingress.example.com](http://ingress.example.com) refers to the domain name through which Web Application Platform will be accessible.
- All the steps are performed on the K8S cluster admin host.
- All commands are prepared to work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.

To deploy the product for testing purposes

1. Install kubectl as described in https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/ .
2. Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install .
3. Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host: bashRDark:.kube ~
]]> Then open to edit *~/.kube/config* and replace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname, e.g., *[https://127.0.0.1:6443](https://127.0.0.1:6443)*.
4. Prepare the required files and directories:
  1. Download and extract the [ATTACHMENT filename='twc-services-charts-2026xHF1.zip'] file.
  2. Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to Downloading installation files .
  3. Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/ twc-services-charts directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts .zip file), extract the **twcloud_<version>_no_install_linux64.zip** file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts /CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/ . You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in *twcloud_<version>_no_install_linux64.zip* or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.
5. Go to the the ~/twc-services-charts directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers. bashRDark
6. Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing SSL certificate' space=''] . bashRDark The *tls.crt* and *tls.key* files will be used for ingress-nginx, so file names in ingress-nginx must be the same.
7. Execute the following commands to prepare the *helm-charts root* directory: bashRDark
8. Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands: bashRDark
9. To get Cassandra files, go to [https://archive.apache.org/dist/cassandra/5.0.5/apache-cassandra-5.0.5-bin.tar.gz](https://archive.apache.org/dist/cassandra/5.0.5/apache-cassandra-5.0.5-bin.tar.gz) (tested with 5.0.5 version) and download the Apatche Cassandra to the twc-services-charts directory.
10. Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra *.tar* file: bashRDark
11. In the *cassandra.yaml* file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.
12. Execute the following command to copy files to the *twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra* directory: bashRDark
13. Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location: bashRDark
14. Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location: bashRDark
15. Edit the application.conf file as shown below.
16. Execute the following commands to edit the jvm.options file: bashRDark
17. Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location: bashRDark
18. Copy all files listed below into their directories:
19. auth*authserver.properties*cassandra*cassandra.yaml**logback.xml*ssl*tls.crt**tls.key*tomcat*catalina.properties**server.xml*twcloud*application.conf**logback.xml**jvm.options*twcloud/ssl*keystore.p12**teamworkcloud.crt*web*revision.txt**log4j2.properties**webappplatform.properties*
20. Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going to use: bashRDark
21. Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the artemis directory). admin-console*admin.war**Dockerfile**ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)authentication*authentication.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)collaborator*collaborator.war**Dockerfile**data*(The directory is located in~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices*/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/*. Before copying, remove the*document-exporter*directory from the*data*directory.)*ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)document-exporter*document-exporter.war**Dockerfile**data* (This directory is located in *<webapp-charts_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.*Remove the *collaborator* directory from the *data* directory.)*ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)oslc*oslc.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)reports*reports.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)resource-usage-map*resource-usage-map.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)resources*resources.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)sgi-crawler*sgi-crawler.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)simulation*simulation.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)sysmlv2-api*sysmlv2-api.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)webapp*webapp.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)twcloud*Dockerfile**TeamworkCloud* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices*.)magiclab*magiclab.war**Dockerfile**ROOT* (The directory is located in *~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)
22. Execute the following command to start the local Docker registry: bashRDark In this case, a local Docker registry is run on the K8S cluster admin host. It uses port 5000, so make sure this port is open on your host firewall and accessible to your Kubernetes cluster. This local Docker registry should be used for testing purposes only and is not considered production-ready. In the production environment, run the image registry with TLS and authentication.
23. Add your repo URL (FQDN of the K8S cluster admin host) to the Docker /etc/docker/daemon.json file.
24. If the *daemon.json*file does not exist, create it. Assuming there are no other settings in the file, it should have the following content:
25. Execute the following command to restart the Docker service: bashRDark
26. Execute the commands below for each service that you want to build images for inside their own directories, as shown in the examples in substeps a, b, and c. bashRDark {IMAGE_REPO_URL} - your image repository URL. It can differ depending on the image registry provider.{APP_NAME} - application name.{VERSION} - version to be used for the tag.
  1. The following command example builds images for Admin Console: bashRDark
  2. The following command example tags the built image: bashRDark
  3. The following command example pushes the image to the registry: bashRDark Check the names of the local repositories after Docker images are pushed. 
curl -s [http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog) 
curl -s [http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2/<repository_name>/tags/list](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list) 
Example: 
curl -s [http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list) 
REGISTRY_URL="[http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2)"; curl -s $REGISTRY_URL/_catalog | jq -r '.repositories[]' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'echo "Repository: {}"; curl -s '"$REGISTRY_URL"'/{}/tags/list | jq -r ".tags[]"'
27. Execute the commands below for ActiveMQ Artemis (this is a working example): bashRDark
28. To be able to pull images to your Kubernetes cluster nodes, add your image repository to the Containerd configuration on all cluster nodes :
  1. Edit the */etc/containerd/config.toml* file by adding the following lines to the "mirrors" part: Keep in mind that alignment is very important, as shown in the example below. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Execute the following command to restart Containerd services on all cluster nodes: bashRDark
29. Execute the following commands to add dependency repos: bashRDark
30. Execute the following command to update chart dependencies: bashRDark
31. Execute the following command to install Custom Resource Definitions (CRD). This will create all services and deployments. bashRDark You can check the status of the services and pods with this command: kubectl get all -n keda Keda and MetalLB should be added as separate resources.Since CRDs (being a globally shared resource) are fragile, you have to assume that once a CRD is installed, it is shared across multiple namespaces and groups of users. For that reason, installing, modifying, and deleting CRDs is a process that has ramifications for all users and systems of that cluster.
32. Execute the following command to deploy MetalLB: bashRDark
33. To check the MetalLB deployment status, execute the following command on the control plane node or the K8S cluster admin host: bashRDark You should see an output similar to this: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you see that the MetalLB status is 'Running,' but READY is 0/1, give it some time to start and repeat the command.
34. Create configuration files for MetalLB as instructed below. Make sure to complete all the substeps of this step to create and apply both configurations. Otherwise, the product will not work properly.
  1. Your IT department should give you a reserved IP address or a range of IP addresses (depending on your needs) with a DNS A record that points to this IP address.At least one IP address with a domain name should be reserved for Ingress.Configuration files should be formatted in "*yaml".*The first IP address configured in *metallb_ipadresspool.yaml* will be assigned for Ingress.Edit the *~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/**metalLB/metallb_ipadresspool.yaml* file as shown below:
  2. Edit (if necessary) the *~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/**metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml* file as shown below. You can change the metadata name as needed. Keep in mind that the ipAddressPool name should match the specifications.
  3. Execute the following command to apply the MetalLB configuration: bashRDark
  4. Execute the following command to check the applied configuration: bashRDark
  5. Execute the following command to create the MetalLB advertisement: bashRDark
  6. Execute the following command to check the advertisement configuration: bashRDark
35. Install HAProxy CRD
  - bashRDark
36. Find the values.yaml file in the parent helm chart ( ~/twc-services-charts/helm-chart ) and provide the values of the parameters in the file to enable or disable specific applications or parts of the configuration.
37. Do one of the following:
  - To deploy services to the default namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory (you can change "twc" to any release name): bashRDark
  - To create a namespace and deploy services in this namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory : bashRDark This helm chart includes Zookeeper and HAProxy ingress as dependencies. They will be deployed automatically.
38. After all web applications are deployed, execute the following command to check their status: bashRDark All pods should be READY:1/1, STATUS:Running.The ingress-nginx service should be Type:LoadBalancer.EXTERNAL-IP should match the address that you provided in the MetalLB configuration.If pods do not run, check for problems by executing this command:kubectl describe pod pod_name
39. Execute the following command to check Ingress rules: bashRDark You should get an output similar to this: )
TLS:
 webapp-tls-secret terminates ingress.example.com
Rules:
 Host Path Backends
 ---- ---- --------
 ingress.example.com
 /admin webapp-adminconsole:8443 (10.233.105.39:8443)
 /authentication webapp-authentication:8443 (10.233.88.69:8443)
 /collaborator webapp-collaborator:8443 (10.233.88.75:8443)
 /document-exporter webapp-docexporter:8443 (10.233.105.22:8443)
 /oslc webapp-oslc:8443 (10.233.105.23:8443)
 /reports webapp-reports:8443 (10.233.73.246:8443)
 /resources webapp-resources:8443 (10.233.88.67:8443)
 /resource-usage-map webapp-rum:8443 (10.233.105.40:8443)
 /simulation webapp-simulation:8443 (10.233.88.123:8443)
 /webapp webapp-webapp:8443 (10.233.105.29:8443)
Annotations: meta.helm.sh/release-name: webapp
 meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: default
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity: cookie
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity-canary-behavior: sticky
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity-mode: persistent
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: https
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: 100m
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-connect-timeout: 600
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: 600
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-send-timeout: 600
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/send-timeout: 600
 nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/session-cookie-name: COOKIE
Events: ]]>
40. Test the product deployment:
  1. In an internet browser, go to *[https://ingress.example.com/webapp](http://ingress.example.com)* (the DNS A record that points to the Ingress external IP reserver earlier). If you added a DNS record to bind the domain name to the IP address, you can use the domain name instead of the IP. The browser should show a warning because of the self-signed certificate. Accept it to proceed, and you will be redirected to the Teamwork Cloud Authentication web page. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Log in with your credentials, and you will be redirected back to Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffbfdc68-df46-41ad-b222-3044a3aca6a3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This deployment example is not intended for production use. Use it for testing purposes only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This chapter provides information on how to deploy the product for testing purposes. Keep in mind that the guidelines provided below are not considered the best practices and do not take into account security settings and password encryption. This deployment has been tested with a specific Kubernetes version, CNI, container runtime, and OS. If you use other solutions, use them at your own risk.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25fec92e-70f2-40c3-94aa-0b5337dcef0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="b660babc-1f5a-4378-b803-8cd9ec478eaf">Kubernetes cluster (for more information, refer to <a href="https://kubernetes.io/">https://kubernetes.io/</a>):<ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">1 virtual machine used as the control plane node and 3 nodes used as worker nodes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1e39146-5348-48d9-ae75-6c2cd9bea3e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The names of worker nodes must match their FQDN.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Control plane node: 4 CPU cores, 8GB RAM</li><li>Worker node: 16 CPU cores, at least 64GB RAM (depends on your deployment size)</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="ada10c5b-f545-4976-bd79-aedde615ad72">Kubernetes version: v1.28 or later</li><li data-uuid="b9a8f8b5-5aa5-4bcd-a7ce-64dce1a81874">Container runtime: Containerd (for more information, refer to <a href="https://containerd.io/">https://containerd.io/</a>)</li><li data-uuid="543e33bb-6a19-40b5-ab9f-75a5842df0ea">CNI: Calico</li><li data-uuid="1fd53b04-7a9f-4de3-b1b1-ed6b0719d781">Load Balancer: MetalLB (the configuration instructions are provided later in this chapter)<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58c35ffc-4f92-4b1a-b221-3379b5ea2473"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Most cloud providers do not support MetalLB.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a7850be0-a869-4544-9880-bda2d74c9e5b">IP: 1 additional IP address with the DNS A record that points to this IP (IP from your network that is not used by your cluster nodes).</li><li data-uuid="3eb1e19b-a246-456d-a06c-2abebb9240fc">Ingress: ingress-nginx (with sticky session support).<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b1109ce-5a64-41c4-a347-fd843dd27752"><ac:rich-text-body>Note that there are two nginx ingresses: ingress-nginx is maintained by the Kubernetes community, while nginx-ingress is maintained by F5 inc. The free F5 inc. version of nginx-ingress does not support “sticky sessions” which are required. Therefore, make sure you use ingress-nginx.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="daefefcc-2b9a-4904-bb2d-bd298ba9509e">A Host with Docker or Podman to build images and push them to the image registry.</li><li data-uuid="2d88b54d-c36b-4548-a781-d90298f9397f">An image registry (repository) such as Harbor, Sonatype Nexus, Gitlab, or Docker registry server to push and pull the images you will build.</li><li data-uuid="33831613-e498-4821-b5c4-afb65380e3c9">The applications needed to run Teamwork Cloud and services:<ul><li>Zookeeper (for more information, refer to <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/">https://zookeeper.apache.org/</a>)</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Artemis MQ (for more information, refer to <a href="https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/">https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/</a>)</span></li><li>Keda CRD (deployed with the Simulation web application)</li><li>MetalLB</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">HAProxy Ingress</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cassandra</span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the compatible versions of the applications in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Third-Party Component Compatibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Third-Party Component Compatibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure displays the environment used in this deployment example.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> In this environment, the K8S cluster admin host (Linux virtual machine) is used to build images, run the image repository, and manage the Kubernetes cluster.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c011d33-341d-48fd-9c80-eb4eb5be3136"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In the workflow below:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="e28ea280-5ded-4a3b-bfde-de289d4ef92a"><em><a href="http://admin.example.com">admin.example.com</a></em> refers to the K8S cluster admin host and should be changed to your K8S cluster admin FQDN.</li><li data-uuid="0869bf56-473a-4310-9b83-5f769a0afc53"><em><a href="http://ingress.example.com">ingress.example.com</a></em> refers to the domain name through which Web Application Platform will be accessible.</li><li data-uuid="cc1ddea4-6e46-4530-a68e-fa30ba6ec4c9">All the steps are performed on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">K8S cluster admin host.</span></li><li data-uuid="de1159f9-a185-45b2-be4e-da3aea980379"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All commands are prepared to work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To deploy the product for testing purposes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4dfe4b0c-17a0-4037-aa80-ef09b3c529a7">Install kubectl as described in <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/</a><span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="ab810c8f-2e22-4854-99e3-175386940fb1">Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in <a href="https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install">https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install</a>.</li><li data-uuid="f00302c1-8c19-4ee1-b15a-566198b979d0"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b334167-5031-4340-b10a-966f47f62f21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scp -r <user@control_plane_address>:.kube ~
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then open to edit <em>~/.kube/config</em> and r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">eplace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname</span>, e.g., <em><span class="nolink"><a href="https://127.0.0.1:6443">https://127.0.0.1:6443</a></span></em>.</p></li><li data-uuid="ad2b2aff-0104-40a5-a5e1-ad642b33cff0"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Prepare the required files and directories</span>:</p><ol><li><span data-ref="05ccbf6e-4a3e-40e8-982e-03ce766ef31f">Download</span> and extract the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc-services-charts-2026xHF1.zip" /></ac:link></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> file.</span></li><li>Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=2026x">Downloading installation files</a>.</li><li data-uuid="5e281fa7-bc4c-4959-a6d2-47f415aba2f7">Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/<em>twc-services-charts</em> directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts<em>.zip</em> file), extract the <em><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span></strong> </em>file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts<em>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/</em>.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80c54b1-810f-4f07-82b9-76a5067f5743"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span></em> or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):</p><ul><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="2255b36c-441d-4ba6-823b-146bc49e966d">Go to the the <em>~/twc-services-charts</em> directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3775817c-b921-47a3-a3a9-0e4c1f23f769"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl req -new -newkey rsa:2048 -days 365 -nodes -x509 -keyout tls.key -out tls.crt -subj "/C=LT/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/OU=Department/CN=example.com"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9fba11ec-f57a-452f-a311-4f1f793bf93b">Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing SSL certificate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing SSL certificate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402beacd-44ef-4ad2-85c7-8f16b5787703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl pkcs12 -export -name teamworkcloud -in tls.crt -inkey tls.key -out keystore.p12 -password pass:nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5a3d33e-6b1d-4ec9-ba61-c6ad8cdb22eb"><ac:rich-text-body>The <em>tls.crt</em> and <em>tls.key</em> files will be used for ingress-nginx, so file names in ingress-nginx <u>must be the same</u>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="eb6aa134-5503-4eec-86d4-1a08f1d8d73e"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to prepare the <em>helm-charts root</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdcc84d9-c996-4072-baed-f0c2b9e63d55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts
mkdir -p configs/{auth,cassandra,ssl,tomcat,twcloud,wap} configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="0768fa14-63df-40b9-9040-7c719471e42e">Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d7aa0b5-984e-4445-9699-a621f48cbe6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts
cp tls.crt tls.key ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/ssl
cp keystore.p12 ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/ssl
cp tls.crt ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/ssl/teamworkcloud.crt]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1e934e4c-23ea-4eeb-9042-300812d43022"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To get Cassandra files, go to <a href="https://archive.apache.org/dist/cassandra/5.0.5/apache-cassandra-5.0.5-bin.tar.gz">https://archive.apache.org/dist/cassandra/5.0.5/apache-cassandra-5.0.5-bin.tar.gz</a></span> (tested with 5.0.5 version) and download the Apatche Cassandra to the <em> twc-services-charts</em> directory.</li><li data-uuid="9c78bf21-feea-471a-8353-dfe1dd67850d"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra <em>.tar</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7796de9-c1cd-4999-ae3b-bbb65ac6673c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tar xzf apache-cassandra-5.0.5-bin.tar.gz
cd ~/twc-services-charts/apache-cassandra-5.0.5/conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="18341fb6-7a56-4384-81b7-dc89576abbc1"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a43330b-ca86-49dc-82ba-36ed595013ff"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sed -i "s/# commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^rpc_address:.*/rpc_address: 0.0.0.0/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_segment_size:.*/commitlog_segment_size: 192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/read_request_timeout:.*/read_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/range_request_timeout:.*/range_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^write_request_timeout:.*/write_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/cas_contention_timeout:.*/cas_contention_timeout: 1000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/truncate_request_timeout:.*/truncate_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/request_timeout:.*/request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_warn_threshold:.*/batch_size_warn_threshold: 3000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_fail_threshold:.*/batch_size_fail_threshold: 5000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|compaction_throughput:.*|compaction_throughput: 512MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/memtable_heap_space:.*/memtable_heap_space: 4092MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/key_cache_size:.*/key_cache_size: 200MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/# file_cache_size: 512MiB/file_cache_size: 3072MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/internode_compression: dc/internode_compression: all/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_reads: 32/concurrent_reads: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_writes: 32/concurrent_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_counter_writes: 32/concurrent_counter_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|# stream_throughput_outbound: 24MiB/s|stream_throughput_outbound: 4096MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/#concurrent_compactors: 1/concurrent_compactors: 1/g" ./cassandra.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="5829a3f8-1bf4-49a2-9857-ced8f683b3bf"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to copy files to the <em>twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5994d30-2dc0-4610-8d7d-818e011e3b0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp cassandra.yaml logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f14399cf-74dc-416c-abf1-34b1736c7357">Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0245011-1932-4d09-98bf-787e8d4d4700"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/auth]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="caebe065-06a5-4647-a47d-ef390e48b62e">Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98ae8211-47f6-49dc-958e-16b3ccb2cbc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/catalina.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
sed -i "s/..\/TeamworkCloud\/configuration\/keystore.p12/.\/shared\/conf\/keystore.p12/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat/server.xml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="47475b9c-0839-4d45-8e0e-4f0b7890faf7">Edit the <em>application.conf</em> file as shown below.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76aeb30b-9279-4d45-bef2-96f2f1aab230"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/contact-points.*/contact-points = [${?CASSANDRA_SEED0}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED1}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED2}]/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/local.size.*/local.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/remote.size.*/remote.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/replication-factor.*/replication-factor = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f4b20e7b-f797-48ec-9246-4da47fa507d1">Execute the following commands to edit the <em>jvm.options</em> file:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="256c7c0a-e45c-48fc-821b-71682f373d00"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/jvm.options ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/-Xmx.*/-Xmx${TWC_XMX}/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/jvm.options]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c9815a4e-2e00-4560-8c22-cc2dce2c26bc">Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f028a28-4081-40e7-8d19-4d51cb04560e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf
cp revision.txt log4j2.properties webappplatform.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/wap/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="68294dfd-1e9d-44fb-8a4c-988516e0378a">Copy all files listed below into their directories:</li><li data-uuid="c5e4294c-bcf3-499c-8907-0d61da3c800d"><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.2451%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 100.0%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>auth</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">authserver.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>cassandra</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>cassandra.yaml</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>tls.crt</em></li><li><em>tls.key</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>tomcat</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>catalina.properties</em></li><li><em>server.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>application.conf</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">jvm.options</span></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud/ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>keystore.p12</em></li><li><em>teamworkcloud.crt<br /></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>web</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>revision.txt</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">log4j2.properties</span></em></li><li><em>webappplatform.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li data-uuid="399f9437-620f-401b-913c-4061de4265ad"><p class="auto-cursor-target"> Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going to use:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0966ee6-a2d5-4092-a627-a525d225d026"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/dockerfiles ~/twc-services-charts/imagebuild
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="df4a4b6b-8deb-4dab-acc2-030ba35b105f">Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the <em>artemis</em> directory).<table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.6938%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 100.036%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>admin-console</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>admin.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>authentication</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>authentication.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>collaborator</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>collaborator.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em><span> </span>(The directory is located in<span> ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices</span><em>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/</em>. Before copying, remove the<span> </span><em>document-exporter</em><span> </span>directory from the<span> </span><em>data</em><span> </span>directory.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>document-exporter</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>document-exporter.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em> (This directory is located in <em>&lt;webapp-charts_root&gt;/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.</em><span> R</span>emove the <em>collaborator</em> directory from the <em>data</em> directory.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>oslc</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>oslc.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>reports</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>reports.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resource-usage-map</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resource-usage-map.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resources</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resources.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>sgi-crawler</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sgi-crawler.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>simulation</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>simulation.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>sysmlv2-api</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sysmlv2-api.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>webapp</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>webapp.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>TeamworkCloud</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em>CATIANoMagicServices</em>.)</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>magiclab</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em><span>magiclab.war</span></em></li><li><em><span>Dockerfile</span></em></li><li><span><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in <em>~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</em>.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li data-uuid="c2e14d28-c745-4390-a378-0960e817758d"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to start the local Docker registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f266a15a-0b1d-41f3-80af-f740267beedd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --restart=always --name registry registry:2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb7ad51-fe04-4e3b-b785-f54effb9b75e"><ac:rich-text-body>In this case, a local Docker registry is run on the K8S cluster admin host. It uses port 5000, so make sure this port is open on your host firewall and accessible to your Kubernetes cluster.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77d086f-90e9-4982-a467-c09761c2f549"><ac:rich-text-body>This local Docker registry should be used for testing purposes only and is not considered production-ready. In the production environment, run the image registry with TLS and authentication.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f1c936e0-a214-483e-a2f5-54dcd197eeb9"><span>Add your repo URL (FQDN of the K8S cluster admin host) to the Docker </span><em>/etc/docker/daemon.json</em> file.</li><li data-uuid="dddb16d0-54f7-4164-bee5-6a8141a6acb5"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the <em>daemon.json</em><span> file does not exist, create it. Assuming there are no other settings in the file, it should have the following content:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dc2674a-6227-49ca-bc4d-6fa51a349241"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
"insecure-registries": ["admin.example.com:5000"]
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e12990bb-81d4-476c-9803-0666e2906cf7"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart the Docker service:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52848786-752a-4d84-9862-cfa28007cc0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart docker]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e8cb65f8-704f-4893-a3df-e6a15188f64a"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the commands below for each service that you want to build images for inside their own directories, as shown in the examples in substeps a, b, and c.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b587e64-bffd-4103-8a02-5f6cc94e9d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker build -f Dockerfile -t {APP_NAME} .
docker tag {APP_NAME}:{VERSION} {IMAGE_REPO_URL}:5000/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}
docker push {IMAGE_REPO_URL}/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f0d92e3-39cb-42e8-8eda-3fdc849c7245"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>{IMAGE_REPO_URL} - your image repository URL. It can differ depending on the image registry provider.</li><li>{APP_NAME} - application name.</li><li>{VERSION} - version to be used for the tag.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example builds images for Admin Console:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c34d30a-7506-46c6-9f90-f9000947b158"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd imagebuild/admin-console
docker build -f Dockerfile -t admin .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example tags the built image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1fe9603-6d3f-4e34-b2a9-d1281cb1be35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker tag admin: latest admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example pushes the image to the registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66dec9b0-a53c-4c0b-9f7d-1a6980426eed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker push admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93a3a193-6652-4358-865c-b0a4155bfe2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Check the names of the local repositories after Docker images are pushed.<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog</a><br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/&lt;repository_name&gt;/tags/list</a><br />Example:<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list</a><br />REGISTRY_URL=&quot;<a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">:5000</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">:5000/v2</a>&quot;; curl -s $REGISTRY_URL/_catalog | jq -r '.repositories[]' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'echo &quot;Repository: {}&quot;; curl -s '&quot;$REGISTRY_URL&quot;'/{}/tags/list | jq -r &quot;.tags[]&quot;'</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f4c771fe-7813-461f-a455-56574d5005e8">Execute the commands below for ActiveMQ Artemis (this is a working example):<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5c560b4-2dc9-4002-9dbf-218979dbfd52"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker pull apache/activemq-artemis:2.32.0
docker tag apache/activemq-artemis:2.32.0 admin.example.com:5000/artemis
docker push admin.example.com:5000/artemis]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="45a2ff4e-18f5-452c-85b9-8caffaa3ffa0">To be able to pull images to your Kubernetes cluster nodes, add your image repository to the Containerd configuration on <strong>all cluster nodes</strong><span>:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit the <em>/etc/containerd/config.toml</em> file by adding the following lines to the &quot;mirrors&quot; part:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce222a88-8173-4138-a552-f7b758ba4225"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.mirrors."admin.exmple.com:5000"]
              endpoint = ["http://admin.example.com:5000"]

      [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com"]
        [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com".tls]
         insecure_skip_verify = true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63a4c74-d89c-4c9d-aac0-6498bdbdfcf9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind that alignment is very important, as shown in the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containerd.PNG" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart Containerd services on all cluster nodes:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30f7224c-5052-4ee9-896d-213ce765affe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart containerd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6194e9db-b4b0-43b1-bf32-5059771124e4"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to add dependency repos:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7b39c59-1b71-49fe-8e78-0655d76946c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm repo add kedacore https://kedacore.github.io/charts
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo add haproxy-ingress https://haproxytech.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a27a6233-cef1-4374-8e66-dd821daf5fb7"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to update chart dependencies:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ed3f41d-dabc-4c86-bdca-efac821fbe0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm dependency update
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="cef31344-04ec-41c4-bbcc-18b046232142"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to install Custom Resource Definitions (CRD). This will create all services and deployments.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab86223e-8aa4-4a3e-a192-1172d374a5fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install keda kedacore/keda --namespace keda --create-namespace --wait --version 2.13.2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1248d479-52cf-44e6-b483-b46e996e0a85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can check the status of the services and pods with this command: kubectl get all -n keda</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8087bbb5-468f-4888-870e-19da350c9cc1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keda and MetalLB should be added as separate resources.</p><p>Since CRDs (being a globally shared resource) are fragile, you have to assume that once a CRD is installed, it is shared across multiple namespaces and groups of users. For that reason, installing, modifying, and deleting CRDs is a process that has ramifications for all users and systems of that cluster.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="8f08c6d4-f16c-45d9-a707-595cc93bb009"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to deploy MetalLB:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd0038ee-6ffe-435d-a203-f7fe63214a71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install metallb bitnami/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace --wait]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="fc8e930f-bcae-4e3e-abd8-89b4facdf237"><p class="auto-cursor-target">To check the MetalLB deployment status, execute the following command on the control plane node or the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a94001-25fe-4080-8187-33a0a6a32644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get -n metallb-system all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">You should see an output similar to this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="output_example.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="038c7120-7519-4732-b67a-6b9c18d2126f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you see that the MetalLB status is 'Running,' but READY is 0/1, give it some time to start and repeat the command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="272d7f46-445e-4b26-b08a-22bf323cf70e"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create configuration files for MetalLB as instructed below. Make sure to complete all the substeps of this step to create and apply both configurations. Otherwise, the product will not work properly.</p><ol><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37369021-132b-4d31-9583-ed17e9327989"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Your IT department should give you a reserved IP address or a range of IP addresses (depending on your needs) with a DNS A record that points to this IP address.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">At least one IP address with a domain name should be reserved for Ingress.</span></li><li>Configuration files should be formatted in &quot;<em>yaml&quot;.</em></li><li>The first IP address configured in <em>metallb_ipadresspool.yaml</em> will be assigned for Ingress.<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Edit the <em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/metallb_ipadresspool.yaml</em> file as shown below:</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1e65202-5d7c-40f3-b00a-53180ac65e38"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: first-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.1/32
  #- 192.168.10.0/24
  #- 192.168.9.1-192.168.9.5
  #- fc00:f853:0ccd:e799::/124]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit (if necessary) the <span><em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml</em> file as shown below. You can change the metadata name as needed. Keep in mind that the ipAddressPool name should match the specifications.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71524bf8-eed4-4074-a4e0-8f79fd8c3ede"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - first-pool]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to apply the MetalLB configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf06588-d304-4971-90b4-cf688c8d7bc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the applied configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8aa8955-bd2a-4fbd-81b4-0475ee7e6e89"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ipaddresspools.metallb.io first-pool -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to create the MetalLB advertisement:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83c1efea-26d2-49cc-8676-00f5981124ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the advertisement configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bdb3e7-5488-4a6d-ad63-49d215cdfc32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe l2advertisements.metallb.io example -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6166d81c-15b0-49aa-8723-a0eaa2075c4c">Install HAProxy CRD<ul><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1772cf7f-895a-4ef3-8634-d719ed1a5481"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haproxytech/kubernetes-ingress/v3.1.0/crs/definition/ingress.v1.haproxy.org_tcps.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="9170eadd-c62f-4137-836a-459126ea2c2d">Find the <em>values.yaml</em> file in the parent helm chart (<em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-chart</em>) and provide the values of the parameters in the file to enable or disable specific applications or parts of the configuration.</li><li data-uuid="f05e51b1-502e-4c1e-a96e-caf5b4122e76"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To deploy services to the default namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory (you can change &quot;twc&quot; to any release name):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09ac308a-be18-47b1-a3cd-e22440970304"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install twc .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a namespace and deploy services in this namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory :<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e8390c7-bc7c-4a31-97f7-759f36729c32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install twc . --namespace=twc --create-namespace --wait]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de256884-47cb-4ca6-9294-ef6b56ea5e45"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This helm chart includes Zookeeper and HAProxy ingress as dependencies. They will be deployed automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="2b8e790e-cd58-49a3-9d8f-350525145c26"><p class="auto-cursor-target">After all web applications are deployed, execute the following command to check their status:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="443b763f-e98f-4938-8800-9ede02d74821"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6792b1-83f5-4892-bb7e-c37c75f84abf"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All pods should be READY:1/1, STATUS:Running.</li><li>The ingress-nginx service should be Type:LoadBalancer.</li><li>EXTERNAL-IP should match the address that you provided in the MetalLB configuration.</li><li>If pods do not run, check for problems by executing this command:</li></ul><pre>		kubectl describe pod pod_name</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="30096205-5d04-43a4-8762-41195f5107ed"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check Ingress rules:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="993736c2-6794-4221-b041-237deb3159d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ingress]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You should get an output similar to this:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07c5cbd4-0fa8-4b89-b05a-446de8bc859e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name:             ingress.resource
Namespace:        default
Address:          **.**.***.***
Default backend:  default-http-backend:80 (<error: endpoints "default-http-backend" not found>)
TLS:
  webapp-tls-secret terminates ingress.example.com
Rules:
  Host                       Path  Backends
  ----                       ----  --------
  ingress.example.com
                             /admin                webapp-adminconsole:8443 (10.233.105.39:8443)
                             /authentication       webapp-authentication:8443 (10.233.88.69:8443)
                             /collaborator         webapp-collaborator:8443 (10.233.88.75:8443)
                             /document-exporter    webapp-docexporter:8443 (10.233.105.22:8443)
                             /oslc                 webapp-oslc:8443 (10.233.105.23:8443)
                             /reports              webapp-reports:8443 (10.233.73.246:8443)
                             /resources            webapp-resources:8443 (10.233.88.67:8443)
                             /resource-usage-map   webapp-rum:8443 (10.233.105.40:8443)
                             /simulation           webapp-simulation:8443 (10.233.88.123:8443)
                             /webapp               webapp-webapp:8443 (10.233.105.29:8443)
Annotations:                 meta.helm.sh/release-name: webapp
                             meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: default
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity: cookie
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity-canary-behavior: sticky
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/affinity-mode: persistent
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: https
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: 100m
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-connect-timeout: 600
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: 600
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-send-timeout: 600
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/send-timeout: 600
                             nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/session-cookie-name: COOKIE
Events:                      <none>

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="8ad7210a-0c08-4611-b059-00632c78d0bc"><span>Test the product deployment:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In an internet browser, go to <em><a href="http://ingress.example.com">https://ingress.example.com/webapp</a></em> (the DNS A record that points to the Ingress external IP reserver earlier).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e86a164e-e4a0-4dd2-aee3-dde0200f624a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you added a DNS record to bind the domain name to the IP address, you can use the domain name instead of the IP. The browser should show a warning because of the self-signed certificate. Accept it to proceed, and you will be redirected to the Teamwork Cloud Authentication web page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="web_app_platform_welcome_screen.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Log in with your credentials, and you will be redirected back to Web Application Platform.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063124 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00383: (2026x) Deployment Guide

- page_id: `247063124`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063124/2026x+Deployment+Guide
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:24:35.354+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The deployment guide explains how to install and configure falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcand related services. To learn more, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The deployment guide explains how to install and configure <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dbb52e2-9183-4496-ba59-6b658e679fb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04c61a6b-b0aa-43c5-9657-0e46e8296721"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>and related services. To learn more, refer to the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````
