<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064640 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00403: (2026x) Enabling the Single Tree View

- page_id: `247064640`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064640/2026x+Enabling+the+Single+Tree+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:28.068+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Publishing documents > (2026x) Document templates > (2026x) Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views.

To enable the Single Tree View

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.
3. In the open Specification window, set the Single Tree View property as true .
4. Click the Close button.
5. Save the template file.

When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views. </p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Single Tree View</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.</li><li>In the open Specification window, set the <strong>Single Tree View</strong> property as <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p><br />When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063820 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00404: (2026x) Encrypting property values

- page_id: `247063820`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063820/2026x+Encrypting+property+values
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-11-28T11:19:50.258+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration > (2026x) Hardening the deployment and its services
- labels: ['encrypt-properties', 'property-encryption', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Generating keys for encryption and decryption

By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the *encryptor.jar* file and the steps below explain how to use it.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The encryption tool uses Java 21. Therefore, the system **PATH** variable needs to point to the location of the *bin*folder of the Java 21 home directory, for example, *C:\Java\Java11\bin* or */etc/alternatives/jre_21*.

#### NOTE: Note on special characters

Note on special characters

Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs the command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, socheck your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.

The following examples use the property value **!&**:

- Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell: texttrue

- Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell: texttrue

To encrypt properties

1. Click the link to download the [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor-2026x.jar'] [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor-2026x.jar'] file.
2. Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the encryptor.jar file is located.
3. Run the java -jar encryptor.jar command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor_open.png']
4. Run the **java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys <path_to_folder_for_keys>** command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. Make sure to replace the**<path_to_folder_for_keys>**placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys.You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated. Keys for encryption and decryptionAfter executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:**propertiesEncryptionKey.pub** - a public key used to encrypt property values.**propertiesEncryptionKey** - a private key used to decrypt property values.
5. To encrypt a property, run the java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt <unencrypted_property_value> <path_to_the public_key> command. Make sure to replace the <unencrypted_property_value> and <path_to_the public_key> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the propertiesEncryptionKey.pub file. The tool will output the encrypted property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.png']

##### Configuring falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcencrypted property values

To configure falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcencrypted property values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ directory and edit the application.conf file.
2. In the *application.conf*file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one:**password=ENC("lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==")**.
3. In the same *application.conf* file, add the **esi.config.decrypt_key_file******property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **esi.config.decrypt_key_file=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/**TeamworkCloud/keys/**propertiesEncryptionKey***. Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in a location that falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwccan access.
4. Restart Teamwork Cloud service.

##### Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values

To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwcadministrator password should look similar to this one:**twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)**.
3. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory and open the catalina.properties file.
4. In the *catalina.properties* file, add the **properties.encryption.private.key.location** property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **properties.encryption.private.key.location=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp**Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey****.* Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.
5. Restart Web Application Platform service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Generating keys for encryption and decryption</h3><p>By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the <em>encryptor.jar</em> file and the steps below explain how to use it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3117ade9-4860-4557-acb9-5fc322b36a4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The encryption tool uses Java 21. Therefore, the system <strong>PATH</strong> variable needs to point to the location of the <em>bin</em><span> folder</span> of the Java 21 home directory, for example, <em>C:\Java\Java11\bin</em> or <em>/etc/alternatives/jre_21</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="372ac1bb-9c88-40b4-a084-4aac35ea6e3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note on special characters</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs th</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">e command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, so </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">check your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;">The following examples use the property value <strong>!&amp;</strong>:</p><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="a52e8f01-717d-47b1-859a-426a6291c455">Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d4517de-776f-4b58-bf41-19396423734d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt '!&']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="3e769774-5a4e-46b2-ae71-89c21ba3c2f4">Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1836d100-6e47-47e3-a552-4d56d5e37d25"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt "!&"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To encrypt properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the link to download the<ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor-2026x.jar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor-2026x.jar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[encryptor.jar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> file.</li><li>Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the <em>encryptor.jar</em> file is located.</li><li>Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar</strong> command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor_open.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys &lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong> command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. M<span>ake sure to replace the </span><strong>&lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong><span> placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys. </span>You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="876d7480-836d-49fb-8183-eefd51c7a612"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Keys for encryption and decryption</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:</p><ul><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</strong> - a public key used to encrypt property values.</li><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey</strong> - a private key used to decrypt property values.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>To encrypt a property, run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt &lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt; &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> command. Make sure to replace the <strong>&lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt;</strong> and<strong> &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</em> file. The tool will output the encrypted property value.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><h3>Configuring <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1e8d78c-fbc0-40f3-abe8-0aee54621e9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="415fa6bb-a1f4-4181-8a1d-d08930f70005"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>encrypted property values</h3><p>To configure <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be0ecc65-7704-4ca9-b816-abb372475e70"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration </em>file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>application.conf </em>file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following <em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) should be in a location that <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063684 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00405: (2026x) Enterprise deployment

- page_id: `247063684`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063684/2026x+Enterprise+deployment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-03-02T15:21:07.325+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.

To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.</p><p>To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP &amp; TWC enterprise deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064921 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00406: (2026x) Exporting comments to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `247064921`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064921/2026x+Exporting+comments+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:47.313+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.

To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Comments .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_comment_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) App bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports all the comments in the document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing long-running tasks' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes</span>. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Export document to PDF or HTML</strong>.</li><li>In the open wizard, click the <strong>Type</strong> drop-down box and select <strong>Comments</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Document scope</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following scope options: <ul><li><strong>Active section</strong> - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.</li><li><strong>Active section's presentation elements</strong> - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_comment_scope.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Active view</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) App bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Whole document</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - exports all the comments in the document.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:</p><ul><li><p>Choose the format of the target document by selecting the <strong>PDF</strong> or <strong>HTML</strong> radio button.</p></li><li><p><span>To add a cover page, turn on the </span><strong>Cover page </strong><span>switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.</span></p></li><li><p><span>To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the </span><strong>Table of contents</strong><span> switch .<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5497a82e-8ce2-4bc9-97b4-e5bde1cc0c4f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the <strong>Password protected</strong> switch and enter the desired password in the <strong>Password</strong> box. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa164650-1553-4a2b-a826-8e0edd916429"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_export_properties.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li>When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the <strong>Download</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing long-running tasks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Task Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064123 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: (2026x) Exporting documents to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `247064123`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064123/2026x+Exporting+documents+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:25:59.097+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats. You can generate PDF documents of 100,000 pages or more.

#### TIP: Customizing PDF templates

Customizing PDF templates

PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/**shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf* directory.

#### NOTE: Limitations

Limitations

By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.

To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Add the export.limit.size.pdf property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).
  - Add the export.limit.size.html property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on right side of the app bar and select an Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Document .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the open section of the document.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section will be exported as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the open view of the document selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using the app bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports the whole document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. Specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
  - Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Automatic numbering - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.
    - None - sections in the generated document are not numbered.
  - Click the Break page at drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document. You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_properties.png']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing long-running tasks' space=''].

#### NOTE: Microsoft TrueType fonts

Microsoft TrueType fonts

If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.

To avoid this, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Cameo Collaborator allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats. You can generate PDF documents of 100,000 pages or more.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a52e3116-ac6c-4de9-bcac-424533f65682"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Customizing PDF templates</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/</em><em>shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf</em> directory.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d112bc1-848f-441a-a9cd-e25f80a269dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Limitations</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.</p><p>To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:</p><ol><li>Open the <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Do one or both of the following:<ul><li>Add the <strong>export.limit.size.pdf</strong> property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).</li><li>Add the <strong>export.limit.size.html</strong> property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).</li></ul></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on right side of the app bar and select an <strong>Export document to PDF or HTML</strong>.</li><li>In the open wizard, click the <strong>Type</strong> drop-down box and select <strong>Document</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Document scope</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:<ul><li><strong>Active section</strong> - exports the open section of the document.</li><li><strong>Active section's presentation elements</strong> - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section will be exported as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_scope.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><strong>Active view</strong> - exports the open view of the document selected in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using the app bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><strong>Whole document</strong> - exports the whole document.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the following export properties:</p><ul><li><p>Choose the format of the target document by selecting the <strong>PDF</strong> or <strong>HTML</strong> radio button.</p></li><li><p><span>To add a cover page, turn on the </span><strong>Cover page </strong><span>switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.</span></p></li><li><p><span>To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the </span><strong>Table of contents</strong><span> switch .<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5497a82e-8ce2-4bc9-97b4-e5bde1cc0c4f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the <strong>Password protected</strong> switch and enter the desired password in the <strong>Password</strong> box. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa164650-1553-4a2b-a826-8e0edd916429"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.</span></li><li><span>Click the </span><strong>Media numberin</strong><span><strong>g</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:</span><ul><li><strong>Generate automatically</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.</span></li><li><strong>Use current numbering</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Automatic numbering</strong><span> - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.</span></li><li><strong>None</strong><span> - sections in the generated document are not numbered.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Break page at</strong><span> drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1daeee3d-6606-4c2f-ba0c-20c311b97331"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_properties.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li>When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the <strong>Download</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing long-running tasks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Task Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e82d5a9-8e7a-4714-90b3-ca1b4af2ea51"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Microsoft TrueType fonts</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To avoid this, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064128 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00408: (2026x) Exporting tables

- page_id: `247064128`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064128/2026x+Exporting+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-05T13:46:32.575+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Sharing and exporting > (2026x) Exporting documents to PDF and HTML
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export Generic, Instance, and custom tables in a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF, HTML, or Excel.

##### Exporting tables to PDF or HTML

To export a table to PDF or HTML

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to PDF/HTML .
3. An export wizard opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
In the open wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
4. Click the Finish button.
5. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

##### Exporting tables to Excel/CSV/JSON

To export a table to Excel/CSV/JSON

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to Excel/CSV/JSON.
3. An export wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_export.png']
4. With each of the options, select whether you want to Include HTML tags.
5. If you select CSV , also choose the CSV delimiter in the list.
6. Click Export and save the file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.</span></li><li><span>Click the </span><strong>Media numberin</strong><span><strong>g</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:</span><ul><li><strong>Generate automatically</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.</span></li><li><strong>Use current numbering</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="2013ae16-5940-4f80-8222-e59473f8f79d">Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="017c0679-f9f9-4764-a227-9d35cfc55362"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Download</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>button.</span></li></ol><h3>Exporting tables to Excel/CSV/JSON</h3><p><br /></p><p>To export a table to Excel/CSV/JSON</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="56061579-85ee-42b6-b59a-a58c535b435c">In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.</li><li data-uuid="3be9b74c-c484-4924-bb76-3d1ad44cd45f">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the table and select <strong>Export to Excel/CSV/JSON.</strong></li><li data-uuid="6bf222ac-8e0b-4878-981e-f46b9aa3c4e6">An export wizard opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_export.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="46228bbd-9233-4546-87d8-0f13b8bc7511">With each of the options, select whether you want to <strong>Include HTML tags.</strong></li><li data-uuid="9ef52714-425d-4b95-8fc4-aea376379475">If you select <strong>CSV</strong>, also choose the <strong>CSV delimiter </strong>in the list.</li><li data-uuid="035dbbb0-df38-4d03-a6a1-464e5c56b25c">Click <strong>Export </strong>and save the file.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064074 space=MCS version=8 -->
## PAGE 00410: (2026x) File locations

- page_id: `247064074`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064074/2026x+File+locations
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-16T14:18:06.714+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default locations for log and configuration files are listed on this page from a standard server installation.

###### Installation paths

| PACKAGE | OS | VERSION | INSTALLATION DIRECTORY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Teamwork Cloud | Linux | 22xR2 and later | <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Windows | 22xR2 and later | <install_root>\CATIANoMagicServices |  |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>\TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Magic Collaboration Studio | Linux | All | <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio |
| Windows | All | <install_root>\MagicCollaborationStudio |  |

CATIANoMagicServices

TeamworkCloud

MagicCollaborationStudio

<install_root>

###### Web Application Platform

Log files (e.g., *webapp.log*, *collaborator.log*, etc.) 
Linux:*<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform*Windows:*<install_root>**\WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform*

Configuration 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties*

###### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud

Log file for 2024x and newer 
Linux*: <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/logs/server.log***Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\logs\**server.log*

Configuration 
Linux:*<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf* 
Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux:*<user.home>/.twcloud/<version>/server.log* 
Windows:*C:\Users\<user>\.twcloud\<version>\server.log*

###### Authentication server

Log file for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform\authentication.log*

Configuration for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux: *<user.home>/.authserver/<version number>**/authserver.log* 
Windows: *C:\Users\<user>\.authserver\<version>\authserver.log*

Configuration for earlier versions 
Linux: *<install_root>**/AuthServer/config/authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**AuthServer\config\authserver.properties*

###### Cassandra

Log file 
Linux: */var/log/cassandra/system.log*

Configuration filesLinux:*/etc/cassandra/conf*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064975 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00411: (2026x) Filtering usages

- page_id: `247064975`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064975/2026x+Filtering+usages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:52.543+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Resource Usage Map
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating resource usage maps' space=''], you can use the Filters pane located on the left side of the application portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. As you can see below, there two filter groups: main filters which you can use in every resource usage map, and filters for suspicious usages which only exist if there are suspicious usages in a map.

To filter resources in a resource usage map, select the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to view and clear the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to hide.

#### TIP: Why a map is empty?

Why a map is empty?

If no usages in your resource usage map meet the criteria of the selected filters, the map is displayed as empty. Select more filters to see resource usages in the map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example of a resource usage map illustrates different types of filters you can use in the Filters pane.

The following tables describes all the filters you can use in a resource usage map:

| Resource usage map filter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Main filters | Main filters are located at the top of the Filters pane. You can use them in every resource usage map to show or hide non-suspicious usages and standard profiles including local used projects. |
| Display non-suspicious usages | Select this filter to show non-suspicious usages in a resource usage map. |
| Display standard profiles | Select this filter to show the usages of standard profiles and local used projects in a resource usage map. |
| Filters for suspicious usages | Filters for suspicious usages are located under the main filters in the Filters pane. You can use them to show or hide different types of suspicious usages. However these filters are displayed only if there are suspicious usages in a resource usage map. |
| Inconsistent version usages | Select this filter to show the resources that use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects. |
| Cyclic usages | Select this filter to show the resources that use themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop. |
| Unused resources | Select this filter to show the resources that are not used by other resources stored in the Teamwork Cloud repository. The Unused resources filter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating resource usage maps" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating a resource usage map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can use the Filters pane located on the left side of the application portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. As you can see below, there two filter groups: main filters which you can use in every resource usage map, and filters for suspicious usages which only exist if there are suspicious usages in a map.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To filter resources in a resource usage map, select the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to view and clear the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to hide.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df5ff02-5fc0-4496-9a1e-81651afaec23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Why a map is empty?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If no usages in your resource usage map meet the criteria of the selected filters, the map is displayed as empty. Select more filters to see resource usages in the map.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filters_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This example of a resource usage map illustrates different types of filters you can use in the Filters pane.</h6><p>The following tables describes all the filters you can use in a resource usage map:</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 76.4125%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.2586%;" /><col style="width: 79.7414%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Resource usage map filter</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Main filters</strong></td><td>Main filters are located at the top of the Filters pane. You can use them in every resource usage map to show or hide non-suspicious usages and standard profiles including local used projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Display non-suspicious usages</strong></td><td>Select this filter to show non-suspicious usages in a resource usage map.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Display standard profiles</strong></td><td>Select this filter to show the usages of standard profiles and local used projects in a resource usage map.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Filters for suspicious usages</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Filters for suspicious usages are located under the main filters in the Filters pane. <span>You can use them to show or hide different types of suspicious usages. </span>However these filters are displayed only if there are suspicious usages in a resource usage map.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Inconsistent version usages</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Cyclic usages</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that use themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Unused resources</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that are not used by other resources stored in the Teamwork Cloud repository. The <strong>Unused resources</strong> filter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063946 space=MCS version=5 -->
## PAGE 00412: (2026x) General parameters

- page_id: `247063946`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063946/2026x+General+parameters
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-01-20T08:15:33.195+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Deployment Guide > (2026x) Configuration > (2026x) Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following parameters are used with the authentication server to ensure proper working functionality.

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| authentication.token.ttl | The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds. | 900 |
| authentication.code.token.ttl | The authentication code expiration time in seconds. | 15 |
| authentication.implicit.token.ttl | The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds for the implicit authentication flow (e.g., used for MagicDraw UML). | 86400 |
| authentication.unlimited.token.ttl | Authentication ID token that has extremely long expiration time and should only be used in server-server requests when there is a need to perform regular batch jobs. | 630720000 |
| authentication.personal.access.token.ttl | Personal access token that should only be used in server-server requests when there is a need to perform regular batch jobs. | 630720000 |
| authentication.client.ids | The valid client IDs, separated by commas. | MAGICDRAW,webApplicationPlatform,twcSynchronizationManager |
| authentication.client.implicit | The implicit authentication flow client IDs (separated by commas). | MAGICDRAW |
| authentication.client.unlimited | Client IDs that use token ID of unlimited expiration (separated by commas). | twcSynchronizationManager |
| authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist | A list of allowed redirect URIs. The authentication server client should pass a valid redirect URI to an authorized endpoint. | https://${webapp.server.ip}:8443/,https://${twc.server.ip}:8111/,https://md_redirect/ |
| authentication.disable.credentials | Ability to disable username/password authentication if external user authentication (CAC, SAML) is used. | false |
| cassandra.connection.delay | Defines the delay (in seconds) between retry attempts when a connection to Cassandra database is refused. | 30 |
| cassandra.connection.retries | Specifies how many times the application should retry a failed Cassandra database connection. | 12 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064150 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00413: (2026x) Generating a personal access token

- page_id: `247064150`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064150/2026x+Generating+a+personal+access+token
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:02.672+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Admin web applications > (2026x) My account application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A personal access token allows you to authenticate command-line tools without using your username and password.

Always keep your token secure and never share it with anyone you do not trust.

To generate a personal access token

1. Open the My account application.
2. In the Personal access token area, click the Generate Token button.
3. Once the token is generated, click [ATTACHMENT filename='copy.png'] to copy it to your clipboard.

You can use a personal access token in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard' space='MDTWRT'] with an SSO user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a personal access token.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A personal access token allows you to authenticate command-line tools without using your username and password.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b667c4fc-bed0-48d0-9104-4eb193eb0901"><ac:rich-text-body>Always keep your token secure and never share it with anyone you do not trust.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To generate a personal access token</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the My account application.</li><li>In the <strong>Personal access token</strong> area, click the <strong>Generate Token</strong> button.</li><li>Once the token is generated, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy.png" /></ac:image> to copy it to your clipboard.<br /><br /></li></ol><p><br />You can use a personal access token in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects Auto Update Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an SSO user.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generating_personal_access_token.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Generating a personal access token.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20c9d454-4427-4a71-8462-0f4afd0364df"><ac:rich-text-body>To invalidate the personal access token, delete it by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" ri:content-title="Assigning roles" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063070 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00414: (2026x) Generating a report

- page_id: `247063070`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063070/2026x+Generating+a+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:24:33.382+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Reports application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a report

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Accessing the Reports application' space='']Open the **Reports** application.
2. Select the desired report template. In this example we use the User details report template: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Select the scope of users. You can select a single, multiple, or all users.
4. Click **Next**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Next, select the format of the report and choose whether to include assigned roles in the reported data: [ATTACHMENT filename='report_options.png']
6. Click Finish and Save to generate and save your report. Below is an example of a User details report file: [ATTACHMENT filename='user_details_report.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In this example we use the User details report template:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reports_application_User_details.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">Select the scope of users.
