<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247063470 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00415: (2026x) Generating FMU

- page_id: `247063470`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247063470/2026x+Generating+FMU
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T12:47:10.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Server-side simulation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Prerequisites

INLINE

You can generate an FMU (Functional Mock-up Unit) for external co-simulation of dynamic models that enables the exchange of simulation results between different simulation tools. The generated FMU exposes the defined input and output parameters of a model, as specified in a Simulation Configuration. The generated FMU runs the server-side simulation of a particular Simulation Configuration using given inputs and returns the outputs after each time step. FMUs can be generated for two platforms: Linux 64-bit and Windows 64-bit.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- You must have the same Java HOME version as the modeling tool’s Java version. To check the Java HOME version, run shell in the command prompt. To check the modeling tool’s Java version, go to Help > About > Environment tab.
- You must have a simulation server certificate to co-simulate the generated FMU.
- You must use the generated FMU with tools that use the same JAVA version as the modeling tool; otherwise, you will not be able to run the generated FMU.
- You must add the JAR files to the plugin library to generate FMU. To learn more, refer to .

FMU generation uses selected Simulation Configuration properties, and can contain the following defined data:

- Start Time, End Time, and Step Size property values.
- Input Parameters property value: the default values from the defined input parameters will be included in the generated FMU.
- Output Parameters property value: t he default values from the defined output parameters will be included in the generated FMU.

To generate an FMU

1. Open a Teamwork Cloud project.
2. Right-click a Simulation Configuration and select Simulation > Generate FMU. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_FMU_Command.png']
3. In the **Generate FMU** dialog specify the following:
  - In the File Directory box, specify the location where the generated file should be stored.
  - Select the Win64 or Lin64 radio button, depending on the platform on which you will run the generated FMU.
  - Optionally, select the **Include login credentials**check box and specify a server-side simulation server address and login credentials or, token to be stored in the generated FMU file. You can leave the **Include login credentials** check box empty and provide the login credentials or token later as FMU parameters while importing an FMU file. In this case, the credentials will not be stored in the generated FMU file.You can also generate a personal accesstoken instead of a username and password. To learn more about generating a token, refer to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='TWCloudTWRT']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click Generate .

In the case of an FMU file generation error, see the detailed error message in the log file.

##### Adding JAR files to the library

An external library is required to generate the FMU files. You must add the *JAR*files to the plugin library folder.

To add the *JAR*files to the plugin library

1. Download the [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu-builder-2.6.1.jar'] and the [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu-framework-2.6.1.jar'] files.
2. Copy the downloaded JAR files to the <modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\lib path .
3. Restart the modeling tool after copying the *JAR* files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c896986-882d-423c-bc34-2d572956f754"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can generate an FMU (Functional Mock-up Unit) for external co-simulation of dynamic models that enables the exchange of simulation results between different simulation tools. The generated FMU exposes the defined input and output parameters of a model, as specified in a Simulation Configuration. The generated FMU runs the server-side simulation of a particular Simulation Configuration using given inputs and returns the outputs after each time step. FMUs can be generated for two platforms: Linux 64-bit and Windows 64-bit.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e966c364-7df9-46fa-9614-72a8d454d5a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="b97484a4-6a54-4d9d-ba75-b41b92c93c54">You must have the same Java HOME version as the modeling tool’s Java version. To check the Java HOME version, run<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2ef2fbe-0556-41cd-abd4-46f88818912b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java --version]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>in the command prompt. To check the modeling tool’s Java version, go to <strong>Help &gt; About &gt; Environment</strong> tab.</li><li>You must have a simulation server certificate to co-simulate the generated FMU.</li><li>You must use the generated FMU with tools that use the same JAVA version as the modeling tool; otherwise, you will not be able to run the generated FMU.</li><li>You must add the <em>JAR</em> files to the plugin library to generate FMU. To learn more, refer to <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding JAR file to the library" />.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>FMU generation uses selected Simulation Configuration properties, and can contain the following defined data:</p><ul><li><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f4baf39-cd8c-4d54-a688-1332ceab5598">Start Time, End Time, </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f4baf39-cd8c-4d54-a688-1332ceab5598">and </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f4baf39-cd8c-4d54-a688-1332ceab5598">Step Size </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f4baf39-cd8c-4d54-a688-1332ceab5598">property </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f4baf39-cd8c-4d54-a688-1332ceab5598">values.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><strong>Input Parameters </strong>property value: the default values from the defined input parameters will be included in the generated FMU.</li><li><strong>Output Parameters </strong>property value: t<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8352a58f-a835-4edb-957e-452ca237a776">he default values from the defined output parameters will be included in the generated FMU.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To generate an <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c3ac53f-8617-462e-933d-4d94474ec0d4">FMU</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open a Teamwork Cloud project.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click a Simulation Configuration and select </span><strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Generate FMU.</strong><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_FMU_Command.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8cbfa9ab-a197-4700-aa31-3d0fdbf9aa01">In the <strong>Generate FMU</strong></ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="594b1f02-5dc9-4850-b26b-066add5d7568">dialog specify the following:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ul><li>In the <strong>File Directory</strong> box, specify the location where the generated file should be stored.</li><li>Select the <strong>Win64 </strong>or <strong>Lin64 </strong>radio button, depending on the platform on which you will run the generated FMU. </li><li><p>Optionally, select the <strong>Include login credentials </strong>check box and specify a server-side simulation server address and login credentials or, token to be stored in the generated FMU file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="580df867-91be-4b31-a58e-13229a5d3e43"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can leave the <strong>Include login credentials</strong> check box empty <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and provide the login credentials or token later as FMU parameters while importing an FMU file. In this case, the credentials will not be stored in the generated FMU file.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can also generate a personal <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">access<span> </span></span>token instead of a username and password. To learn more about generating a token, refer to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Generating a personal access token" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate FMU dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ul></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a175496-cc94-432d-85e7-cc7605480050">Click</ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b8177df-46a2-4a1d-9f4f-7b709cc7c4e5">Generate</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9aaf3281-df90-4fb0-987d-07749a422358"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the case of an FMU file generation error, see the detailed error message in the log file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Adding JAR files to the library</h3><p>An external library is required to generate the FMU files. You must add the <em>JAR </em>files to the plugin library folder.</p><p>To add the <em>JAR </em>files to the plugin library</p><hr /><ol><li>Download the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu-builder-2.6.1.jar" /></ac:link><em> </em>and the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu-framework-2.6.1.jar" /></ac:link> files.</li><li>Copy the downloaded <em>JAR </em>files to the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\lib </em>path<em>.</em></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Restart the modeling tool after copying the <em>JAR</em> files.</span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064942 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00416: (2026x) Generating resource revisions report

- page_id: `247064942`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064942/2026x+Generating+resource+revisions+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:49.750+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Resources application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export the history of any Teamwork Cloud project into an Excel spreadsheet. Sharing project revision reports can be a quick and easy way to show progress to team leads or project managers.

To generate and download the resource revisions report

1. Navigate to the Resources application and go to the category where the relevant resource is located.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Generate revisions report.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details' space='']Open the **Resource** pane. On the resource details card click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Generate revisions report .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details' space='']Open the **Resource** pane . On the top right of the History card click the icon displayed below: [ATTACHMENT filename='generate_revisions_report_icon_on_history_card.png']
3. In the window that opens, select Download to generate and download the report.

Below is an example of a resource revisions report in XSLS format:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resource revisions report.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can export the history of any Teamwork Cloud project into an Excel spreadsheet. Sharing project revision reports can be a quick and easy way to show progress to team leads or project managers.</p><p>To generate and download the resource revisions report</p><hr /><ol><li>Navigate to the <strong>Resources</strong> application and <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">go to the category where the relevant resource is located.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></span><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select<strong> Generate revisions report.</strong></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Resource</strong> pane</ac:link-body></ac:link>. On the resource details card click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and </span>select<strong> Generate revisions report</strong><span>.</span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Resource</strong> pane</ac:link-body></ac:link>. On the top right of the <strong>History</strong> card click the icon displayed below:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generate_revisions_report_icon_on_history_card.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>In the window that opens, select<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Download </strong>to generate and download the report.</li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Below is an example of a resource revisions report in XSLS format:</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Resource revisions report" ac:alt="Resource revisions report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_revisions_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Resource revisions report.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064719 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00417: (2026x) Getting document information

- page_id: `247064719`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064719/2026x+Getting+document+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:31.439+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document contains detailed document and project information. The following steps outline how to access this information.

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document information

1. Open a Cameo Collaborator document.
2. In the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Document information .

The document information screen opens, displaying the following information:

- Document information:
  - Document creation time
  - The name of the user who created the document
  - The last time the document was published
  - The name of the user who published the latest document version
  - The number of the latest document version
  - Indication if commenting and/or editing is enabled and the template that was used to publish the document
- Project information:
  - Project creation time
  - The name of the user who created the project
  - The last time the project was modified
  - The name of the last user to modify the project
  - The number of the latest project version
  - Project branch name

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of the Cameo Collaborator document information screen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Each Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document contains detailed document and project information. The following steps outline how to access this information.</p><p><br /></p><p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document information</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a Cameo Collaborator document.</li><li>In the app bar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Document information</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The document information screen opens, displaying the following information:</p><ul><li>Document information:<ul><li>Document creation time</li><li>The name of the user who created the document</li><li>The last time the document was published</li><li>The name of the user who published the latest document version</li><li>The number of the latest document version</li><li>Indication if commenting and/or editing is enabled and the template that was used to publish the document</li></ul></li><li>Project information:<ul><li>Project creation time</li><li>The name of the user who created the project</li><li>The last time the project was modified</li><li>The name of the last user to modify the project</li><li>The number of the latest project version</li><li>Project branch name</li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="document_information.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of the Cameo Collaborator document information screen.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247064510 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00418: (2026x) Getting resource links

- page_id: `247064510`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247064510/2026x+Getting+resource+links
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-14T11:26:23.568+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Resources application > (2026x) Working with resources
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to share a Teamwork Cloud project or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link, you can get it as described below.

To get a Teamwork Cloud project link

1. Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the project, and select Get project link .
3. When the window with the project link opens, click Copy .

Once you complete the steps above, the link to the project is copied to your clipboard.

To get a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link

1. Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the project, and do one of the following:
  - Select Get resource link to if you want to get the link for registered users.
  - Select Get public resource link if you want to share the link publicly.
3. When the window with the project link opens, click Copy .

Once you complete the steps above, the document link is copied to your clipboard and you can share it with reviewers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you want to share a Teamwork Cloud project or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link, you can get it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a Teamwork Cloud project link</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the project, and select <strong>Get project link</strong>.</li><li>When the window with the project link opens, click <strong>Copy</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Once you complete the steps above, the link to the project is copied to your clipboard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the project, and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Get resource link</strong> to if you want to get the link for registered users.</li><li>Select <strong>Get public resource link</strong> if you want to share the link publicly.</li></ul></li><li>When the window with the project link opens, click <strong>Copy</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />Once you complete the steps above, the document link is copied to your clipboard and you can share it with reviewers.</p><p><br /></p>
````
